conditioner before making the setting set the dip switch of on the outdoor electronic control board to on to deactivate pre heat control func tion mxz set the dip switch of on the outdoor electronic mxz on auto line correcting mxz outdoor unit has an auto line correcting function which automatically detect and correct improper wiring or piping improper wiring or piping can be automatically detected by pressing the piping wiring correction switch when improper wiring or piping is detected wiring line are corrected this will be completed in about to minute how to activate this function check that outside temperature is above this function doe not work when outside temperature is not above check that the stop valve of the liquid pipe and gas pipe are open check that the wiring between indoor and outdoor unit is correct if the wiring is not correct this function doe not work turn on the power supply and wait at least minute press the piping wiring correction switch on the electronic control board do not touch energized part press the switch to cancel note indoor unit cannot be operated while this function is activated when this function is activated while indoor unit is operating the operation will be stopped operate indoor unit after the auto line correcting is finished pressing the switch during detection cancel this function be sure to check for closed valve behind the service panel indication press the switch for more than second led will show the record of auto correcting for about second as shown in the table below note activate this function to confirm the correct wiring after replacing the electronic control board previous record are deleted when the electronic control board is replaced the record cannot be shown if auto line correcting is not canceled refer to how to activate this function red once time yellow once time green lighting lighting not corrected corrected number of blink wiring line the record of auto line correcting can be supply voltage check the indoor outdoor connecting wire for mis wiring take care of the following during servicing before servicing the air conditioner be sure to turn off the unit first with the remote controller and then after confirm ing the horizontal vane is closed turn off the breaker and or disconnect the power plug be sure to turn off the power supply before removing the front panel the cabinet the top panel and the electronic control board when removing the electrical part be careful to the residual voltage of smoothing capacitor when removing the electronic control board hold the edge of the board with care not to apply stress on the component when connecting or disconnecting the connector hold the housing of the connector do not pull the lead wire mxz mxz mxz mxz failure effective may specification subject to change without notice page ccd color camera user manual page precaution page packing content page user manual page camera page adapter cable page screw page feature and function page installation page power connection page video cable page view angle and focus adjustment page specification page address page content page precaution do not attempt to disassemble the camera there are no user serviceable part inside ask qualified service person for servicing handle the camera with care do not misshandle the camera avoid striking shaking etc improper handling or storage could damage the camera do not expose the camera to rain or moisture or try to operate it in wet area turn the power off immediately and ask qualified service person for servicing moisture can damage the camera do not use strong or abrasive detergent when cleaning the camera body use dry cloth to clean the camera when dirty in case the dirt is hard to remove use mild detergent and wipe gently afterward wipe off the remaining part of the detergent with dry cloth clean the objective faceplate with care do not clean the objective with strong or abrasive detergent use len tissue or cotton tipped applicator and ethanol clean the sphere with care do not clean the sphere with strong or abrasive detergent use len tissue or cotton tipped applicator and glass cleaner never face the camera toward the sun do not aim the camera at bright object whether the camera is in use or not never aim it at the sun or other extremely bright object otherwise blooming or smear may be caused do not operate the camera beyond the specified temperature humidity or power source rating use the camera in condition where temperature is between humidity is below without moisture and the power source is dc precaution page packing content usermanual camera adapter cable screw fixing screw packing content page auto light control alc function electronic light control elc function automatic gain control agc function automatic tracing white balance atw function internal minimum illumination of lux signal to noise ratio db agc off horizontal resolution of tv line olpf built in unlike usual camera this camera filter unnecessary noise signal in dark view and transmit them with more clear picture signal this make it possible to eliminate unpleasant dark noise in low lux condition and ensure an extraordinary clear picture in any light condition feature and function page power connection connect the dc powercable with the and gnd terminal in addition to use the screw terminal you can connect the adapter cable to this terminal and then you can connect power by nes plug and the video signal by bnc plug installation power and signal terminal camera bottom dc gnd gnd cable type rg rg rg rg recommended max cable length page view angle and focus adjustment open the dome cover to adjust the angle of view zoom and focus to adjust focus and the viewing angle zoom you have to loosen the adjustment lever by turning left afterward you can slide the adjustment lever right and left adjust at first the viewing angle and afterward the focus to get sharp picture after overhead console with lcd screen instruction manual ww english safety precaution note the illustration of the display and the panel appearing in this manual are example used to explain more clearly how the control are used therefore what appear on the display in the illustration may differ from what appear on the display on the actual equipment and some of the illustration on the display may represent something impossible in actual operation cleaning the unit if this unit is stained wipe it with dry soft cloth such as silicon cloth if this unit is stained badly wipe the stain off with cloth moistened with neutral cleaner then wipe it again with clean soft dry cloth applying spray cleaner directly to the unit may affect its mechanical part wiping this unit with hard cloth or using volatile liquid such as thinner or alcohol may scratch the surface or erase character screen brightness during low temperature when the temperature of the unit fall such as during winter the liquid panel screen will become darker than usual normal brightness will return after using the monitor for while warning to prevent injury or fire take the following precaution to prevent short circuit never put or leave any metallic object such as coin or metal tool inside the unit do not touch the liquid fluid if the lcd is damaged or broken due to shock the liquid fluid may be dangerous to your health or even fatal if the liquid fluid from the lcd contact your body or clothing wash it off with soap immediately caution to prevent damage to the machine take the following precaution make sure to ground the unit to negative dc power supply do not install the unit in spot exposed to direct sunlight or excessive heat or humidity also avoid place with too much dust or the possibility of water splashing do not use your own screw use only the screw provided if you use the wrong screw you could damage the unit if smoke or abnormal odor is detected turn off the unit immediately and contact the dealer using the unit continuously can result in fire or other accident be sure to have the unit repaired by the dealer if you repair the unit fire or other accident can result english component name and function monitor release knob unlock the motor dome light switch on the light goe on door the light doe on out with the opening closing of vehicle door off the light goe out ir transmitter audio specified sealant quantity engine coolant temperature gauge unit atd part no as required engine coolant temperature sensor nut locking part no as required oil pressure switch atd part no as required oil pan mitsubishi genuine part no as required oil pump case mitsubishi genuine part no as required oil seal case mitsubishi genuine part no as required dec motor corporation engine form in place gasket the engine has several area where the form in place gasket fipg is in use to ensure that the gasket fully serve its purpose it is necessary to observe some precaution when applying the gasket bead size continuity and location are of paramount importance too thin bead could cause leak too thick bead on the other hand could be squeezed out of location causing blocking or narrowing of the fluid feed line to eliminate the possibility of leak from joint therefore it is absolutely necessary to apply the gasket evenly without break while observing the correct bead size the fipg used in the engine is room temperature vulcanisation rtv type and is supplied in gram tube part no or since the rtv harden as it react with the moisture in the atmospheric air it is normally used in the metallic flange area the fipg part no can be used for sealing both engine oil and coolant while part no can only be used for engine oil sealing disassembly the part assembled with the fipg can be easily disassembled without use of special method in some case however the sealant between the joined surface may have to be broken by striking with mallet or similar tool flat and thin gasket scraper may be hammered in between the joined surface in this case however care must be taken to prevent damage to the joined surface surface preparation thoroughly remove all substance deposited on the gasket application surface using gasket scraper or wire brush check to ensure that the surface to which the fipg is to be applied is flat make sure that there are no oil grease and foreign substance deposited on the application surface do not forget to remove the old sealant remaining in the bolt hole form in place gasket application fipg when assembling part with the fipg you must observe some precaution but the procedure is very simple as in the case of conventional pre cut gasket applied fipg bead should be of the specified size and without break also be sure to encircle the bolt hole circumference with completely continuous bead the fipg can be wiped away unless it is hardened while the fipg is still moist in less than minute mount the part in position when the part are mounted make sure that the gasket is applied to the required area only the fipg application procedure may vary on different area observe the procedure described in the text when applying the fipg dec motor reused in order to allow re installation of the belt so that it travel in the same direction as before it was removed mark the direction of travel with an arrow before removing it caution as water or oil on the belt can seriously reduce its usable life ensure that the timing belt sprocket and tensioner stay clean and dry while removed and never wash them part that have become too dirty should be replaced when any of the part are oily check to see whether there are any oil leak in any of the oil seal or the camshaft oil seal on the front of the engine ab camshaft sprocket bolt using the special tool hold the camshaft sprocket remove the camshaft sprocket bolt inspection timing belt check the belt in detail if the following is evident replace the belt hardened back surface rubber back surface glossy non elastic and so hard that even if finger nail is forced into it no mark is produced cracked back surface rubber cracked or separated canva cracked tooth bottom cracked side of belt side of belt badly worn note normal belt should have clear cut side as if cut with sharp knife dec motor corporation peeling crack crack crack rounded edge abnormal wear fluffy strand engine timing belt badly worn tooth initial stage canva is worn fluffy canva fibre are visible rubber is gone and colour has changed to white canva texture is not clear last stage canva is worn out and rubber exposed and its width is reduced missing tooth auto tensioner check for oil leak if oil leak are evident replace the auto tensioner check the rod end for wear or damage and replace the auto tensioner if necessary measure the rod projection length if the reading is outside the standard value replace the auto tensioner standard value mm press the rod by force of to and measure the rod stroke if the measured value exceed the standard value replace the tensioner standard value mm or less installation service point aa camshaft sprocket bolt using the special tool hold the camshaft sprocket torque the camshaft sprocket bolt to the specified torque ba auto tensioner if the auto tensioner rod is fully extended set it in the retracted position by the following procedure set the auto tensioner in vice making sure it is not tilted dec motor corporation rubber exposed tooth missing and canva fiber exposed stroke engine timing slowly close the vice to force the rod in until the set hole of the rod is lined up with the set hole of the cylinder insert metal wire mm in diameter into the set hole remove the auto tensioner from the vice install the auto tensioner on the cylinder block through the oil pump case ca timing belt turn the crankshaft sprocket to position its timing mark tooth away from the timing mark on the crankcase that is lower the no piston from the top dead centre on the compression stroke caution if the camshaft sprocket is turned with the piston at the top dead centre on the compression stroke valve may interfere with the piston align the timing mark for the left bank camshaft sprocket align the timing mark for the right bank camshaft sprocket caution the camshaft sprocket may turn un intentionally due to the valve spring tension take care not to injure your finger align timing mark for the crankshaft sprocket install the timing belt over the sprocket in the following sequence place the timing belt over the crankshaft while applying tension to the belt foreign matter do not disassemble lash adjuster when cleaning lash adjuster use clean diesel fuel only immerse the lash adjuster in clean diesel fuel while pushing down inner steel ball using the special tool air bleed wire move the plunger up and down four or five time to bleed air use of the special tool retainer help facilitate the air bleeding of the rocker arm mounted type lash adjuster adjuster again and test again if the plunger is still loose replace the lash adjuster caution upon completion of air bleeding hold lash adjuster upright to prevent inside diesel fuel from spilling crankshaft oil clearance can be measured easily by using plastic gauge as follow remove oil and grease and any other foreign material from crankshaft journal and bearing inner surface install the crankshaft cut the plastigauge to the same length as the width of bearing and place it on journal in parallel with its axis gently place the crankshaft bearing cap over it and tighten the bolt to the specified torque remove the bolt and gently remove the crankshaft bearing cap measure the width of the crushed plastic gauge at its widest section by using scale printed on the plastigauge package standard value mm limit mm cylinder block visually check for scratch rust and corrosion also use flaw detecting agent and the like to check for crack if there are any defect rectify the cylinder block value mm limit mm check for scratch or seizure of the cylinder wall if there are any defect correct bore it oversize or change the cylinder block measure the inside diameter and the ovality of the cylinder if the cylinder is overly worn correct it to larger size and change the piston and the piston ring standard value cylinder inside diameter mm ovality mm dec motor corporation plastigauge mm off steady or blinking condition abnormal temperature the ventilation grille are clogged with dust or dirt the room temperature is high the protection circuit is working or the lamp is in an abnormal condition lamp operation time warning total lamp operation time reached hour while the power was on lamp operation time warning total lamp operation time reached hour while the power was off lamp operation time warning total lamp operation time reached hour while the power was off the lamp cover isn closed abnormal possible solution clean the ventilation grille relocate the projector to cooler place wait until the status indicator goe out then press the power button if the above solution is not effective replace the lamp replace the lamp change with new lamp if this problem recur close the cover correctly contact your dealer duration when lamp mode of the installation menu is set to low when it is set to standard this duration will be shorted duration when lamp mode is set to standard this duration will be shorted up to hour this projector has indicator each of which show the operation condition of the projector the following offer solution to possible problem if these problem persist turn the projector off and consult your dealer normal condition abnormal condition power indicator status indicator power steady red steady green steady green status off blinking green steady green condition stand by lamp stand by the power is on normal condition note the power button cannot be used to switch between on and off en replacing the lamp this projector is equipped with lamp to project image this lamp is consumable it may burn out or its brightness may decrease during use in such case replace the lamp with new one as soon as possible be sure to replace the lamp with new lamp separately sold that is exclusive to this projector contact your dealer for purchase of the lamp warning be careful not to drop the lamp fixing screw into the inside of the projector don put metal piece or flammable object into the projector using the projector with any foreign object present inside may result in electric shock or fire when you can remove the foreign object from the inside contact your dealer for removal be sure to install the lamp securely when the lamp isn installed properly it won illuminate improper installation may also result in fire don shake the removed lamp or don lift it above your face you may be injured in your eye by flying or falling glass fragment don replace the lamp immediately after use because the lamp cover is very hot you may get burned turn off the power using the power button after the lamp goe out wait about minute until the intake and exhaust fan for cooling the lamp stop rotating then turn off the main power switch and unplug the power cord and wait at least hour to allow the lamp to cool down enough to the touch do not touch the lamp directly it may break and cause injury or burn interval of lamp replacement the recommended interval of lamp replacement is about hour of consecutive use such interval differ depending on the operating environment and may be shorter than hour decrease in luminescence and or color brightness indicate that the lamp need to be replaced when the lamp operation time exceed hour the indicator will blink alternately between green and red while the lamp is lit when the lamp is not lit the indicator will be lit red only and the lamp replacement message will appear on the screen for minute everytime the lamp is turned on when the lamp has been used for about hour the replacement message lamp exchange will appear on the screen for minute every hour thereafter when the lamp operation time exceed hour the projector automatically shut off and can be used until the lamp is replaced and the lamp operation time is reset duration when lamp mode of the installation menu is set to low when it is set to standard this duration will be shorted duration when lamp mode is set to standard this duration will be shorted to hour caution don remove the lamp for any purpose other than replacement unnecessary removal of the lamp may result in breakdown this projector use high pressure mercury lamp as the light source the high pressure mercury lamp may explode or fail to illuminate permanently because of an impact scratch or deterioration through use the period of time until explosion or permanent failure to illuminate vary considerably from lamp to lamp depending on operation condition therefore the lamp may explode soon after the start of use the possibility of explosion of the lamp increase if you use it beyond its replacement period when the instruction of lamp replacement appear replace it with new one immediately even if the lamp is still lighting normally when the high pressure mercury lamp explode glass fragment will scatter inside or outside the lamp box and the gas charged inside the lamp will diffuse inside or outside the projector the gas inside the lamp contain mercury be careful not to breathe it or avoid it from entering your eye or mouth if you should breathe it or if it should enter your eye or mouth see doctor immediately in the event that the high pressure mercury lamp exploded glass fragment may have been scattered inside the projector ask your dealer to replace the lamp and to inspect the inside of the projector when you clean or replace the lamp by yourself be sure to hold the handle of the lamp you may get injured by glass fragment don replace the lamp while the projector is installed on the ceiling glass fragment may fall from the projector entering your eye or mouth causing injury when placing the projector upside down to replace the lamp or conduct maintenance ensure that the projector is securely placed before working with it en english to replace the lamp reverse the projector gently loosen the screw using phillip screwdriver and remove the lamp cover loosen the screw using phillip screwdriver pull up the handle pull out the lamp box by the handle while holding the projector pull the lamp box out of the projector slowly if it is pulled out quickly the lamp may break scattering glass fragment do not spill liquid on the removed lamp box or do not place it near any flammable object or where child can reach to prevent injury or fire load new lamp box securely into the projector in the right orientation put the handle back to the original position make sure that the handle is locked tighten up the screw using phillip screwdriver tighten up the screw using phillip screwdriver to secure the lamp cover reset of the lamp operation time plug the power cord into the wall outlet and turn on the main power switch press the and power button on the control panel at the same time important unless these button are pressed at the same time the lamp operation time isn reset make sure that the status indicator is lit twice and the lamp operation time is reset successfully the projector doesn turn on if the lamp cover isn attached securely be sure to reset the lamp operation time whenever you replace the lamp do not reset the lamp operation time unless you replace the lamp the lamp is fragile if it is broken be careful not to cut yourself with glass fragment contact your dealer for new lamp this projector use lamp that contain mercury disposal of the lamp or the projector with the lamp may be regulated due to environmental consideration for disposal or recycling information please contact your local authority or the electronic industry alliance org for the only maintenance caution be sure to turn off the lamp and unplug the power cord from the wall outlet before you perform any maintenance on the projector cleaning of the projector and the ventilation slot use soft cloth to clean the projector and the ventilation grille when the grille become dirty wipe them with soft cloth dampened with diluted mild detergent and then wipe them with dry cloth to protect the surface of the projector from fading or deteriorating do not spray insecticide on or inside the projector do not use benzine or thinner to clean it do not let rubber or plastic material contact with projector cleaning of the len use standard len cleaning brush or piece of len tissue dampened with len cleaning fluid the len surface is fragile use only recommended nonabrasive len cleaning material do not touch the len with your that customer pet child and you are not exposed to any live electrical supply insulation test the following test must be performed prior to and upon completion of all repair to dyson floorcare product and before any functional check you must ensure that full visual inspection of the product is completed prior to repair these test are vital to avoid any possibility of personal injury to the end user the seaward primetest or equivalent should be used to test the electrical insulation of class appliance it indicate any electrical leakage insulation test point test the two screw highlighted in the upright switch cover test result an insulation test reading of mq is acceptable reading of below mq is not considered safe and further investigation rectification and testing must be completed before the product is used the following component must be visually inspected the wiring behind the upright switch cover all wiring behind the switch housing test the two screw highlighted in the rear of the duct assembly if you cannot repair product with an insulation test reading of below you must inform the customer that the product is unsafe to use please inform the customer of the required action to repair the product including the charge structure if the product is left un repaired please indicate on your paperwork hand held device that the product is electrically unsafe and attach warning product electrically unsafe sticker in visable location on the product technical info technical info dc service manual wiring schematic brushbar switch switch area upright switch main on off switch temperature degree celcius excessive temperature within the motor are usually caused by machine filter blockage the cleaner head is fitted with tco reset switch that will shut the brushbar motor off if it is put under an excessive load blocked seized brushbar etc the cause of the shut down should be cleared and the tco reset by pressing the switch if the cause of the brushbar motor cut out is not rectified the tco will repeatedly reactivate preventing the brushbar from turning electrical fault diaignosis note all connection number and continuity check refer to the illustration and table on weight kg noise level fan sound pressure max db en gb general safety regulation general safety regulation obligation and responsibility these instruction are to be regarded as for guidance only and entail no responsibility whatsoever on the part of derstad ab and or its representative full responsi bility for the use transportation maintenance and servicing of the machine lie with the owner driver these instruction cover large number of machine configuration local condition affecting crop rotation soil type climate etc may require procedure different to those mentioned in these instruction it is the full responsibility of the owner driver to ensure that the machine is used correctly in all respect the owner is also responsible for ensuring that all individual who use the machine have read and understood this instruction manual and that they work in accordance with all valid provision and regulation if an individual working with the machine detect any safety defect these must be addressed immediately all derstad machine have passed quality assurance inspection and operational test prior to delivery however the user owner shall bear full responsibility for the machine functioning correctly when in use in the field in the event of complaint please refer to the general delivery provision for the derstad group design modification form part of the continual improvement of our machine description of the machine therefore refer to the form and design of the machine at the time of writing the user manual may contain image which show machine that doe not look exactly like the one you have received depending on which optional addition have been fitted the model or whether any update have been carried out some description in these instruction may not be relevant to your machine the operating instruction are designed according to the guideline of the iso standard before using the machine carefully read through these instruction and ensure that you have understood their content learn to use the machine correctly and carefully in the wrong hand or if carelessly operated the machine can become dangerous implement the machine will form part of your workplace and the workplace of your colleague it is therefore important to ensure that everyone is protected and that functioning safeguard are in place how to read these instruction letter within bracket refer to corresponding letter in the image and are used as reference in text reference reference information for which the order is important is indicated using numbered action instruction when referring to image figure are also used in the same way as the alphabetical list if the reference outnumber the letter in the alphabet start by then description of safety symbol alway pay extra attention to text or figure indicated by this symbol the symbol indicate danger that may result in death serious bodily harm or considerable material damage if action are not taken to avoid them alway pay extra attention to text or figure indicated by this symbol the symbol indicate danger that may result in death serious bodily harm or considerable material damage if action are not taken to avoid them signal word used to indicate potentially hazardous situation which if not avoided could result in minor or moderate injury however such distinction can be too subtle to affect behaviour or to be translated in certain circumstance signal phrase such as danger of death risk of blinding or beware of fume can be more effective at drawing greater attention to some instruction or safety information than signal word this indicate special situation or an activity required for proper machine handling failure to follow these instruction may cause disruption to the machine or to its surrounding information marked with this symbol may be worth noting as it relate to useful tip or particularly useful piece of information that will help you handle the machine properly used to clarify information en gb general safety regulation used to present information in the form of bullet point the order in which the information is set out is not indicative of any particular order of precedence which must be followed safety instruction make sure that the machine is set to the correct height correct height of the machine will ensure that the loading height is correct the loading height vary according to the machine type risk of eye injury never look into the radar optic while in operation alway follow the instruction and safety precaution for the host machine alway make sure that the coupling are clean and free from dirt to maintain the machine high level of quality and operational safety use only genuine derstad spare part the warranty and any claim under will become null and void if non genuine part are used en gb general safety regulation scrapping and recycling when the machine or any of its part are worn out derstad ab recommend that scrapping be carried out according to applicable regulation in order to avoid toxic and environmentally hazardous material ending up in the environment during its lifespan replaced wear part in steel should be sent to metal scrapping company replaced wear part in rubber plastic or the like should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility oil oil filter battery and other electrical component may have negative impact on the environment and should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility in the case of scrapping the machine mostly consist of steel that is suitably scrapped by an authorised metal scrapping company other component should be taken care of according to local recycling regulation reach art reach is regulation adopted to improve the protection of human health and the environment from the risk that can be posed by chemical for more information on how derstad ab follow reach article visit vaderstad com reach alway follow current local recycling regulation and applicable ordinance en gb tractor for isobus control please refer to the separate user guide en gb control system control system for isobus control please refer to the separate user guide en gb filling and emptying filling and emptying before filling the hopper check before filling that the machine is empty clean and dry that the discharge hatch is closed that the correct rotor is fitted that the level guard is at the correct height fill the hopper safety first never walk or stand under suspended load make sure nobody is on the machine or in the hopper during delivery of the product to the machine do not approach the hopper unless absolutely necessary avoid contact with and inhalation of seed dressing to fill the hopper use loader with pallet place the bag on the pallet set the machine to the correct loading height when at the correct loading height the ladder will be at good angle and the correct distance from the ground so that the operator can easily access the platform use the provided access way when approaching the platform adjust the level guard location of the level guard figure the height of the level guard can be adjusted so that the sensor can be moved between the different bracket when drilling higher rate or large size product the level guard can be placed higher up in the hopper when drilling low rate or small size product the level guard can be placed at lower level on the hopper disassembly and assembly turn the level guard counter clockwise and loosen from the bracket turn the level guard clockwise to assemble on to the other bracket level scale for seed scale with interval of is installed inside the hopper to measure the level of the product the scale has limit of en gb filling and emptying opening the discharge door figure before opening the discharge door check that the fan is turned off the seed feed housing is emptied in single operation figure fan lever turn off the fan by switching off the hydraulic remote on the tractor if the agitator is required for efficient emptying use the ball valve instead figure turn the knob to release both the discharge door en gb calibration calibration for isobus control please refer to the separate user guide calibrating the biodrill figure figure turn the ball valve on thebiodrill hopper to switch off the fan start the hydraulic system for thebiodrill hopper to rotate the agitator figure open the latch by pushing and turning the grey knob see figure to calibrate follow the procedure for fertiliser calibration see fertiliser calibration see separate manual close the hatch and shut off the hydraulic system for the biodrill hopper figure switch on the fan with the ball valve en gb maintenance and service maintenance and service alway shut down the tractor and remove the ignition key in conjunction with work using the hopper and when performing service and maintenance the machine must be supported safely on stand for all work underneath the machine or when there is risk of crush injury ensure also that the surface supporting the trestle is firm enough to support them alway lower the machine to the ground prior to performing any service on the hydraulic system depressurise the hydraulic system before service and maintenance the machine should be in the lowered position and standing on flat surface when carrying out service and maintenance alway switch off the ipad control when performing maintenance and service work on the machine regular maintenance to preserve the high quality of the drill alway use genuine derstad spare part perform maintenance check at regular interval and at the end of the season check that there is no seed or any bit of packaging remaining in the biodrill hopper seed feed housing rotor or feed system check that there has been no seed or any bit of packaging in the seed hose or in the outlet of the distributor head check that no seed has run down into the ejector tube and associated air hose check that the seed hose and coupling are not trapped or damaged cleaning cleanliness is of vital importance when undertaking any kind of work with the machine hydraulic system wipe with clean paper or cloth place part on clean surface not directly on the work bench rinse before assembly for example with degreasing agent never rinse the bearing using high pressure water supply it is important to grease the bearing after washing in order to remove any water that has remained behind never clean electrical electronic component directly with high pressure water jet clean electrical electronic component by blow ing them with air or by wiping them with dampened cloth exercise extreme caution when washing with high pressure water in the area around the seed feed housing after cleaning air and dry all component carefully before reassembly keep the fan running for while after cleaning so that the whole system is blown dry any remaining seed that sprout can block the air and seed hose seed can also attract small rodent which can damage the seed drill clean biodrill hopper rotor seed feed housing seed hose the tramlining motor and outlet in the distributor head clean the seed feed housing open and disassemble according to seed meter allow all the part to dry thoroughly prior to reassembly clean the radar en gb maintenance and service seed transport distributor head flex figure open the cover by loosening and removing the six wing nut replace the distributor head tramlining motor replace the distributor head tramlining motor if needed figure to remove faulty motor from the distributor head loosen and remove the three screw remove the rubber lid and the three electrical screw on the circuit board use the torx screwdriver supplied fit new motor with the three electric screw and the three electric screw use the torx screwdriver supplied to tighten the electrical screw assemble the rubber lid repair and replace the seed hose figure repair if seed hose is damaged due to abrasion or folding it can be jointed with sleeve cut the hose at the centre of the fold or damage where necessary the smallest possible part of the hose can be cut away if the hose become too stiff at the joint and cannot be bent sufficiently when the machine is lowered it may be necessary to replace the entire seed hose or to replace part of the hose and joint it in two place changing seed hose use soapy water on the hose when fitting them to seed coulter twist the hose anti clockwise when removing or fitting this will help the spiral reinforcement to open the hose little cut replacement hose to the same length as the piece it is to replace seed meter biodrill is fitted with an electrically powered fenix seed feed housing monitoring and manoeuvring can be done using control setting sliding hatch figure ac ba en gb maintenance and service when the sliding hatch located beneath the sealing cover is pressed in the supply from the hopper to the metering unit is blocked when the supply from the hopper is blocked it is possible to replace the roller or perform service on the seed feed housing even though the hopper is full during drilling the sliding hatch must alway be completely open and the wing nut tightened disassembly and assembly figure open the catch and turn the motor in clockwise direction remove the motor from the seed feed housing changing feed roller before changing the roller it may be necessary to empty the lower part of the seed feed housing to gather up the seed figure loosen the locking pin and pull out the cotter pin pull the plug out figure separate the roller and the motor switch to the required roller note that there are two groove along the motor shaft that must match the roller note that the roller can only be attached in one direction fit the output motor after cleaning carefully air and dry all component in the seed hopper before reassembly figure check that the seed feed housing is empty clean inside when necessary clean outside the flange on the outside of the housing en gb maintenance and service figure insert the motor and roller in the seed feed housing the motor can only be fitted with the motor cable downward and the arrow upward turn the motor counter clockwise until it click into place remove the plug in the seed feed housing loosen the locking pin and pull out the cotter pin in figure pull out the plug fit the plug in the seed feed housing figure attach the plug to the seed feed housing attach the plug in the correct direction check that the pinhole in the plug is in line with the pinhole in the plate check that the entire plug is inserted completely the flat surface of the plug must be on the same level as that of the profile assemble the cotter pin assemble the locking pin clean the feed system the feed system on the drill should be cleaned regularly and alway at the end of the season seed residue attract small rodent when in storage for long period when not in use the machine should be stored indoor to protect the machine electronic instrument these electronic instrument are of very high standard and have high tolerance for moisture we nonetheless recommend storage indoor make sure that the seed drill has been emptied and thoroughly cleaned the feed roller should be completely dry before being put into storage and should be kept in dry dark and rodent free place when the machine is not in use en gb troubleshooting decal carefully read through these instruction and ensure that you have understood their content refer to these instruction and safety pro vision as needed when working alway ensure that the implement entire working and extension area is alway kept clear never walk under suspended section make sure that the wing section are locked when folded up for road transport parking and or maintenance check that the functionality of the locking hook is not impeded by soil and plant residue warning tape beware of crush or shock hazard also used on safety component never work under the machine unless it has been properly secured with jack stand or other strong support on firm surface lock the lifting cylinder using the appropri ate yellow locking device crushing risk warning cfe en gb general safety provision alway make sure that the working area of the bout marker are free from obstruction bear in mind that there is risk of injury when the bout marker are being unfolded and risk of crushing between the seed drill and the bout marker when they are being retracted note the bout marker are alway retracted when the machine is lifted no matter what is shown on the control unit the indicated bout marker are alway extended when the machine is lowered for this reason alway switch off the control unit when the machine is not in the field setting and machine data are saved when the control unit is switched off do not climb on the machine wheel or packer as these may rotate even when the machine is stationary make sure nobody is on the drill during operation make sure nobody is on the seed drill when front loading seed and or fertiliser the machine ladder step and platform are not intended to be used for manual loading from small seed bag extra transport height warning beware of overhead cable via duct gate and tree etc alway check the maximum permitted navigable height warning against cutting oil jet as the hydraulic system contain accumulator exercise extreme caution when disconnecting hy draulic coupling from the machine never point hydraulic coupling at any part of the body empty the accumulator of oil before service and repair do not stand on the machine grating do not stand between the tractor and the machine when the tractor is being reversed for hitching jack en gb general safety provision safety instruction safety instruction during installation and maintenance alway park the implement on level and stable surface depressurise the machine until it rest on its wheel disc and the support stand keep in mind that the ground pressure under the support stand is considerable especially when the hopper is full never stand under the intermediate packer or seed drill if the intermediate packer is raised and only secured with the tractor hydraulic lifting arm prior to any service on the intermediate packer be sure to secure it properly with trestle etc on stable surface any welding work on the machine must be carried out to professional standard note that poor welding may result in serious personal injury or fatality if you are unsure contact professional welding technician for instruction on machine that are delivered packaged you will find two packing box of machine part in the seed hopper the packaging for these packing box can get damp in transit check the packaging in the seed hopper when unloading if the packaging is damp or wet the content must be unloaded by hand regularly inspect the tractor hitch device and the machine towing eye for wear replace the towing eye when it has been worn down to the wear limit the machine is hydraulically pressurised when the tractor is connected and in operation alway shut down the tractor and disconnect the feed electrical system when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill prior to connecting the hydraulic hose alway make sure the seed drill and the tractor coupling are clean and free of dirt to maintain the machine high level of quality and operational safety use only derstad genuine spare part the warranty and any claim under it will become void if part other than genuine part are used safety instruction during transport the owner operator has sole responsibility when transporting the seed drill on public road ensure that at least of the tractor weight is still being carried by its front wheel when the seed drill has been connected loaded this is to ensure that the steering ability of the whole vehicle is maintained good judgement and extreme caution should be exercised when transporting the seed drill on public road we recommend the use of tractor with gross weight that is at least equal to the gross weight of the seed drill if the seed drill is not equipped with brake alway comply with national legislation concerning brake equipment when transporting the machine on public road show good judgement and drive carefully pay close attention to the machine width and turning circle of the implement during transport the rear view is very restricted check the positioning of the tractor rear view mirror please be aware that if the machine is running on very steep and or rough terrain there is risk that the machine may overturn use the light on the seed drill in accordance with local traffic regulation en gb general safety provision in order to avoid any risk arising from mistake during road transport all electronic control equipment inside and outside of the tractor cab must be switched off before commencing transport on the road during transportation on public road the pre implement must be raised retracted so that the transport width and transport height of the implement comply with national regulation before driving ensure that all nut and bolt are tight after couple of hour driving re tighten the wheel nut regularly check that they are tight note that nut must be tightened to specific torque do not stand on the platform during operation keep the platform clean to prevent any risk of slipping use the yellow locking device to block the lifting cylinder when transporting the drill on the road over longer distance please be aware that if the machine is running on very steep and or rough terrain there is risk that the machine may overturn never fill the tyre to pressure higher than tyre manufacturer recommendation this may result in great danger on the road this machine and its tyre are designed for maximum speed of km during transportation on the public highway pay attention to national speed limit this speed should be reduced on uneven road surface the intermediate packer should alway be in the raised position when being transported on road this will avoid unnecessary tyre wear fold up the lower step before transport to avoid the tractor damaging it safety instruction during work ensure that people who are near the seed drill when the tractor engine is in operation maintain an adequate safety distance from suspended load and from raised or moving machine part alway make sure that the following harrow working area is free of obstruction bear in mind that there is risk of injury when the following harrow is being lowered to the ground alway shut down the tractor and take out the ignition key in conjunction with work in the seed hopper as well as when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill when carrying out service and maintenance the drill should be in the lowered position and standing on flat surface the ladder on the inside of the seed hopper is only intended for use in conjunction with service work the machine is equipped with platform do not stand on the platform during operation the machine platform is not intended to be used for manual loading from small bag if small bag are to be used the best way to fill the seed drill is to use loader with the bag on pallet watch out for hanging power line when the machine is being transported do not work with the machine during thunder storm and when there is risk of lightning strike do not stand on or next to the machine en gb general safety provision the intermediate packer should alway be in the raised position when reversing the seed drill if the packer is not in the raised position the wheel carrier and the wheel themselve could be damaged fold up the lower step before transport to avoid the tractor damaging it design change the user owner is liable for design change addition or modification that have not been approved by derstad ab design change can affect wear and may therefore cause the machine to no longer comply with the legislative requirement in accordance with which it has been approved configuration change the user owner is solely responsible for any configuration change that have not been approved by derstad ab conversion of the machine invalidate the type approval for the machine en gb general safety provision moving the machine when not hitched to tractor if the machine must be moved when not hitched to tractor it must be transported on machine trailer or lorry flatbed the machine must be rolled onto and off the transport vehicle using tractor lifting with crane is prohibited for data pertaining to the dimension and weight of the machine see technical data alway make sure that you comply with applicable national regulation concerning transport dimension requirement for escort vehicle or similar figure switch the machine to its transport position see switching to transport mode activate the wheel retraction function see wheel retraction function raise the front tool to highlift height reverse the machine onto the trailer or flatbed if using flatbed ramp loading platform or similar will be required exercise extreme care check that no machine part are damaged during loading lower the machine adjust the support stand so that the machine is resting on the wheel disc and support stand or the intermediate packer in the lowered position ensure that the machine hydraulic system is depressurised prevent the machine transport wheel from rolling using chock or similar secure the tarpaulin using tie down strap or similar unhitch the tractor from the machine secure the machine using suitable lashing equipment in accordance with applicable regulation the lashing equipment must be attached to the machine at the location indicated by the decal see figure en gb general safety provision scrapping and recycling when the machine or any of its part are worn out derstad ab recommend that scrapping be carried out according to applicable regulation in order to avoid toxic and environmentally hazardous material ending up in the environment during its lifespan replaced wear part in steel should sent to metal scrapping company replaced wear part in rubber plastic or the like should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility oil oil filter battery and other electrical component may have negative impact on the environment and should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility in the case of scrapping the machine mostly consist of steel that is suitably scrapped by an authorised metal scrapping company other component should be taken care of according to local recycling regulation reach art reach is regulation adopted to improve the protection of human health and the environment from the risk that can be posed by chemical for more information on how derstad ab follow reach article visit vaderstad com reach the hose must be adjusted so that they hang freely even at sharp turn figure when the hydraulic hose are disconnected from the tractor the hose holder must be folded backward lock the holder in the transport transport brake hydraulic brake the machine can be fitted with hydraulic brake the system comprise parking brake and an emergency brake system that is applied if the machine become unhitched from the tractor parking brake figure alway park the implement on stable and level surface activate the parking brake by undoing the pin and rotating the lever handle tighten the lever release the parking brake by moving the lever forward and then to the upright position if the implement has to be parked on or close to public road the machine must also be secured by putting chock under the transport wheel if the machine is to be parked for longer period of time the parking brake should be disengaged the machine should instead be secured with chock emergency brake in order for the emergency brake to function the wire in the parking brake lever must be secured to the tractor see figure the emergency brake engage if the machine become unhitched from the tractor pneumatic brake the machine can be fitted with pneumatic brake the pneumatic brake are linked to the tractor compressed air coupling and are controlled by the pressure applied to the tractor brake pedal the pneumatic brake have compressed air in the brake pipe but the last section out toward the brake drum consist of closed oil system when the tractor brake are applied the braking effect is proportionally transferred to the machine and ensure effective braking connecting the brake connect the brake system compressed air line and steering link to the tractor brake fitting the compressed air line has red mounting and must be connected to the tractor red coupling the control line has yellow mounting and must be connected to the tractor yellow coupling disconnecting the brake unhitching and parking must alway take place on flat firm surface disconnect the brake hose and electric cable from the tractor and hang them on the hose holder figure release the brake by pressing in the retarder valve lower and secure the support stand disconnect the hitching hook agricultural drawbar device parking pneumatic brake alway park the implement on level and stable surface the brake are automatically applied when the implement is unhitched from the tractor en gb transport figure when parking the implement on or close to public road be sure to put chock under the wheel the wheel chock are located on the front platform if the machine is to be parked for longer period of time the parking brake should be disengaged the machine should instead be secured with chock switching between transport and working position for switching between transport mode and working mode in ipad see the wing folding menu changing to working mode to view respective colour marking see dimension and colour coding on the transport check whether the wheel retraction mechanism has been reset by verifying that the wheel beam of the wing section are in the folded out position if this is not the case this will be immediately obvious since the wing section will be hanging down this can be rectified by retracting the folding cylinder lower the machine until the wheel retraction mechanism engage figure rda figure retraction function check that front tool and following implement do not exceed the metre transport width en gb transport wheel retraction function there is risk of crushing in the spring loaded wheel retraction function alway use an appropriate tool when operating the locking device doe not apply to rda without idc the seed drill is equipped with function that allow the wing section wheel to be pulled in once the machine has been folded up into its transport position this is to minimise the machine transport width figure to activate the function the locking device one on each wing section must be lifted up to position once the machine has been folded up figure use the enclosed key or other suitable tool when the machine is folded out to working position the wheel retraction mechanism is reset automatically and the wheel return to normal position the seed drill should not be fully raised when the wing section are unfolded lower it approx cm from top position before starting the folding process if the wheel retraction mechanism automatic reset has not worked this is shown by the machine hanging at an angle in the extended position to rectify this retract the folding cylinder lower the machine until its wheel retraction mechanism engage the machine can be folded up and folded out even without this function being activated with the only consequence being that the wing section wheel then stay in normal position and the transport width is therefore greater to avoid serious damage to the machine if the machine is on soft ground and folded up do not lower it to the drilling position if the machine is folded up the wheel must not be drawn up from the ground using the hydraulic system ac en gb general setting general setting horizontal alignment figure adjust the longitudinal inclination of the machine using the push rod with the machine resting on solid level ground when correctly adjusted the front and rear row of drill disc should reach the ground at the same time when the machine is lowered figure rda lock nut figure rda and rda lock nut which is fitted with reinforcement sleeve loosen the lock nut or adjust to the desired position and then tighten the lock nut use the spanner with mm holding width supplied with the machine the measurement must never exceed mm the measurement must never exceed mm make sure you tighten the lock nut properly locking parallel transfer rod to prevent the parallel transfer rod from rotating and causing the machine to have an incorrect inclination bracket can be mounted figure mount the bracket and screw it into place slide the plate onto the rod to the desired position screw the screw onto it pcs checking in the field figure horizontal alignment can be verified in the field by ensuring that the front seed coulter drill to the same depth as those at the rear first pack the soil and then scrape off very thin layer of soil until the seed are exposed the seed in all row should be exposed at the same time if they are not this suggest that the machine is not running parallel to the ground dc eb en gb general setting radar angle setting the radar angle never look into the radar window while it is operating risk of eye injury the radar must be calibrated before operating regularly clean the radar ensure that the radar operating radius is clear from disruptive element such as hose or cable figure the angle of the radar unit should be adjusted it should be set at an angle of in relation to the ground surface the angle of the radar is optimal when the surface is parallel to the ground and when is parallel to the machine frame after adjustment as described in horizontal alignment remove the screw and adjust the bracket along the oblong hole calibrating the radar for isobus control please refer to the separate user guide adjusting the master slave system figure the figure above doe not apply to rda without idc the seed drill drilling depth is controlled by three hydraulic cylinder connected in sery in so called master slave system before the seed drill is brought into use the cylinder must be bled and reset in relation to each other bleeding raise the machine to its top position so that all hydraulic cylinder are fully extended keep the hydraulic lever in this position and the tractor idling for approx second the cylinder permit seepage when in the top position that allow oil to overflow through the system and expel any air repeat this procedure for few second once the tractor has been connected before adjusting the seed drill after folding out and few time during the working day after bleeding and resetting the master slave system the height level for the three section should then be checked and adjusted in relation to each other if necessary this is important in order to ensure that the drilling depth is identical in all section adjusting the height of the wing section against the centre section rda adjustment should be made with the seed drill on flat and solid surface the setting should then be checked with the machine in working mode in the field check that the folding cylinder is fully extended and the hydraulic pressure of the weight transfer cylinder is normal bar adjust the weight transfer if necessary see weight transfer or if the machine is an rda see adjusting the wing section on rda below en gb general setting lower the machine until its lowest disc is approx cm above the ground figure compare the height of the outermost disc on the centre section with the innermost disc on the wing section the illustration above adjustment must take place without any of the piston rod end coming loose from the machine see figure extend the piston rod to lower the wing section shorten the piston rod to raise the wing section it might be difficult to turn the piston rod in the hydraulic cylinder end position do not unscrew the piston rod end more than mm tighten the lock nut raise the machine to the highlift position then lower the machine back down to cm above the ground in order to ensure that the setting is unchanged check the setting with the machine in working mode in the field adjusting the wing section on rda position the bolt must be tightened to torque of nm the number of washer should not exceed the number supplied by the factory if you wish to lift the front tool even higher in the raised position in job is to prevent the soil ridge that would otherwise result from the work of the disc the spill prevention plate are somewhat different from tool to tool and should therefore be adjusted in different way adjusting spill prevention plate on system disc system agrilla system crossboard heavy figure adjust the spill prevention plate by loosening the screw adjust the spill prevention plate adjusting spill prevention plate on system disc aggressive the spill prevention plate work on the surface of the soil and should be encumbered only by their own weight and flexible in their parallelogram the vertical setting of the spill prevention plate should be adjusted in case of considerable change to the seed depth figure adjust the spill prevention plate by removing the screw move the holder up or down the hole sery to the desired position adjust the screw that prevent the spill prevention plate rotating or sticking to the same height as the screw the plate is also equipped with wire which prevent the plate from going down into the soil when in the lowlift position the wire is adjusted by opening the shackle with plier and moving it along the hole sery bout marker in the folded up prevent the valve from slipping along the track figure if the correct setting is not obtained after adjusting the changeover valve above the spring can be moved to another appropriate hole in the figure the scraper position can be adjusted in two place figure if the setting is insufficient the holder can be adjusted the distance between the scraper blade and the tyre must not be less than mm rotate the tyre by hand to verify that this distance is not less than mm at any point depending on the circumstance larger distance may be required for optimum result if the scraper doe not operate satisfactorily then try with different scraper setting example if there is lot of plant residue in the soil and it is humid the scraper will usually provide better result when the distance is increased to approx mm if the soil is humid without plant residue the scraper will provide better result when mounted in the default position mm track eradicator for road transport the track hopper check before filling that the machine is empty clean and dry that the metering unit is are set according to the seeding table that the emptying hatch es is are closed before re filling rda figure the seed level should be lower than half way down the sight glass before re filling take place so the operator will know that both feed box are dispensing the same amount figure any build up of deposit will be evident if the level are different the setting of the metering unit can be adjusted individually see individual adjustment of the metering unit scale value rda filling from bulk bag think about safety never walk under suspended load make sure nobody is on the drill when the seed is brought up to it it is best to use an extension handle on the knife do not go up to the seed hopper unless absolutely necessary avoid contact with and inhalation of seed dressing figure the safest method of filling is from the rear or from the side with the wing folded up rda if the filling is done from the front this must take place diagonally from the right hand side with the tractor turned sideway so it is possible to get up on the platform without having to walk under the lifting arm filling from small bag put safety first never stand under suspended load make sure nobody is on the drill when the seed is brought up to it do not go up to the seed hopper unless absolutely necessary avoid contact with and inhalation of seed dressing the ladder and platform on the machine are not intended to be used for manual loading from small bag ok en gb filling and emptying figure the best way to fill is to use loader and put the bag on pallet lift the pallet diagonally from the front right with the tractor sideway at the same height as the hand rail so the platform can be accessed safely emptying the seed hopper the seed hopper is emptied with one single operation figure pull the sliding hatch right out if smaller amount is to be emptied out just pull the hatch little way up do not forget to close the sliding hatch after emptying en gb calibration calibration for control please refer to the separate user guide the seed drill is equipped with hydraulically powered feed output from the seed meter individual adjustment of the metering unit scale value rda figure press together and undo the spring suspended intermedi ate shaft from the left metering unit on the machine turn the hexagonal head on the outgoing shaft on the metering unit to the desired position reassemble the intermediate shaft test drive to check the actual feed volume test drive can be performed this is the precision of the scale from affecting the calibration it is recommended to weigh at least kg of the selected seed or fertiliser it is recommend to replace the scale battery once year figure weighing of calibration sample should be carried out as follow press the start reset button hang the empty calibration bag on the scale hook the weight of the bag is displayed wait until hold is shown on the display the scale automatically shut off after approximately minute it is therefore advisable to note the empty bag weight press the start reset button hold en gb calibration remove the bag and fill it with the calibration sample weigh the now filled bag the scale now show the net weight of the sample whilst driving the scale should be stored safely in the calibration box regularly check the scale against known weight and alway prior to the start of the season replace the battery type when the battery indicator show one bar or less en gb seed drilling seed drilling the drilling depth used must be adjusted to suit the current crop the soil type and drilling condition at the time of drilling continuously and carefully monitor the drilling depth the drilling depth is set mechanically using the lifting cylinder stop device or from the tractor cab using the control unit in field with varying soil type the depth setting should be checked and adjusted to ensure the correct drilling depth across the entire field feed system operation at too high speed can lead to the seed bouncing around at the bottom of the seed furrow this result in an uneven drilling depth operation at too low speed in relation to the application rate speed can lead to the seed hose getting blocked setting the amount of air the fan rotation speed must not exceed rpm the amount of air is regulated by mean of the fan rotation speed the fan rotation speed is regulated by the tractor flow valve there is flow valve on most tractor model with constant pressure hydraulic system if the tractor has priority hydraulic coupling use that one controlling the flow from the tractor hydraulic system provide for optimum function of both the hydraulically powered fan and the tractor set the fan rotation speed in accordance with the table check the fan rotation speed in the display on ipad normal mode fan rotation speed rpm the recommended speed are guideline at low application rate it may be good idea to reduce the speed at extremely high application rate high speed the fan speed may need to be increased the alarm warning of too low rotation speed is best set for rpm below the preset rotation speed and the alarm warning of too high rotation speed to rpm above the preset rotation speed the alarm level are set in the general setting menu table recommended fan rotation speed rda rda rda small size seed rpm rpm rpm grain rpm rpm rpm if full fan speed is not required the fan rotation speed should be lowered using the tractor flow valve setting the drilling depth reset and bleed the system for approx second several time during the working day raise the machine to its top position so that all hydraulic cylinder are fully extended keep the hydraulic lever in this position and the tractor idling master cylinder stop bolt figure when the interactive depth control function is used it is good idea to set the stop bolt to safety depth maximum desired drilling depth should problem arise with the interactive depth control function then the manual drilling depth setting can be used and the drilling can continue en gb seed drilling mechanical adjustment of drilling depth rda with master required position see figure the scale indication doe not give an absolute value of the drilling depth in centimetre but serve as reference if there are wide variation in drilling depth the horizontal alignment may need to be adjusted see horizontal alignment mechanical adjustment of drilling depth rda without master slave system figure the drilling depth of the centre section is set using the stop bolt the drilling depth of the wing section is set with the relevant crank the scale indication do not constitute absolute value of the drilling depth in centimetre but serve as reference if there are wide variation in drilling depth the horizontal alignment may need to be adjusted see horizontal alignment ab en gb seed drilling weight transfer the weight transfer is normally preset to bar this rda bar accumulator for machine with wing packer rda bar rda bar tramlining the most important thing to remember about tramlining when drilling is to switch off the auto advance function as soon as the drill finish drilling or making normal turn on headland in the bout this mean the auto advance is switched off before the seed drill is raised to drill around post well or other obstacle in the bout when it is time to fill the seed hopper or take break raise the seed drill out of the most recent bout and wait approx second then switch off the auto advance however the auto advance need not be switched off if no additional lifting will take place during the pause engage the auto advance before the seed drill is raised again for the first time when you continue drilling ad en gb seed drilling tramlining hatch figure tramlining shut off take place with tramlining hatch the hatch are integrated into the tramlining motor that is mounted next to every output that must be sealed when tramlining is active in activated mode the hatch are pulled up and seal each output the motor are controlled via four busbar located in the bottom section of the distributor head the connection are on right on left ground and alarm signal carry out function control of the tramlining at the start of the season and at regular interval throughout the season setting tramline with the rda tramline can be laid at and so on with the rda tramline can be laid at and so on with the rda tramline can be laid at and so on the rda can also lay tramline at this doe however require modification to be made to are going to be before starting work press to access the general setting select tramlining setting using the button select the tramlining programme by entering the number of cycle bout to be included programme maximum of tramlining cycle for more detail refer to the specific instruction manual for service en gb seed drilling double tramlining system figure table the most commonly used tramlining alway travel forward when it is lowered into the ground keep the hydraulic lever in the push position the lowered position until the bout marker has been folded out completely check that the
