lowlift mode the bout marker retract on arrival at the headland when the seed drill is lifted if lowlift is engaged the machine is lifted to lowlift height when the seed drill is lifted at the headland the bout marker and tramlining automatically advance by one step if the seed drill doe not lift up lift stop is probably engaged and if the bout marker and tramlining do not advance auto advance is probably disengaged when turning in lowlift mode the following harrow work throughout the turn before reversing the tractor train lowlift must be switched off and the seed drill raised to highlift position drilling the headland in dry and fine condition the headland can be drilled first in wet condition or when drilling soil sensitive to compaction higher yield can be obtained if the headland is drilled last figure the best way to mark out the headland is by completing the last tillage operation before drilling the autumn harrowing by harrowing the headland last so that the outermost headland pass has the right distance from the edge of the field if seed coulter become blocked do not go under the machine unless it has first been properly secured with the yellow locking device securing the machine for service drilling around obstacle switch off the auto advance before raising the seed drill to sow around post well or other obstacle in the bout en gb seed drilling the bout marker are retracted to get past an obstacle by activating the lift stop and moving the hydraulic lever to the lifting position the bout marker will retract while the seed drill will remain in its working position fold down the bout marker again in this case the function selector for the auto advance or the marker do not need to be touched mechanical following harrow do not reverse the tractor unless the drill has been fully raised and the following harrow has sufficient clearance height if the machine has been set to the maximum drilling depth and the following harrow is subject to high load the clearance in the transport position may be limited figure select suitable working angle from hole sery figure the work pressure of the following harrow is adjusted using the adjusting screw use the ratchet supplied adjust the following harrow to remain in the work position when turning on the headland in the low lift position this will eradicate out the track of the drill when turning figure check that the following harrow tine trail between the wheel and in the wheel track this indicate that the following harrow tine are going between the furrow when this is happening high pressure can be applied on the following harrow without disrupting the seed this allow vaporisation layer to be harrowed while sowing ba en gb maintenance and service maintenance and service the machine must alway be secured before any type of service or maintenance work is commenced never stand near hydraulic hose under pressure drain the system accumulator before servicing the hydraulic system securing prior to servicing alway shut down the tractor and take out the ignition key in conjunction with work in the seed hopper and when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill never work under the drill during maintenance or service work unless it has been secured with trestle and the lifting cylinder has been blocked machine should be placed in the topmost position before the catch can be mounted be sure to alway lower the drill to the ground prior to performing any service on the hydraulic system also ensure that the surface supporting the trestle is firm enough to support them if the hopper is full which it ought not to be during maintenance work the weight of the seed drill is considerable when carrying out service and maintenance the drill should be in the lowered position and standing on flat surface exercise extreme caution when undertaking any kind of work with the machine hydraulic system wipe with clean paper or cloth place part on clean surface not directly on the work bench rinse before assembly for example with decreasing agent never rinse the bearing with high pressure water supply it is important to grease the bearing after washing in order to remove any water that has remained behind never clean bearing directly with high pressure water jet clean electrical component by blowing them with air or by wiping them with dampened cloth to maintain the machine high level of quality and operational safety use only derstad genuine spare part the warranty and any claim under it will become void if part other than genuine part are used figure lubricate the machine according to the interval specified in the lubrication schedule and alway before and after winter storage and after high pressure water cleaning before operation ensure that all nut and bolt have been tightened doe not apply to bolt in flexible joint during the season check periodically that the nut and bolt have not worked loose and inspect joint and hydraulic ram mounting for wear securing the machine for service when work is to be performed on the implement the machine must alway be parked on stable and level surface en gb maintenance and service when working on the hydraulic system the wing must be folded down and the implement lowered to the ground if the hydraulic system include accumulator these must be drained of oil before carrying out the service or repair work be very careful when working with the hydraulic system even if the tractor has been shut down and is depressurised there may still be some pressure left in the hydraulic hose check that the wing section locking hook are engaged before carrying out work underneath the folded up section for all work underneath the seed drill or when there is risk of crush injury the machine must be supported safely on stand secure the seed drill using trestle or similar and lock all the lifting cylinder using the appropriate yellow locking device ensure that the surface supporting the trestle is sufficiently firm if the hopper is full which it ought not to be during maintenance work the weight of the seed drill is considerable figure secure the machine by placing trestle in the appropriate securely supported figure checking disc wear the system disc is wearing part and must be replaced the multi tool can be used to measure the wear on the system disc place the multi tool on disc bolt read off the system disc radius the disc should be replaced when the radius is less than seal must alway be replaced at the same time as the hub changing disc the front tool must be securely placed on trestle before the disc are changed the disc are sharp so wear glove when replacing disc use ratchet wrench or better still nut runner the hub ring see figure must not be pinched during installation disc hub ensure that the seed drill is safely supported on stand be sure to keep dirt away whenever work need to be performed on the bearing figure clean edge thread and the shaft with steel brush before fitting the hub fit the cover ring and hub to the shaft fit the nut tighten the nut to tightening torque nm fit the ring at the same time as the disc the ring must not be pinched seed coulter installation height before commencing any work under the drill be sure to secure it in the raised position figure en gb maintenance and service to ensure satisfactory sowing result it is important that the seed coulter are correctly installed on the drill select the installation height based on the condition and disc wear when the drill has been lowered with the disc resting on firm ground the seed coulter should not touch the surface mm please note that as the measurement is reduced the seed coulter become more susceptible to impact with stone upon delivery the seed coulter are installed in position this installation height suit most operating condition figure drilling under very moist condition with lot of plant residue in the soil surface or shallow drilling in light or humus soil may cause the disc to stop occasionally this can be avoided by moving the coulter up to position position however can result in impaired seed placement the illustration below show an example of drilling when there is lot of plant residue on the soil surface in this case the seed coulter should be installed in position figure tightening nut figure the seed coulter are spring mounted on two bolt and the soft washer press the seed coulter closer to the disc as the nut are tightened the nut should not be tightened more than allow for the coulter to be rocked with the hand the coulter must not have too close contact with the disc since this will cause increased wear and rotational friction for very loose soil and or shallow drilling depth it may be necessary to loosen the nut when new seed coulter are mounted it is necessary to check the width of the slot between the disc and the coulter the spring should be wider at the top otherwise straw and plant residue may collect here if the seed coulter do not make contact at the correct place this can be adjusted by moving the metal plate at position and inside or outside the fixing bracket the point of contact can also be moved somewhat by tightening the front nut more than the rear one position reference measurement between the disc and the seed coulter mm mm mm changing the seed coulter mounting bolt the disc arm has two recessed bolt for coulter mounting if these cannot be driven out they must be cut and ground level with the arm it may be necessary to remove the whole disc arm to facilitate this figure en gb maintenance and service the bolt can then be driven out using mm diameter punch burr may be left in the hole after the bolt have been removed file them away to facilitate driving in the new bolt drive or press in the new bolt or pull them into place with the aid of few washer and nut not locknut oil the thread the bottom of the washer and the nut thread liberally use the nut to pull the bolt into place be sure to use sufficient number of washer to prevent the nut from reaching the bottom of the threaded section of the bolt locking device figure do not lubricate the locking device check its function regularly the locking hook on the back of the seed hopper must go into the lug on the side section front tool system disc system disc aggressive figure after couple of hour of work retighten the bolt intermediate packer never stand under the intermediate packer or seed drill if the intermediate packer is raised and only secured with the tractor hydraulic lifting arm prior to any service on the intermediate packer be sure to secure it properly with trestle etc on stable surface figure retightening all bolt it is imperative that all bolted joint be retightened after the first hectare work following this bolted joint need only be retightened once at the end of each season re adjustment figure if longitudinal play approx mm occur in the intermediate packer drawbar this can be adjusted by installing washer on the shaft in front of the drawbar if play of more than approx mm occur in the steering head pivot pin this may also be adjusted by installing washer on the underside en gb maintenance and service wheel leg pivot pin check that the large nut is fully tightened if play more than mm can still be felt in the large pivot pin spacer should be installed as follow figure raise the packer and disconnect the parallel stay loosen the nut after which the wheel leg can be retracted install new washer let the old one remain in place check for abnormal wear clean all pivot pin surface with diesel fuel or similar apply small amount of grease and reinstall figure tighten the nut to torque of nm check that all joint can move without any excessive effort and that there is no longer any play if the play still exist two additional spacer may be required wheel bearing adjustment it is important that the wheel hub bearing are retightened check that the wheel hub are free of play when the packer is in the raised position if considerable play can be felt the bearing should be tightened figure disassemble the wheel hub cap undo the locking pin that hold the castle nut in place and tighten the castle nut until the play disappear lock the nut with the pin clean the hub cap and fill it with fresh grease then refit it wheel changing wheel set the drill on solid surface hydraulic system the hydraulic system must alway be bled after servicing make sure there is nobody in the immediate working area of the machine move the lifting cylinder the bout marker cylinder and the front tool cylinder to their fully extended and fully retracted position few time until all air has been evacuated from the hydraulic system service of hydraulic component during servicing of the hydraulic system the machine must alway be folded out and lowered to the ground the hydraulic circuit must be depressurised never stand near hydraulic hose under pressure drain the system accumulator before servicing the hydraulic system all work and repair must be carried out in line with qualified procedure the highest possible level of cleanliness should be observed when replacing sealing kit be careful not to damage any surface of the hydraulic component while working and that all gasket are mounted correctly check the component for abnormal wear and tear for example burr or scratch which may indicate the presence of dirt in the hydraulic system or wrongly weighted component emptying the hydraulic system accumulator before starting service work on the part of the hydraulic covering the folding and weight transfer system the accumulator must first be drained of oil emptying the hydraulic system accumulator mean that the pressure on the manometer will be zero when performing service and or repair work on the hydraulic system the seed drill must alway be folded out figure open the weight transfer cock en gb maintenance and service figure raise the seed drill to its top position release the pressure from the seed drill folding system using the hydraulic control check that the pressure on the manometer is zero lower the seed drill back down to set the pressure again see setting the weight transfer inspection of hydraulic hose check the hydraulic hose every three month or every hour whichever come first date marking and max pressure on hydraulic hose the hose end are marked with the date of manufacture year and month and max pressure bleeding the hydraulic system see adjusting the master slave system replacing the oil filter make sure that service and maintenance work to the hydraulic system is done in clean surrounding alway depressurise the system before changing the filter the oil filter must be changed according to the interval in the lubrication schedule and after every season figure unscrew and remove the filter housing place the filter housing on clean surface take off the old filter element and fit new one replace and tighten the filter housing by brake hydraulic brake at the start of the season figure adjust the brake pad screw the adjustment screw clockwise while rotating the wheel slowly turn the adjustment screw until you feel resistance in the wheel as they rotate this mean that the brake pad are in gentle contact with the brake drum to decrease the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum turn the adjustment screw clockwise to increase the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum turn the adjustment screw anticlockwise check that all line hose and brake cylinder are undamaged and that they are not leaking ca ba en gb maintenance and service changing brake equipment component the brake pad must not be replaced individually all the brake pad must be replaced simultaneously the same apply when replacing the wheel cylinder which must also all be replaced at the same time the brake system wheel cylinder brake pad and brake drum are wearing part bleeding the brake system following maintenance or other work on the hydraulic system the brake system must be bled before use bleed as follow figure top up the container with oil to the max marking connect brake bleeding tool max bar to the main cylinder or connect the compressed air line and control line to the tractor and apply the brake or connect the compressed air line and the control line to an external compressed air source max bar figure bleed the system using the nipple which is located adjacent to the brake line connection at each wheel be careful first bleed the wheel that is furthest away from the main cylinder the wheel furthest away on the right hand side and continue to bleed left wheel right wheel until all the air has been forced out of the line connect the transparent hose to the nipple and allow surplus oil to run into suitable container close the nipple when no more air bubble can be seen in the hose if the oil is not topped up when the level is below the min marking there is risk of air penetrating the system en gb maintenance and service pneumatic brake daily maintenance drain off the condensation water from the compressed air container figure open the discharge valve on the underside of the container figure check the oil level in the container on the main cylinder the level should be between the max and min marking on the container top up with mineral oil type gl or equivalent if the level is too low the mineral oil should be type gl or equivalent other type of brake fluid will damage the system seal at the start of the season figure adjust the brake pad turn the adjustment screw clockwise while rotating the wheel slowly turn the adjustment screw until you feel resistance in the wheel as they rotate this mean that the brake pad are in gentle contact with the brake drum turn the adjustment screw clockwise to decrease the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum turn the adjustment screw anticlockwise to increase the distance between the brake pad and the brake drum check the oil level top up with fresh oil if necessary only use mineral oil type gl or equivalent the mineral oil should be type gl or equivalent other type of brake fluid will damage the system seal check that all line hose and brake cylinder are undamaged and that they are not leaking checking the pneumatic brake system figure en gb maintenance and service check for wear in the brake on the indicator plate when the indicator pin move from to it is time to check and service the system check that the fluid level in the brake fluid reservoir doe not fall below the minimum indicator line check that all line hose and brake cylinder are undamaged and that they are not leaking figure if the brake action is slow remove the line filter on the air pipe and clean or replace the filter as necessary changing brake equipment component incorrect handling can result in the brake not working risk of great danger in road traffic in case of any uncertainty contact professional service personnel the brake shoe must not be changed separately all brake shoe on the same shaft must be changed at the same time the same apply to brake cylinder which must also be changed in pair bleeding the pneumatic brake following maintenance or other work on the brake system it must be bled before use figure top up the container with iso mineral oil or equivalent for hydraulic system to the max marking figure connect transparent hose to the nipple and allow any excess oil to run into suitable container close the nipple when no more air bubble can be seen in the hose connect brake bleeding tool max bar to the main cylinder or connect the compressed air line and control line to the tractor and apply the brake or connect the compressed air line and control line to an external compressed air source max bar the mineral oil should be iso or equivalent other type of brake fluid will damage the system seal if the oil is not topped up when the level is below the min mark there is risk of air penetrating the system bleed the system using the nipple which is located adjacent to the brake line connection on the left of each wheel be careful bleed the wheel from the right and continue to bleed all the wheel until all air has been forced out of the line bc en gb maintenance and service figure when the system has been fully bled the indicator pin should be shorter than mm and indicate figure press in the indicator pin turn the lever on the control panel back to half full hopper and apply the brake check that the indicator pin still point to if not turn the lever to the maintenance position and repeat from step press in the indicator pin turn the lever on the control panel to full hopper and apply the brake check that the indicator pin point to if not turn the lever to the maintenance position and repeat from step seed transport metering unit and rape seed brush the interior of the metering unit must be cleaned and checked at regular interval for wear of the plastic and rubber part it is particularly important to ensure that additive are not deposited in the flute reducing their volume clean the seed meter as follow the cover with scale is drained from the bottom and can be flushed out after the metering unit has been turned to almost zero figure when necessary clean inside the transparent transmission cover figure check that the cell wheel flap do not seize but lie against the bottom of the track when setting from zero and upward check the cabling rape seed brush when setting and sowing oilseed plant the rape seed brush es must alway be inspected and cleaned when necessary en gb maintenance and service check that the brush rotate easily on their shaft rape seed brush must not be lubricated cleaning the seed head outlet check at regular interval and at the end of each season that no seed or packaging remnant have become stuck in the seed hose or the seed head outlet check the function of the tramlining hatch at the same time and clean these figure open the cover when cleaning by loosening the three screw retractable distributor head rda and only to facilitate cleaning and service of the seed hopper one of the distributor head is retractable as result of its split delivery pipe the lever has two fixed catch position folded or closed distributor head fold the distributor head press down on the catch and lift the lever crushing risk pull the lever up until it click into the catch position close the distributor head check that the contact surface and the ring that is to be sealed against the delivery pipe part are free from waste matter or seed if the ring doe not seal properly air leakage will occur and this will disturb the seed feed output press down on the catch and lower the lever crushing risk lower the lever all the way down and press down until it click into the catch position aa en gb maintenance and service replacement of the distributor head tramlining motor figure the distributor head valve can be replaced as needed to undo these figure to remove faulty motor from the distributor head undo the three screw use the torx screwdriver supplied fit new motor with the three screw use the torx screwdriver supplied also see tramline motor and circuit board repair and replacement of the seed hose figure repair if seed hose is damaged due to abrasion or folding it can be jointed with sleeve cut the hose at the centre of the fold or damage where necessary the smallest possible part of the hose can be cut away if the hose become too stiff at the joint and cannot be bent sufficiently when the machine is lowered it may be necessary to replace the entire seed hose or to replace part of the hose and joint it in two place changing seed hose use soapy water on the hose when fitting them to seed coulter twist the hose anticlockwise when removing or fitting which will help the spiral reinforcement to open the hose little cut replacement hose to the same length as the one it is to replace when storing for longer period when the seed drill is not being used it should be stored indoor this is very important given that the seed drill has electronic instrument disconnect the battery to prevent current leakage discharging from the battery braked machine should not have the parking brake applied but be secured with chock with pneumatic brake the brake are released by pressing the retarder valve in the control box and the battery should be stored at room temperature when stored for long period machine part with shiny finish such as piston rod and wearing part should be treated with rust protection before longer storage period make sure that the machine has been emptied and thoroughly cleaned ab en gb hydraulic hydraulic rda folding figure valve block folding hydraulic block folding master slave system system derstad ab visit vaderstad com en gb troubleshooting troubleshooting general information on troubleshooting many of the function of the seed drill are controlled by sery of electrical hydraulic and mechanical component good way to immediately exclude many of the source of fault is to first determine whether or not the fault is electrical because of this first check whether the electrical circuit is intact right up to the last electrical component in the chain then continue troubleshooting by first performing the easiest inspection in order to quickly exclude other source of fault electrical fault general check in the event of electrical fault check that all the connector pin and socket are clean free from damage and are not pushed inward spray the switch with contact spray that is intended for use with electronic equipment check that all cable are correctly connected and that no cable is trapped or otherwise damaged check that the power cable fuse is intact hydraulic fault general check in the event of hydraulic fault check that the hydraulic hose are connected to the correct socket on the tractor hose with the same colour coding constitute pair make sure the hydraulic quick coupler are designed for and suited to the tractor coupling there is wide variety of connector on the market all standardised but still there are problem problem can manifest themselve so that female and male coupling operate as non return valve that is that machine can be raised but not lowered or vice versa the problem may be aggravated by large flow or worn coupling hydraulic electric valve an electric valve has coil which act as an electromagnet when an electrical current is connected to the valve it is easy to determine whether or not the power is on the connecting contact diode light up and the coil become warm after few minute the top nut will also become magnetic figure use small chisel or the side of blade to determine whether or not the top nut has become magnetic the nut is alway weakly magnetic so try both with and without power figure the bout marker valve and receive current when the control unit indicate active bout marker while the machine is below lowlift height the lift stop valve receive current when the lift stop is engaged or during lift in the lowlift mode the pre emergence bout marker solenoid valve receive current when the control unit indicate active tramlining while the machine is below lowlift height the bout marker stop valve receive current when the control system is activated to finely adjust the drilling depth the sink stop valve receive current when the machine reach the set drilling depth outgoing signal to bout marker and the pre emergence bout marker are not activated until the machine is below lowlift height en gb troubleshooting reed switch figure magnetic switch known also as reed switch is switch or sensor that react to magnetic field the magnetic switch is glass tube containing two metal reed that attract each other when subjected to magnetic field from magnet see the illustration reed switch operation can easily be tested using multimeter and magnet inductive sensor figure react to metal object passing within distance of mm function testing can be carried out easily since diode at the rear of the sensor light up each time an object is detected capacitive sensor figure react to object that contain moisture for example grain or hand and so on function testing is easily carried out since diode at the rear of the sensor light up each time such an object is detected the sensitivity of the sensor can be adjusted by use of the screw next to the led various type of grain and fertiliser contain different amount of moisture for this reason adjustment may be necessary under certain condition en gb troubleshooting troubleshooting list the control unit doe not operate when the main switch is switched on see electrical fault the bout marker cannot be unfolded check in accordance with electrical fault check in accordance with hydraulic fault check whether the control unit is indicating that bout marker is active check whether the machine is at or above the lowlift height for safety reason the bout marker cannot be unfolded in the lowlift mode or at higher height check the lowlift switch remove the switch connector to workstation and check the bout marker function check the wing lowering switch this switch block the bout marker valve current signal when the seed drill is folded up to prevent bout marker from unintentionally being lowered in this mode remove the switch connector to workstation and check the bout marker function check the power supply to the two centre solenoid valve and on the top of the valve block see hydraulic electric valve the bout marker valve must receive current when the respective indication lamp are lit the bout marker fail to retract probably due to dirt in the solenoid valve note that dirt causing fault is often invisible it is recommended that solenoid valve are changed it should alway be possible to retract both bout marker regardless of the setting and indication on the control unit and even if the control unit is switched off the folded up bout marker slowly extend inadvertently check whether the indication for this bout marker show that it is inactive on the control unit check that there is no current being supplied to the solenoid valve see hydraulic electric valve swap the right and left bout marker solenoid valve if the fault shift to the other side that solenoid valve must be faulty if the fault remain the bout marker hydraulic cylinder is probably defective remove the solenoid valve and check that it is free from dirt and that the outer seal of the valve are intact and undamaged note first lower the seed drilling unit to the ground and release the hydraulic pressure check that the bout marker cylinder is not leaking externally or internally if the seal need to be changed see service of hydraulic component if the bout marker unfold when the machine is parked set the stop device so that the machine is resting on the wheel disc and support stand use the cotter pin en gb troubleshooting automatic switching of the bout or tramline marker doe not take place is auto advance selected on the control unit has the bout marker function selector been set to the alternating position the indicator lamp in the button should light has the correct tramlining program been selected is the lowlift switch correctly adjusted see setting the lowlift height when lowlift position is reached the signal for auto advance is given check whether the lowlift switch holder is crooked the holder is stuck in the wrong position or the magnetic sensor is damaged has the magnet come off or become loose check for possible break in the wiring or short circuit the seed drill can be raised even though the lift stop is engaged check that the solenoid valve on the valve block is receiving current see hydraulic electric valve check the lowlift switch as described above if the lift stop valve has no current see electrical fault if the lift stop valve has voltage see hydraulic fault the seed drill cannot be raised or lowered check that the lift stop or lowlift mode has not been engaged see hydraulic fault if the seed drill cannot be raised check that the lift stop or lowlift mode has not been engaged see hydraulic fault check whether the lift stop solenoid valve is receiving current either via short circuit or because of another fault see hydraulic electric valve switch off the control unit and check whether the seed drill can be raised if the unit still cannot be raised the fault must be hydraulic check whether the short circuit is in the cable braiding or intermediate cable and which function is affected the control unit is giving warning that the fan speed is too low or too high note no warning occur if fan hose has come loose or the ejector hook are not in place fan check that the hydraulic hose are connected properly and that the hydraulic outlet is activated check the control unit programming are the pressure and flow as they should be on the tractor bar and at least min is the tractor flow valve correctly adjusted is the fan speed regulator correctly adjusted not supplied as standard used when the tractor doe not have flow control en gb troubleshooting the shaft seal in the fan hydraulic motor is leaking leakage from the hydraulic motor shaft seal may be due to damage during assembly wear because of dirt wear because of high pressure in the drain line destroyed seal because of extremely high pressure in the drain line the pressure in the drain line can be measured on the block use bar pressure gauge the maximum pressure during operation should be bar high pressure may be due to large oil flow in the drain line back pressure in the tractor hydraulic valve detach the drain line from the tractor and remove the quick release coupling start the fan collect the drained oil in container measure the pressure measure the amount of oil that arrive over period of one minute considerable decrease in pressure indicate problem with the tractor hydraulic valve slight decrease in pressure indicate large oil flow the drain flow should normally be less than litre per minute large oil flow may be due to worn motor leaking non return valve in the connection block oil from the return line leaking into the drain line the control unit warn there is problem with the metering unit operation if there is sensor fault check the operation of the sensor see inductive sensor check that the toothed washer in the metering unit is not damaged check that the inductive sensor is sufficiently close to the rotating toothed washer adjust if necessary the area meter speedometer show no value or an incorrect value has the correct number of pulse per metre been programmed in if too low speed or too small an area is displayed then reduce the number of pulse per metre if too high speed or too large an area is displayed then increase the number of pulse per metre isobus control change the number of pulse by pressing the field which display the radar pulse metre see the section on calibration in the instruction manual for service the tramlining hatch get stuck check whether the long piston rod of the folding cylinder is completely extended and that the rod doe not slowly retract during driving check the setting of the wing section see adjusting the wing section on rda for the weight transfer setting see setting the weight transfer check the setting of the master slave system see adjusting the master slave system en gb troubleshooting disc do not rotate properly are the seed coulter exposed to undue stress the seed coulter should sit very is the soil very loose it may be necessary to compact the soil using roller is the drilling depth too shallow have the seed coulter been installed too low the disc provide better traction if the seed coulter are raised one notch are the disc excessively worn doe the soil surface contain lot of plant residue are the front tool tine being used too roughly making the soil very loose the seed drill doe not place seed at the bottom of the furrow are the seed coulter excessively worn are the seed coulter set one notch too high the drill will place the seed more precisely if the coulter are set low this doe not normally need to be changed for different soil type the fan speed is irregular replace the hydraulic oil filter the seed application rate is irregular replace the proportional valve in the fan block the left metering unit doe not rotate even though the drive unit is rotating check the locking pin between the metering unit and the drive unit and replace if necessary en gb seeding table seeding table alway perform seed calibration test the content of the seeding table must be viewed as guideline only new feed sample should be taken regularly in the case of small feed quantity check the area driven and the amount of seed fed every time you fill up wheat rye corn oat wheat rye corn oat kg scale value kg ha bean pea lupin vetch maize bean pea lupin vetch maize kg scale value kg ha service manual provide information about the specification system introduction service procedure and adjustment of the new mg vehicle how to use this manual general to assist in the use of this manual it is divided into section and sub section the relevant sub section title is given at the header of each page there is single content section at the front of the manual which is divided by section and sub section the book is numbered from page the page number is given at the bottom of each page the individual action of the repair operation must be followed in the sequence in which they appear item number in the illustration refer to the relevant numbered text under the illustration adjustment and repair operation include reference to service tool number and the associated illustration depict the tool in use adjustment and repair operation also include reference to wear limit relevant data torque figure and specialist information and useful assembly detail each adjustment or repair operation is given unique repair operation number warning caution note and tip have the following meaning when encountering warning you will be asked to take necessary action or not to take prohibited action if warning is not heeded the following consequence may occur serious bodily injury or death to the technician serious bodily injury or death to other technician in the workplace area serious bodily injury to the driver and or passenger of the vehicle if the vehicle has been improperly repaired caution call special attention to necessary action or to prohibited action if caution is not heeded the following consequence may occur damage to the vehicle unnecessary vehicle repair unnecessary component replacement improper operation or performance of the system or component under repair damage to any system or component which are dependent upon the proper operation of the system or component under repair improper operation or performance of any system or component which are dependent upon the proper operation or performance of the system or component under repair damage to fastener basic tool or special tool the leakage of coolant lubricant or other vital fluid note statement emphasize necessary characteristic of diagnostic or repair procedure note statement are designed to do the following clarify procedure present additional information for accomplishing procedure give insight into the reason or reason for performing procedure in the manner recommended present information that will help to accomplish procedure in more effective manner present information that give the technician the benefit of past experience in accomplishing procedure with greater ease tip give helpful information reference reference to the lh or rh side given in this manual are made when viewing the vehicle from the rear with the engine and gearbox assembly removed the crankshaft pulley end of the engine is referred to as the front operation covered in this manual do not include reference to testing the vehicle after repair it is essential that work is inspected and tested after completion and if necessary road test of the vehicle is carried out this is of particular importance where safety related item are concerned dimension the dimension quoted are to design engineering specification service limit are included where applicable our company are constantly seeking to improve the specification design and production of their vehicle and alteration take place accordingly while every effort has been made to ensure the accuracy of this manual it should not be regarded as an infallible guide to current specification of any particular vehicle repair and replacement part when replacement part are required it is essential that only our company recommended part are used attention is particularly drawn to the following point concerning repair and the fitting of replacement part and accessory safety feature and corrosion prevention treatment embodied in the vehicle may be impaired if other than smc recommended part are fitted torque wrench setting figure given in this manual must be used locking device where specified must be fitted if the efficiency of locking device is impaired during removal it must be renewed the term of the vehicle warranty may be invalidated by the fitting of part other than smc recommended part warranty smc dealer are obliged to supply only smc recommended part special tool special tool have been developed to facilitate removal dismantling and assembly of mechanical component in cost effective and time efficient manner the use of special tool also help prevent the potential for damage to component some operation in the manual cannot be carried out properly without the aid of the relevant special tool special tool can be obtained from the following supplier shanghai runyue industrial development co ltd address skirt building lianhua building tianzhou road shanghai post code tel content general information general
