socket glossary list general information general information general precaution dangerous substance handled with care can be hazardous to both personal health and the environment warning many liquid and other substance used in vehicle are poisonous and should under no circumstance be consumed and should as far as possible be kept from contact with the skin these liquid and substance include acid antifreeze brake fluid fuel washer additive lubricant refrigerant and various adhesive warning alway read carefully the instruction printed on label or stamped on component and obey them implicitly such instruction are included for reason of your health and personal safety never disregard them rubber many ring seal hose flexible pipe and other similar item which appear to be natural rubber are in fact made of material called fluoroelastomer under normal operating condition this material is safe and doe not present health hazard however if the material is damaged by fire or excessive heating it can break down and produce highly corrosive hydrofluoric acid contact with hydrofluoric acid can cause serious burn on contact with skin if skin contact doe occur remove any contaminated clothing immediately irrigate affected area of skin with copious amount of cold water or limewater for to minute obtain medical assistance immediately should any material be in burnt or overheated condition handle with extreme caution and wear protective clothing seamless industrial glove protective apron etc decontaminate and dispose of glove immediately after use lubricating oil avoid excessive skin contact with used lubricating oil and alway adhere to the health protection precaution warning avoid excessive skin contact with used engine oil used engine oil contain potentially harmful contaminant which may cause skin cancer or other serious skin disorder warning avoid excessive skin contact with mineral oil mineral oil remove the natural fat from the skin leading to dryness irritation and dermatitis health protection precaution the following precaution should be observed at all time wear protective clothing including impervious glove where practicable avoid prolonged and repeated contact with oil particularly used engine oil do not put oily rag in pocket avoid contaminating clothe particularly those next to the skin with oil overall must be cleaned regularly discard heavily soiled clothing and oil impregnated footwear first aid treatment should be obtained immediately for open cut and wound apply barrier cream before each work period to help prevent lubricating oil from contaminating the skin wash with soap and water to ensure all oil is removed proprietary skin cleanser and nail brush will help use moisturiser after cleaning preparation containing lanolinhelpreplacetheskin snaturaloilswhichhavebeen removed do not use petrol gasoline kerosene diesel fuel oil thinner or solvent for cleaning skin where practicable degrease component prior to handling if skin disorder develop obtain medical advice without delay wear eye protection goggle or face shield if there is risk of eye contamination eye wash facility should be provided in close vicinity to the work area general information safety instruction jacking caution it is best to use the lift when working under the vehicle be sure to hold the wheel with chock and apply parking brake alway use the recommended jacking point alway ensure that any lifting apparatus has sufficient load capacity for the weight to be lifted ensure the vehicle is standing on level ground prior to lifting or jacking apply the parking brake and chock the wheel warning do not work on or under vehicle supported only by jack alway support the vehicle on safety stand do not leave tool lifting equipment spilt oil etc around or on the work bench area alway keep clean and tidy work area brake shoe and pad alway fit the correct grade and specification of brake lining when renewing brake pad and brake shoe alway replace as complete axle set brake hydraulic observe the following recommendation when working on the brake system ensure that hose run in natural curve and are not kinked or twisted ensure that hose run in natural curve and are not kinked or twisted fit brake pipe securely in their retaining clip and ensure that the pipe cannot contact potential chafing point container used for brake fluid must be kept absolutely clean do not store brake fluid in an unsealed container it will absorb water and in this condition would be dangerous to use due to lowering of its boiling point do not allow brake fluid to be contaminated with mineral oil or put new brake fluid in container which has previously contained mineral oil do not re use brake fluid removed from the system alway use clean brake fluid or recommended alternative to clean hydraulic component after disconnection of brake pipe and hose immediately fit suitable blanking cap or plug to prevent the ingress of dirt only use the correct brake fitting with compatible thread observe absolute cleanliness when working with hydraulic component cooling system cap and plug expansion tank cap and coolant drain or bleed screw when the engine is hot especially if it is overheated to avoid the possibility of scalding allow the engine to cool before attempting removal general information environmental precaution general this section provide general information which can help to reduce the environmental impact from the activity carried out in workshop emission to air many of the activity that are carried out in workshop emit gase and fume which contribute to global warming depletion of the ozone layer and or the formation of photochemical smog at ground level by considering how the workshop activity are carried out these gase and fume can be minimised thus reducing the impact on the environment exhaust fume running car engine is an essential part of workshop activity and exhaust fume need to be ventilated to atmosphere however the amount of time engine are running and the position of the vehicle should be carefully considered at all time to reduce the release of poisonous gase and minimise the inconvenience to people living nearby solvent some of the cleaning agent used are solvent based and will evaporate to atmosphere if used carelessly or if can are left unsealed all solvent container should be firmly closed when not needed and solvent should be used sparingly suitable alternative material may be available to replace some of the commonly used solvent similarly many paint are solvent based and the spray should be minimised to reduce solvent emission refrigerant discharge and replacement of these material from air conditioning unit should only be carried out using the correct equipment engine alway adhere to the following don leave engine running unnecessarily minimise testing time and check where the exhaust fume are being blown material keep lid on container of solvent only use the minimum quantity consider alternative material minimise over spray when painting gase use the correct equipment for collecting refrigerant don burn rubbish on site discharge to water oil petrol solvent acid hydraulic oil antifreeze and other such substance should never be poured down the drain and every precaution must be taken to prevent spillage reaching the drain handling of such material must take place well away from the drain and preferably in an area with kerb or wall around it to prevent discharge into the drain if spillage occur it should be soaked up immediately having spill kit available will make this easier checklist alway adhere to the following disposal and spillage prevention instruction never pour anything down drain without first checking that it is environmentally safe to do so and that it doe not contravene any local regulation store liquid in walled area protect bulk storage tank from vandalism by locking the valve transfer liquid from one container to another in an area away from open drain ensure lid are replaced securely on container make sure that tap on liquid container are secure and cannot be accidentally turned on have spill kit available near to point of storage and handling of liquid spill kit special material are available to absorb number of different substance they can be in granular form ready to use and bought in convenient container for storage disposal of used spill absorbing material is dealt with in waste management section land contamination oil fuel and solvent etc can contaminate any soil that they are allowed to contact such material should never be disposed of by pouring onto soil and every precaution must be taken to prevent spillage reaching soil waste material stored on open ground could also leak or have polluting substance washed off them that would contaminate the land alway store these material in suitable robust container checklist alway adhere to the following don pour or spill anything onto the soil or bare ground don store waste material on bare ground see spillage prevention list general information local issue number of environmental issue will be of particular concern to resident and other neighbour close to the site the sensitivity of these issue will depend on the proximity of the site and the layout and amount of activity carried on at the site car alarm testing panel beating hammering and other such noisy activity should whenever possible be carried out indoor with door and window shut or as far away from house as possible be sensitive to the time of day when these activity are carried out and minimise the time of the noisy operation particularly in the early morning and late evening another local concern will be the smell from the various material used using less solvent paint and petrol could help prevent this annoyance about traffic congestion noise and exhaust fume be sensitive to these concern and try to minimise inconvenience from delivery customer and servicing operation checklist alway adhere to the following identify where the neighbour who are likely to be affected are situated minimise noise smell and traffic nuisance prevent litter by disposing of waste in the correct manner have waste container emptied regularly waste management one of the major way that pollution can be reduced is by the careful handling storage and disposal of all waste material that occuronsite what the waste material are but also to have the necessary documentation and to know local regulation that apply handling and storage of waste they should be stored in such way as to prevent the escape of the material to land water or air they must also be segregated into different type of waste oil metal battery used vehicle component this will prevent any reaction between different material and assist in disposal disposal of waste are authorised to carry those particular waste material and have all the necessary documentation the waste carrier is responsible for ensuring that the waste is taken to the correct disposal site dispose of waste in accordance with the following guideline fuel hydraulic fluid anti freeze and oil keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor refrigerant collect in specialist equipment and reuse detergent safe to pour down the foul drain if diluted paint thinner keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor component send back to supplier for refurbishment or disassemble and reuse any suitable part dispose of the remainder in ordinary waste small part reuse any suitable part dispose of the remainder in ordinary waste metal can be sold if kept separate from general waste tyre keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor packaging compact as much as possible and dispose of in ordinary waste asbesto material keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor oily and fuel waste rag used spill kit material keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor air filter keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor rubber plastic dispose of in ordinary waste hose dispose of in ordinary waste battery keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor airbag explosive keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor electrical component send back to supplier for refurbishment or disassemble and reuse any suitable part dispose of the remainder in ordinary waste catalyst can be sold if kept separate from general waste used spill absorbing material keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor general information general fitting instruction component removal whenever possible clean component and surrounding area before removal blank off opening exposed by component removal immediately seal fuel oil or hydraulic line when aperture are exposed use plastic cap or plug to prevent loss of fluid and ingress of dirt close the open end of oilway exposed by component removal with tapered hardwood plug or conspicuous plastic plug immediately component is removed place it in suitable container use separate container for each component and its associated part clean bench and provide marking material label and container before dismantling component dismantling particularly when brake fuel or hydraulic system part are being worked on particle of dirt or cloth fragment could cause serious malfunction if trapped in these system blow out all tapped hole crevice oilway and fluid passage with an air line ensure that any ring used ifdisturbed during the process use marking ink to identify mating part and ensure correct reassembly do not use centre punch or scriber to mark part they could initiate crack or distortion in marked component wire together mating part where necessary to prevent accidental interchange roller bearing component attach label to all part which are to be renewed and to part requiring further inspection before being passed for reassembly place these part in separate container from those containing part for rebuild do not discard part due for renewal until after comparing it with new part to ensure that its correct replacement has been obtained cleaning component alway use the recommended cleaning agent or equivalent ensure that adequate ventilation is provided when volatile degreasing agent are being used do not use degreasing equipment for component containing item which could be damaged by the use of this process general inspection all component should be inspected for wear or damage before being reassembled never inspect component for wear or dimensional check unless it is absolutely clean slight smear of grease can conceal an incipient failure when component is to be checked dimensionally against recommended value use the appropriate measuring equipment surface plate micrometer dial gauge etc ensure the measuring equipment is calibrated and in good serviceable condition reject component if its dimension are outside the specified tolerance or if it appear to be damaged part may be refitted if its critical dimension is exactly to its tolerance limit and it appear to be in satisfactory condition use plastigauge for checking bearing surface clearance general information ball and roller bearing general when removing and installing bearing ensure that the following practice are observed to ensure component serviceability remove all trace of lubricant from bearing under inspection by cleaning with suitable degreasant maintain absolute cleanliness throughout operation hold inner race of bearing between finger and thumb of one hand and spin outer race to check that it revolve absolutely smoothly repeat holding outer race and spinning inner race rotate outer ring gently with reciprocating motion while holding inner ring feel for any check or obstruction to rotation reject bearing if action is not perfectly smooth lubricate bearing with generous amount of lubricant appropriate to installation inspect shaft and bearing housing for discoloration or other marking which indicate movement between bearing and housing ensure that shaft and housing are clean and free from burr before fitting bearing if one bearing of pair show an imperfection it is advisable to replace both with new bearing an exception could be if the faulty bearing had covered low mileage and it can be established that damage is confined to only one bearing never refit ball or roller bearing without first ensuring that it is in fully serviceable condition when hub bearing are removed or displaced new bearing must be fitted do not attempt to refit the old hub bearing when fitting bearing to shaft only apply force to the inner ring of the bearing when fitting bearing into housing only apply force to the outer ring of the bearing in the case of grease lubricated bearing eg hub bearing fill the space between bearing and outer seal with the recommended grade of grease before fitting seal alway mark component of separable bearing eg taper roller bearing when dismantling to ensure correct reassembly never fit new roller in used outer ring alway fit complete new bearing assembly general information oil seal general alway renew oil seal which have been removed from their working location whether as an individual component or as part of an assembly never use seal which has been improperly stored or handled such as hung on hook or nail carefully examine seal before fitting to ensure that it is clean and undamaged ensure the surface on which the new seal is to run is free of burr or scratch renew the component if the original sealing surface cannot be completely restored protect the seal from any surface which it has to pass when being fitted use protective sleeve or tape to cover the relevant surface lubricate the sealing lip with recommended lubricant before use to prevent damage during initial use on dual lipped seal smear the area between the lip with grease ifasealspringisprovided ensurethatitisfittedcorrectly place lip of seal toward fluid to be sealed and slide into position on shaft use fitting sleeve where possible to protect sealing lip from damage by sharp corner thread or spline if fitting sleeve is not available use plastic tube or tape to prevent damage to the sealing lip grease outside diameter of seal place square to housing recess and press into position using great care and if possible bell piece to ensure that seal is not tilted in some case it may be preferable to fit seal to housing before fitting to shaft never let weight of unsupported shaft rest in seal use the recommended service tool to fit an oil seal if the correct service tool is not available use suitable tube approximately mm in smaller than the outside diameter of the seal use hammer very gently on drift if suitable press is not available use the recommended service tool to fit an oil seal if the correct service tool is not available use suitable tube approximately mm in smaller than the outside diameter of the seal use hammer very gently on drift if suitable press is not available general information joint and joint face general fit joint dry unless specified otherwise alway use the correct gasket as specified when sealing compound is used apply in thin uniform film to metal surface take care to prevent sealing compound from entering oilway pipe or blind tapped hole if gasket and or sealing compound is recommended for use remove all trace of old sealing compound prior to reassembly do not use tool which will damage the joint face and smooth out any scratch or burr using an oil stone prior to reassembly blow through any pipe channel or crevice with compressed air locking device general alway replace locking device with one of the same design tab washer alway release locking tab and fit new locking washer do not re use locking tab locking nut alway use backing spanner when loosening or tightening locking nut brake and fuel pipe union roll pin alway fit new roll pin of an interference fit in the hole circlip alway fit new circlip of the correct size for the groove key and keyway remove burr from edge of keyway with fine file and clean thoroughly before attempting to refit key cleanandinspectkeyclosely if indistinguishable from new as any indentation may indicate the onset of wear split pin alway fit new split pin of the correct size for the hole in the bolt or stud general information screw thread general metric thread to iso standard are used damaged nut bolt and screw must alway be discarded cleaning damaged thread with die or tap impair the strength and fit of the thread and is not recommended alway ensure that replacement bolt are at least equal in strength to those replaced castellated nut must not be loosened to accept split pin except in recommended case when this form part of an adjustment do not allow oil or grease to enter blind threaded hole the hydraulic action on screwing in the bolt or stud could split the housing alway tighten nut or bolt to the recommended torque figure damaged or corroded thread can affect the torque reading to check or re tighten bolt or screw to specified torque figure first loosen quarter of turn then re tighten to the correct torque figure oil thread before tightening to ensure free running thread except in the case of thread treated with sealant lubricant and selflocking nut fastener identification bolt identification an iso metric bolt or screw made of steel and larger than mm in diameter can be identified by either of the symbol iso or embossed or indented on top of the bolt head thetopofthe bolt head is also marked with symbol indicating the strength grade as an alternative some bolt and screw have the and strength grade symbol stamped on the flat of the hexagon encapsulated bolt and screw encapsulated bolt and screw have microencapsulated locking agent pre applied to the thread they are identified by coloured section which extend around the thread the locking agent is released and activated by the assembly process and is then chemically cured to provide the locking action unless specific repair procedure state otherwise encapsulated bolt may be reused providing the thread are undamaged and the following procedure is adopted remove loose adhesive from the bolt and housing thread ensure thread are clean and free of oil and grease apply an approved locking agent general information an encapsulated bolt may be replaced with bolt of equivalent specification provided it is treated with an approved locking agent self locking bolt and screw self locking bolt and screw nylon patched or trilobular thread can be reused providing resistance can be felt when the locking portion enter the female thread nylon patched bolt and screw have locking agent pre applied to the thread they are identified by the presence of coloured section of thread which extend for up to around the thread trilobular bolt powerlok have special thread form which create slight interference with the thread of the hole or nut into which it is screwed do not reuse self locking fastener in critical location enginebearing alwaysusethecorrectreplacement self locking nut bolt or screw do not fit non self locking fastener in application where self locking nut bolt or screw is specified nut identification nut with an iso metric thread is marked on one face or on one of the flat of the hexagon with the strength grade symbol or some nut with strength grade or are also marked and some have the metric symbol on the flat opposite the strength grade marking when tightening slotted or castellated nut never loosen it to insert split pin except where recommended as part of an adjustment if difficulty is experienced alternative washer or nut should be selected or the washer thickness reduced where bearing preload is involved nut should be tightened in accordance with special instruction self locking nut self locking nut nylon insert or deferred thread nut can be reused providing resistance can be felt when the locking portion of the nut pass over the thread of the bolt or stud where self locking nut have been removed it is advisable to replace them with new one of the same type don use non self lock nut in the area where self lock nut must be used general information flexible pipe and hose general when removing and installing flexible hydraulic pipe and hose ensure that the following practice are observed to ensure component serviceability clean end fitting and the area surrounding them as thoroughly as possible obtain appropriate plug or cap before detaching hose end fitting so that the port can be immediately covered to prevent the ingress of dirt clean hose externally and blow through with airline examine carefully for crack separation of ply security of end fitting and external damage reject any faulty hose when refitting hose ensure that no unnecessary bend are introduced and that hose is not twisted before or during tightening of union nut fit cap to seal hydraulic union and plug to its socket after removal to prevent ingress of dirt absolute cleanliness must be observed with hydraulic component at all time after any work on hydraulic system carefully inspect for leak underneath the vehicle while second operator apply maximum brake pressure to the brake engine running and operate the steering fuel system hose all fuel hose are made up of two lamination an armoured rubber outer sleeve and an inner viton core if any of the fuel system hose have been disconnected it is imperative that the internal bore is inspected to ensure that the viton lining has not become separated from the armoured outer sleeve new hose must be fitted if separation is evident cooling system hose the following precaution must be followed to ensure that integrity of cooling hose and their connection to system component are maintained hose orientation and connection correct orientation of cooling hose is important in ensuring that the hose doe not become fatigued or damaged through contact with adjacent component where timing mark are provided on the hose and corresponding connection these must be used to ensure correct orientation hose must be pushed fully onto their connection point usually moulded form on the stub pipe provide positive indicator hose clip marking are usually provided on the hose to indicate the correct clip position if no marking are provided position the clip directly behind the retaining lip at the end of the stub as shown worm drive clip should be oriented with the crimped side of the drive housing facing toward the end of the hose or the hose may become pinched between the clip and the stub pipe retaining lip worm drive clip should be tightened to nm unless otherwise stated ensure that hose clip do not foul adjacent component heat protection alway ensure that heatshield and protective sheathing are in good condition replace if damage is evident particular care must be taken when routing hose close to hot engine component such as the exhaust manifold and the exhaust gas recirculation egr pipe hose will relax and deflect general information when hot ensure this movement is taken into account when routing and securing hose fuel handling precaution general fuel vapour is highly flammable and in confined space is also explosive and toxic the vapour is heavier than air and will alway fall to the lowest level the vapour can be easily distributed throughout workshop by air current consequently even small spillage of fuel is potentially very dangerous the following information provide basic precaution which must be observed if fuel is to be handled safely it also outline other area of risk which must not be ignored this information is issued for basic guidance only if in doubt consult your local fire officer alway have fire extinguisher containing foam gas or powder close at hand when handling or draining fuel or when dismantling fuel system fire extinguisher should also be located in area where fuel container are stored alway disconnect the vehicle battery before carrying out dismantling or draining work on fuel system whenever fuel is being handled drained or stored or when fuel system are being dismantled all form of ignition must be extinguished or removed any lead lamp must be flameproof and kept clear of spillage warning no one should be permitted to repair component associated with fuel without first having specialist training warning do not remove fuel system component while the vehicle is over pit fuel tank draining fuel tank draining should be carried out in accordance with the procedure outlined in the fuel delivery section of this manual and observing the following precaution warning do not remove fuel system component while the vehicle is over pit extraction or draining of fuel must be carried out in well ventilated area the capacity of container must be more than adequate for the amount of fuel to be extracted or drained the container should be clearly marked with its content and placed in safe storage area which meet the requirement of local authority regulation fuel tank removal when the fuel line is secured to the fuel tank outlet by spring steel clip the clip must be released before the fuel line is disconnected or the fuel tank is removed this procedure will avoid the possibility of fume in the fuel tank being ignited when the clip is released general information as an added precaution fuel tank should have fuel vapour warning label attached to them as soon as they are removed from the vehicle fuel tank repair no attempt should be made to repair plastic fuel tank if the structure of the tank is damaged new tank must be fitted body repair plastic fuel pipe are particularly susceptible to heat even at relatively low temperature and can be melted by heat conducted from some distance away when body repair involve the use of heat all fuel pipe which run in the vicinity of the repair area must be removed and the tank outlet plugged warning if welding is to be carried out in the vicinity of thefueltank removed before welding commence electrical precaution general the following guideline are intended to ensure the safety of the operator while preventing damage to the electrical and electronic component fitted to the vehicle where necessary specific precaution are detailed in the individual procedure of this manual equipment prior to commencing any test procedure on the vehicle ensure that the relevant test equipment is working correctly and any harness or connector are in good condition it is particularly important to check the condition of the lead and plug of main operated equipment polarity never reverse connect the vehicle battery and alway ensure the correct polarity when connecting test equipment high voltage circuit whenever disconnecting live ht circuit alway use insulated plier and never allow the open end of the ht lead to contact other component particularly ecus exercise caution when measuring the voltage on the coil terminal while the engine is running high voltage spike can occur on these terminal connector and harness the engine compartment of vehicle is particularly hostile environment for electrical component and connector alway ensure electrically related item are dry and oil ensure disconnected multiplug and sensor are protected from being contaminated with oil coolant or other solution contamination could impair performance or result in catastrophic failure never force connector apart using tool to prise apart or by pulling on the wiring harness alway ensure locking tab are disengaged before disconnection and match orientation to enable correct reconnection ensure that any protection cover insulation etc is replaced if disturbed having confirmed component to be faulty switch off the ignition and disconnect the battery remove the component and support the disconnected harness when replacing the component keep oily hand away from electrical connection area and push connector home until any locking tab fully engage general information battery disconnection before disconnecting the battery disable the alarm system and switch off all electrical equipment caution to prevent damage to electrical component alway disconnect the battery when working on the vehicle electrical system the ground lead must be disconnected first and reconnected last caution alway ensure that battery lead are routed correctly and are not close to any potential chafing point battery charging alway ensure any battery charging area is well ventilated and discipline remove the handset prior to making any connection or disconnection in the system to prevent electrical surge caused by disconnecting live connection damaging electronic component ensure hand and work surface are clean and free of grease swarf etc grease collect dirt which can cause electrical tracking shortcircuit or high resistance contact when handling printed circuit board treat with care and hold by the edge only note that some electronic component are susceptible to body static connector should never be subjected to forced removal or refit especially inter board connector damaged contact can cause short circuit and open circuit fault condition prior to commencing test and periodically during test touch good vehicle body earth to discharge static charge some electronic component are vulnerable to the static electricity that may be generated by the operator electrical multiplug lubricate in order to prevent corrosion some multiplug under bonnet and carpet is wiped special lubricate in factory if these is destroyed in maintaining repair and replacing process special lubricate should be newly wiped supplementary restraint system precaution general precaution the srs system contain component which could be potentially hazardous to the service engineer if not serviced and handled correctly the following guideline are intended to alert the service engineer to potential source of danger and emphasise the importance of ensuring the integrity of srs component fitted to the vehicle warning it is imperative that before any work is undertaken on the srs system the appropriate information is read thoroughly warning it is imperative that before any work is undertaken on the srs system the appropriate information is read thoroughly warning the airbag module contain sodium azide which is poisonous and extremely flammable contact with water acid or heavy metal may produce harmful or explosive compound do not dismantle incinerate or bring into contact with electricity before the unit has been deployed warning alway replace seat belt assembly that has withstood the strain of severe vehicle impact or if the webbing show sign of fraying warning alway disconnect the vehicle battery before carrying out any electric welding on vehicle fitted with an srs system caution do not expose an airbag module or seat belt pre tensioner to heat exceeding it should be noted that these precaution are not restricted to operation performed when servicing the srs system the same care must be exercised when working on ancillary system and component located in the vicinity of the srs component these include but are not limited to steering system steering wheel airbag rotary coupler front fascia passenger front airbag interior trim ics head curtain airbag post trim post upper trim grab handle headlining above front door srs electronic control unit ecu beneath centre console side impact crash sensor under carpet beneath front seat seat side thorax airbag front seat belt pretensioner electrical system srs harness link lead and connector making the system safe before working on or in the vicinity of srs component ensure the system is rendered safe by performing the following procedure general information remove the handset from the docking station disconnect both battery lead earth lead first wait minute for srs ecu the srs ecu back up power circuit to discharge the srs system use energy reserve capacitor to keep the system active in the event of electrical supply failure under crash condition it is necessary to allow the capacitor sufficient time to discharge minute in order to avoid the risk of accidental deployment caution disconnect the battery before starting the srs operation disconnect the battery negative terminal first do not disconnect the battery in the reverse order installation in order to ensure system integrity it is essential that the srs system is regularly checked and maintained srs so that it is ready for effective operation in the event of collision carefully inspect srs component before installation srs do not install part that show sign of being dropped or improperly handled such as dent crack or deformation warning the integrity of the srs system is critical for safety reason ensure the following precaution are alway adhered to never install used srs component from another vehicle srs or attempt to repair an srs component never use the srs part without clear identification srs label never use an airbag or srs ecu that has been dropped part never apply electrical power to an srs component unless instructed to do srs so as part of an approved test procedure ensure the bolt are tightened to the correct torque alway use new fixing srs when replacing srs component ensure the ecu is alway installed correctly srs ecu srs ecu there must not be any gap between the ecu and the bracket to which it is mounted srs ecu an incorrectly mounted ecu could cause the system to malfunction do not supply power to the ecu are connected srs before all srs component srs ecu before all srs component caution ensure srs component are not contaminated with oil grease detergent or water caution torque wrench should be regularly checked for accuracy to ensure that all fixing are tightened to the correct torque caution following seat belt pretensioner deployment the seat belt can still be used as conventional seat belt but will need to be replaced as soon as possible to re establish full srs protection caution if the srs component is to be replaced the bar code of the new unit must be recorded srs component testing precaution the srs component are triggered using relatively low operating current alway adhere to the following precaution warning do not use multimeter or other general purpose test equipment on srs system component or accidental deployment may occur only use the recommended diagnostic equipment to diagnose system fault warning do not use electrical test equipment on the srs harness while it is connected to any of the srs system component it may cause accidental deployment and personal injury general information handling and storage alway comply with the following handling precaution warning the srs component are sensitive and potentially hazardous if not handled correctly alway comply with the following handling precaution ecu and airbag must be stored in cabinet in dry room at normal room temperature not exceeding ensure that heating fire water and other chemical corrosive substance cannot contaminate the stored srs component for safety reason do not store inflammable good close to the area where the srs component are stored keep new airbag module in the original packaging until just before fitting the storage area must comply with all legal requirement it must have suitable fire extinguisher or other fire extinguishing equipment never drop an srs component the airbag diagnostic control unit is particularly shock sensitive device and must be handled with extreme care never wrap your arm around an airbag module if an airbag module has to be carried hold it by the cover with the cover uppermost and the base away from your body never transport airbag module or seat belt pre tensioner in the cabin of vehicle warning never attach anything to an airbag cover or any trim componentcovering an airbag module do not allow anything to rest on top of an airbag module caution do not apply grease or cleaning solvent to seat belt pretensioner unit component failure could result warning store the airbag module with the deployment side uppermost if it is stored deployment side down accidental deployment will propel the airbag module with enough force to cause serious injury warning airbag module and seat belt pre tensioner are classed as explosive device for overnight and longer term storage they must be stored in secure steel cabinet which has been approved as suitable for the purpose and has been registered by the local authority warning when recovering the seat belt pretensioner do not aim the piston pipe at yourself or other keep the piston facing the ground hold the case instead of the seat belt warning store the airbag module or seat belt pre tensioner in designated storage area caution improper handling or storage can internally damage the airbag module making it inoperative if you suspect the airbag module has been damaged install new module and refer to thedeployment disposal procedure for disposal of the damaged module srs harness and connector alway observe the following precaution srs with regard to srs system electrical wiring never attempt to modify splice or repair srs wiring general information never install electronic equipment such as mobile telephone two way radio or in car entertainment system in such way that it could generate electrical interference in the airbag harness seek specialist advice when installing such equipment note srs system wiring can be identified by special yellow outer sleeve protecting the wire black with yellow stripe protective covering are sometime used warning alway ensure srs wiring is routed correctly be careful to avoidtrapping or pinching the srs wiring do not leave the connector hanging loose or allow srs component to hang from their harness look for possible point of chafing precaution for vehicle owner for the airbag to work effectively and protect vehicle owner follow the precaution listed below driver and passenger must use seat belt correctly correctly using the seat belt can protect the body and reduce injury in the event of an accident of the seat belt pretensioner or airbag do not place any object on the steering wheel or instrument panel that could penetrate an inflating airbag or be thrown item likely to cause injury never fit cover on seat which fitting side airbag child under year old should not sit in the front seat only genuine accessory part are allowed to be installed only authorised people can remove airbag module ecu srs system wiring harness and connector srs ecu and srs system wiring harness and connector if the airbag and seat belt pre tensioner are deployed during an accident srs ecu must be replaced and discarded every srs system on every car srs has been paired and identified srs illegally adding or modifying the srs system and the wiring harness could injure people modifying the vehicle structure or srs system is strictly not allowed srs and may cause wrong airbag deployment or failure to deploy when required rotary coupler precaution caution alway follow the procedure for fitting and checking the rotary coupler as instructed in the srs repair section comply with all safety and installation procedure to ensure the system function correctly observe the following precaution do not unlock and rotate the rotary coupler when it is removed from the vehicle do not turn the road wheel when the rotary coupler is removed from the vehicle alway ensure the rotary coupler is removed and installed in its centred position and with the front road wheel in the straight ahead position procedure srs refer to the srs repair section for the correct removal and installation if new rotary coupler is being installed ensure the locking tab holding the coupler rotational position is not broken unit with broken locking tab should not be used warning label warning symbol are displayed at various position in the vehicle srs component have additional warning label displayed on them to indicate that particular care is needed when handling them these include airbag module srs ecu seat belt pre tensioner and the rotary coupler the following warning symbol may be displayed at various location on the vehicle general information the need for caution when working in close proximity to srs component srs in close proximity to srs component refer to the publication where the procedure srs instruction and advice can be found usually workshop manual or owner handbook for working on the srs system do not use rear facing child seat in the front passenger seat if the vehicle is fitted with passenger airbag warning it is imperative that before any work is undertaken on the srs system the appropriate information is read thoroughly the following list indicate possible location and content for srs component not deployed normal towing procedure are unlikely to cause an airbag to deploy however as precaution remove the handset from the docking station and then disconnect both battery lead disconnect the negative lead first towing srs component not deployed once the driver airbag has been deployed the vehicle must have suspended tow however as precaution remove the handset from the docking station and then disconnect both battery lead disconnect the negative lead first srs component deployed if vehicle is to be scrapped and contain an undeployed airbag module the module must be manually deployed alway observe the following precaution warning only personnel who have undergone the appropriate training should undertake deployment of airbag and seat belt pre tensioner module warning deployed airbag or seat belt pre tensioner is very hot do not return to deployed airbag module until at least minute have elapsed since deployment general information warning only use approved deployment equipment and only deploy srs component in well ventilated designated area ensure srs component are not damaged or ruptured before deployment warning contact with chemical from deployed and damaged srs component could present health hazard wear protective clothing when handling do not eat drink or smoke when handling srs component warning deployment of airbag module and seat belt pre tensioner can cause injury to personnel in the close vicinity of the deploying unit in case of injury seek urgent medical advice possible source of injury include impact due to inflating airbag or pretensioner operation causing component kick hearing due to noise produced by deploying airbag and seat belt pretensioner unit burn hot component part and gase irritation to eye and lung from deploying gase or combustion residue warning ensure the srs component to be deployed is securely fastened to its mounting warning compliance with the following precaution must be ensured only use deployment equipment approved for the intended purpose before commencing deployment procedure ensure the deployment tool function properly deployment of airbag pre tensioner module should be performed in well ventilated area which has been designated for the purpose ensure airbag pre tensioner module are not damaged or ruptured before attempting to deploy notify the relevant authority of intention to deploy airbag and pretensioner unit when deploying airbag and seat belt pre tensioner unit ensure that all personnel are at least metre away from the deployment zone when deploying seat belt pretensioner in the vehicle ensure the pre tensioner unit is fully secured to its fixing point when removing deployed airbag and seat belt pre tensioner module wear protective clothing use glove and seal deployed unit in plastic bag following deployment of any component of the srs system within the vehicle all srs component must be replaced do not re use or salvage any part of the srs system do not lean over airbag module or seat belt pre tensioner unit when connecting deployment equipment general information srs component replacement policy impact which do not deploy the airbag or pre tensioner check for structural damage in the area of the impact paying particular attention to bumper armature longitudinal crash can and bracketry impact which deploy the airbag or pre tensioner the within airbag and pre tensioner will deteriorate over period of time as result it is essential that the airbag and pre tensioner are periodically replaced to maintain occupant safety airbag seat belt pre tensioner and the rotary coupler should be replaced at year interval air conditioning system precaution general the air conditioning system contain fluid and component which could be potentially hazardous to the service engineer or the environment if not serviced and handled correctly the following guideline are intended to alert the service engineer to potential source of danger and emphasise the importance of ensuring the integrity of the air conditioning operating condition and component fitted to the vehicle where necessary additional specific precaution are detailed in the relevant section of this manual which should be referred to prior to commencing repair operation hydrofluorocarbon warning servicing must only be carried out by personnel familiar with both the vehicle system and the charging and testing equipment all operation must be carried out in well ventilated area away from open flame and heat source warning is hazardous liquid and when handled incorrectly can cause serious injury suitable protective clothing consisting of face protection heat proof glove rubber boot and rubber apron or waterproof overall must be worn when carrying out operation on the air conditioning system remedial action if an accident involving should occur conduct the following remedial action if liquid enter the eye do not rub it gently run large quantity of eye wash over affected eye to raise the temperature if an eye wash is not available cool clean water may be used to flush the eye after rinsing cover the eye with clean pad and seek immediate medical attention if liquid is splashed onto the skin run large quantity of water over the affected area to raise the temperature implement the same action if the skin come in contact with discharging cylinder wrap the contaminated body part in blanket or similar material and seek immediate medical attention if the debilitating effect of inhalation of vapour is suspected seek fresh air if the affected person is unconscious move them away from the contaminated area to fresh air and apply artificial respiration and or oxygen and seek immediate medical attention general information warning due to its low evaporating temperature must be handled with care splashed on any part of the body will cause immediate freezing of that area also refrigerant cylinder and replenishment trolley when discharging will freeze skin to them if contact is made service precaution observe the following precaution when handling component used in the air conditioning system air conditioning unit must not be lifted by their hose pipe or capillary line hose and line must not be subjected to any twist or stress the efficiency of the system will be impaired by kink or restriction ensure that hose are correctly positioned before tightening coupling and ensure that all clip and support are utilised flexible hose should not be positioned close to the exhaust manifold less than mm unless protected by heat shielding completed assembly must be checked for refrigeration line touching metal panel any direct contact of component and panel may transmit noise and so must be eliminated the appropriate torque wrench must be used when tightening refrigerant connection to the stipulated value an additional spanner must be used to hold the union to prevent twisting of the pipe when tightening connection before connecting any hose or pipe ensure that refrigerant oil is applied to the seat of the new ring but not to the thread of the connection all protective plug must remain in place to seal the component until immediately prior to connection ensure component are at room temperature before uncapping to prevent condensation of moisture from the air that enter it component must not remain uncapped for longer than minute in the event of delay the cap must be fitted when disconnecting immediately cap all air conditioning pipe to prevent ingress of dirt and moisture into the system the receiver drier contain desiccant which absorb moisture it must be positively sealed at all time receiver drier that has been left uncapped must not be used fit new unit the receiver drier should be the last component connected to the system to ensure optimum dehydration and maximum moisture protection of the system whenever the refrigerant system is opened the receiver drier must be renewed immediately before evacuating and recharging the system use alcohol and clean lint free cloth to clean dirty connection ensure that all new part fitted are marked for use with refrigerant oil refrigerant oil easily absorb water and must not be stored for long period do not pour unused refrigerant oil back into the container alway use an approved refrigerant oil when replacing component in the system drain the refrigerant oil from the component being replaced into graduated container on assembly add the quantity of refrigerant oil drained to the new component compressor when fitting new compressor slowly release the sealing cap gas pressure should be heard to vent as the seal is broken caution do not remove the cap until immediately prior to connecting the air conditioning pipe to the compressor rapid refrigerant discharge the system is punctured the refrigerant will discharge rapidly the rapid discharge of refrigerant will also result in the loss of most of the oil from the system the compressor must be removed and all the remaining oil in the compressor drained and refilled as instructed in the air conditioning section of this manual precaution for refrigerant recovery recycling and recharging when the air conditioning system is recharged any existing refrigerant is first recovered from the system and recycled the system is then charged with the required weight of refrigerant and volume of refrigerant oil warning refrigerant must alway be recycled before re use to ensure that the purity of the refrigerant is high enough for safe use in the air conditioning system warning recycling should alway be carried out with equipment which is design certified by underwriter laboratory inc for compliance with sae other equipment may not recycle refrigerant to the required level of purity warning refrigerant recovery recycling recharging station must not be used with any other type of refrigerant general information warning refrigerant from domestic and commercial source must not be used in motor vehicle air conditioning system caution the system must be evacuated immediately beforerechargingcommence delaybetweenevacuation and recharging is not permitted air conditioning compressor replacement replacement instruction new compressor is supplied filled with full charge of refrigerant oil the new compressor must be drained and calculated quantity of oil added before fitting to calculate the quantity of oil to be added carry out the following procedure remove the filler drain plug from the old compressor invert the compressor and gravity drain the oil into calibrated measuring cylinder rotate the compressor clutch to ensure the compressor is completely drained record the quantity of oil drained discard the oil remove the filler drain plug from the new compressor invert the compressor and gravity drain the oil into calibrated measuring cylinder rotate the compressor clutch to ensure the compressor is completely drained add the same amount of oil drained from the old compressor to the new compressor discard the remaining oil drained from the new compressor fit and tighten the compressor filler drain plug general number attached on the top surface of the front part of the transmission housing general information lifting towing lifting towing lifting lifting description note the following point before lifting the vehicle from the ground the vehicle should be parked on level and firm ground apply the hand brake the transmission is in or to avoid damage to the lower body component of the vehicle be sure to follow the support procedure below do not place the jack or the axle support under the following component body structural part bumper fuel line brake line front suspension arm steering linkage rear suspension arm fuel tank engine oil pan transmission case note jacking and supporting the front of the vehicle should only be done under the customer front jacking point and or the jacking bracket on the front section of the front subframe lifting and supporting vehicle front and rear supporting point of vehicle place the head of the jack on the supporting point of the front and rear sill on the vehicle caution do not jack or support the vehicle on any other point than those indicated failure to comply is likely to result in damage to the body or chassis component warning alway chock the wheel when jacking warning do not work on or under vehicle supported only by jack alway support the vehicle on safety stand warning do not commence work on the underside of the vehicle until suitable safety support have been positioned under the sill reinforced bracket vehicle jack the jack of the vehicle is only applied to replace wheel in emergency never use it for other purpose if using the jack to replace wheel be sure to place the jack on solid level ground and position it under the supporting point near the wheel to be removed note that the snap head of the jack must be placed in the appropriate groove of the deck hydraulic jack the hydraulic jack must have load capacity of at least kg lbs column lifter lifting towing general information put the mat under the jacking point on the sill roadside assistance instruction for roadside assistance it is recommended that rescue trailer or two wheel rescue vehicle shall be used in emergency use towing vehicle with front towing point front towing eye in the front of the vehicle removable towing eye can be used for rescue operation the front towing eye is usually kept in the vehicle toolbox and should be put back after using when fitting the towing eye remove the towing eye cover at the right side of the front bumper first then screw the towing eye into the mounting position of the front bumper assembly bumper beam manual transmission for four wheel towing operate from the front via the towing eye before towing release the hand brake put the shift lever at the neutral gear and the ignition switch at on position the towing speed shall not exceed the following precaution must be taken in order to ensure that the vehicle still has steering capability when it is towed it is necessary to insert the ignition key and general information lifting towing put it to on position this state must be maintained when the vehicle is moving when the transmission or drive shaft fail the vehicle could not be towed ensure that the shift lever is at the neutral gear and parking brake is disabled if the engine is not in operation during towing more care must be taken in brake and steering operation caution if the transmission case occur malfunction and the transmission fluid level is too low the four wheel of the vehicle cannot be towed absolutely automatic transmission generally vehicle with automatic transmission should be towed with the front wheel off the road with front wheel hanged up caution rear suspended tow must not be used for vehicle with an automatic transmission as serious damage will be caused to the transmission in order to ensure that the vehicle still has steering capability when it is towed it is necessary to insert the ignition key and put it to on position this state must be maintained when the vehicle is moving for vehicle with automatic transmission when towing the four wheel from the front attention must be paid to the following warning when the vehicle is being towed the selector must be at km or for distance exceeding km do not tow if the gearbox or drive shaft is faulty ensure the gear lever is in neutral and the parking brake is released remember that greater effort than normal will be necessary to operate the brake and steering if the vehicle is being towed without the engine running caution do not tow on four wheel if the gearbox or drive shaft are touching the body or frame note vehicle with an automatic transmission cannot be started by towing or pushing vehicle shipping if your vehicle need to be shipped we recommend you to use special transporter to ship fix the vehicle on the transporter as follow apply the parking brake and place the transmission shift lever in or position position the brake pad on the front and rear wheel fit the tether around the tyre and secure it to the trailer tighten the tether to fix the vehicle firmly lubrication maintenance general information lubrication maintenance capacity liquid capacity the following capacity value are merely approximate value for the corresponding system component system capacity fuel tank engine oil refill from dry state replacement of the engine oil and oil filter manual transmission oil refill refill from dry state auto transmission oil refill cooling system coolant expansion tank cooling system steering system brake fluid for brake system refill from dry state windshield washer reservoir fluid fuel following recommended oil must be applied for gasoline engine and above gasoline ron is recommended do not use gasoline containing methanol antifreeze fluid the mixture with water and oat shall be used the antifreeze containing organic acid technology oat preservative and summer coolant or ethylene glycol based antifreeze methanol not contained are applied to protect the cooling system only the antifreeze with oat antiseptic can be used do not use other type of antifreeze to supplement or refill the cooling system in emergency if the antifreeze of this type is unavailable the clean water can be used to fill the cooling system however this may lower the frost protection capability the correct antifreeze concentration must be recovered as soon as possible drain the cooling system and clean it then refill the cooling system with appropriate coolant after refilling antifreeze attach the warning label at prominent position of the vehicle to show the type of sothatcorrectantifreeze can be used for next refill concentration of antifreeze fluid the overall antifreeze concentration shall not be less than of the volume meanwhile make sure the antisepsis property of the coolant antifreeze concentration more than is not recommended as it will weaken the cooling efficiency the antifreeze capacity recommended in the following table shall provide frost protection at engine concentration capacity of antifreeze fluid brake fluid use brake fluid dot only pas fluid use the liquid that conform to the dexron specification general information lubrication maintenance lubricating fluid overview the engine and other lubrication system are filled with high performance lubricating fluid to extend the service life caution alway use high quality oil of the correct viscosity range in the engine the use of oil of the incorrect specification can lead to high oil and fuel consumption and ultimately to damaged component the use of the engine oil of recommended specification containing additive may decrease the corrosive acid caused by combustion and prevent formation of residue these residue may block the oil passage other engine oil additive should not be used repair and maintenance must be carried out within the specified interval engine oil engine use engine oil in compliance with acea specification engine oil in compliance with acea specification and engine oil in compliance with specification the engine oil viscosity temperature range refer to the following table transmission fluid failure to use specified transmission fluid may camshaft position sensor engine engine mechanical water pump and thermostat water pump thermostat water pipe engine mechanical engine fuel rail assembly fuel rail assembly fuel injector bolt fuel rail ring fuel injector clip fuel injector engine engine mechanical description overview the nse engine feature in line cylinder valve dohc timing chain drive and electronically controlled multi point fuel injection system it is mainly composed of cylinder block cylinder head crankshaft connecting rod and camshaft the cylinder head is made of cast aluminum and connected with the cast iron cylinder block through the multi layer cylinder gasket by using high strength bolt and there are additional high strength bolt directly connected with the cast iron cylinder block providing high overall strength and reliable structure cylinder head component the cylinder head adopt valve per cylinder structure with spark plug mounted at the centre of the combustion chamber due to adoption of overhead valve structure powerful whirlpool will be formed in the combustion chamber to make the mixed gas evenly distributed which is good for transmission of flame to improve combustion condition so as to enhance the fuel economy power performance and emission performance of the engine the mechanical tappet are fitted at the top of each valve and directly driven by the camshaft the bottom boss of the mechanical tappet are grouped by size and the valve clearance is optionally fitted with appropriate grouped mechanical tappet the valve stem oil seal is press fitted on the cylinder head oil seal seat and can be used as the valve spring seat to support the valve spring the exhaust valve is of carbon deposit removal type and the valve stem with machined side can clean the carbon buildup at the end of valve duct and in combustion chamber so as to prevent valve from seizing the metal cylinder gasket is of multi layer structure the cylinder opening adopt pressing ring structure which enhance sealing at cylinder opening local rubber coat technology is adopted to ensure the sealing performance there are four oil duct at the front bearing cap of the camshaft one oil inlet duct one oil return duct and two operating oil duct oil enter the oil control solenoid valve via the oil duct the tail end of the intake exhaust camshaft is equipped with camshaft signal wheel to provide signal for the camshaft position sensor there is timing pin hole on the camshaft for controlling camshaft timing phase during assembly phase modulator is fitted at the drive end of the intake and exhaust camshaft which is fixed by bolt main part of the phase modulator are made of aluminum the housing stator driven by timing chain is connected with inner rotor through five blade fixed at camshaft end in hydraulic way when the engine is at rest pin of an elastic load lock both halve of the modulator when the engine operate they are loosened in hydraulic way to ensure stable starting two oil control valve are fitted on the front camshaft bearing cap and fixed with an ring and bolt respectively the control valve is four way proportioning valve and consist of an oil inlet port an oil return port and two oil duct connected with the phase modulator working chamber cylinder block component the cylinder block is constructed from cast iron material this provide high overall strength reducing the likelihood of deformation the cylinder liner is formed by cylinder hole honing shaped water passage are processed between two adjacent cylinder to ensure heat dissipation effect of the cylinder block the aluminum alloy thermal expansion piston is provided with full floating piston pin which can rotate freely to reduce the friction resistance and the generation and delivery of heat so that the reliability and smoothness of high speed of engine rotation can be enhanced the forging steel connecting rod has the structure of the section the big end of connecting rod move on the bearing shell of crankshaft journal the connecting rod adopt forging cracking process the crankshaft has four balance weight and is supported by five bearing shell the end float is controlled by the thrust washer on the top of the central main bearing shell the radial clearance of the bearing shell is optional which is controlled by the bearing shell of two different grade the oil groove offered in the upper half part of the main bearing shell supply oil to the connecting rod big end bearing shell through the oil hole in the crankshaft lower crankcase component the lower crankcase assembly consist of lower crankcase oil pump assembly oil strainer and oil filter the aluminum lower crankcase is designed into shape that is suitable for accumulating oil around the strainer the oil pump and oil strainer are fitted to bottom of crankcase after being combined and oil pass through the oil strainer with screen and then the oil filter by pressurization of the oil pump clean oil is distributed to the main bearing and cylinder head via the main oil passage of the engine block to lubricate the crankshaft connecting rod and camshaft etc the mating surface of the lower crankcase and engine block is sealed with sealant and secured with bolt and the oil drain plug is located inthedirectionofengine front end surface engine mechanical engine engine accessory the accessory are single ribbed belt drive system driven by crankshaft pulley the system drive the water pump alternator and compressor and tension the belt via damping tensioner crankcase ventilation the ventilation system redirect the exhaust gas escaped to the crankcase back into the intake system with the help of intake system vacuum level then send it to the cylinder for combustion so as to reduce the air pollution oil and gas in the crankcase are separated via an oil separating device in the camshaft cover and oil flow back to the crankcase engine engine mechanical operation lubricating oil circuit cylinder head oil duct cylinder block main oil duct crankshaft cross oil duct engine oil inlet pipe engine oil filter engine oil pump main oil duct timing chain tensioner oil control valve the lubrication system is full flow filtration forced oil supply system oil is drawn from the oil pan through the oil strainer by the oil pump and the oil strainer is equipped with screen which can prevent impurity from entering the oil pump the oil pump with pressure limiting valve is driven by the crankshaft via belt when the oil pressure reach certain value the pressure relief valve will open the excess oil return to the inlet side of oil pump and this help maintain the oil pressure within certain operating pressure range and ensure the normal operation of engine oil flowing from the oil pump outlet enter into the full flow oil filter the full flow oil filter is directly fitted on the oil pan the oil filter is equipped with by pass valve when the filter element is blocked this valve will open and ensure the free flow of oil but the oil filter shall be replaced as soon as possible then andlubricatesthe main bearing shell directly via the cross oil circuit the cross oil circuit on the crankshaft deliver the oil from the main bearing shell to the connecting rod big end bearing shell the cylinder head has two side oil passage connecting with the main oil passage of the engine block after entering the side oil passage the oil flow into horizontal oil passage of the standard length and is supplied to each mechanical tappet and camshaft journal engine mechanical engine the oil splashed by the crankshaft lubricate the cylinder wall the oil collected by the lower side of the piston crown lubricate the connecting rod small end and oil is supplied to the piston pin from the connecting rod small end through the oil circuit engine engine mechanical service procedure powertrain remove and refit at remove disconnect the battery negative drain the coolant coolant drain drain the automatic cylinder pressure test test run the engine until it reach normal operating temperature the battery must be fully charged or almost fully charged stop the engine disable the fuel system pull out the harness connector of the fuel pump start the engine again until it stop so as to consume the fuel remaining in the fuel rail disable the ignition system disengage the ignition coil for each cylinder and engine harness connector remove the ignition coil for each cylinder remove the spark plug for all cylinder measure the compression pressure of the engine with the following procedure fit the adapter of the spark plug hole into the spark plug hole fit the compression pressure gauge into the adapter of the spark plug hole fully depress the accelerator pedal with assistance turn the key to start the starter until no increased pressure is indicated by the compression pressure gauge note do not keep the starter motor running for at time and the interval for next start shall not be less than check and record the reading on the pressure gauge remove the compression pressure gauge from the adapter of the spark plug hole remove the adapter of the spark plug hole from the spark plug hole repeat step to test the pressure of other cylinder the lower limit value for the engine cylinder under normal pressure temperature is bar difference between each cylinder pressure and average pressure for each cylinder for gasoline engine it shall not be greater than here are some example of measurement when the compression pressure is normal the compression pressure of each cylinder will increase quickly and evenly to the specified value if the low compression pressure is caused by piston ring the compression pressure will drop in the stroke and gradually increase in the subsequent stroke however it will never reach the normal value after oil refilling the compression pressure will increase substantially refill engine oil about through the spark plug hole and retest the cylinder pressure if the low compression pressure is caused by valve low compression pressure will be measured in the stroke and it will not increase even when the oil is refilled the same consequence can be resulted from leaking cylinder head gasket and worn valve but for the crankcase the problem may be due to engine coolant in addition leaking cylinder head gasket will result in low compression pressure reading of the cylinder in pair resume the ignition system resume the fuel system engine engine mechanical special tool tool number description picture engine lift bracket engine support
