clamp radiator to engine pipeline clamp engine expansion tank overflow pipe clip engine expansion tank overflow pipe clamp heater to engine pipeline clamp cooling system pipeline pipeline assembly heater to engine clamp radiator expansion tank overflow pipe pipeline assembly engine to heater clamp heater to engine pipeline clamp heater to engine pipeline clip engine expansion tank overflow pipe engine overflow pipe engine engine cooling system description overview the cooling system circulate the engine coolant in the loop of engine and heater core the coolant is the mixture of water and organic acid technique oat which help to maintain the optimum operating temperature of the engine when the environment and engine working condition are different caution engine coolant will damage paint finished surface if spilled immediately rinse area with plenty of water the coolant shall be replaced regularly to keep its optimum efficiency and corrosion resistance performance component of cooling system coolant pump the coolant circulate through the rotor type pump on the rear upper part of the engine crankcase drive which is sealed to the cylinder block by an ring and driven by the crankshaft timing belt coolant hose the rubber coolant hose deliver coolant among all component the coolant hose is fixed to each component by spring clamp the heater core complete the fluid inlet by connecting the outlet elbow of the engine to the inlet pipe of the inlet hard pipe of heater core passing through the dash panel then goe to oil cooler water inlet pipe through the water outlet pipe of heater core and flow back to the engine cylinder block through the oil cooler radiator pressure seal cap radiator pressure cap is mounted on the top of the radiator which is equipped with relief valve and vacuum valve when the system pressure is within bar relief valve open to release system pressure when the system pressure is within bar vacuum valve open and fluid is replenished to the system from outside so that the cooling system can run within certain pressure range expansion tank the coolant expansion tank is fitted on the left side of the cooling module cooling fan one overflow return pipe is connected to the pressure cover at the top of the radiator through the bottom part of the expansion tank when the coolant is expanded during warm up additional coolant in the cooling system will flow back to the expansion tank through the overflow return pipe when the coolant in the cooling system is insufficient coolant in the expansion tank will flow back to the cooling system through the overflow return pipe thermostat this thermostat is wax type component with pressure relief characteristic it is located in the sealing housing on engine pipe when starting cold engine the coolant temperature is low the wax type component of the thermostat is in solid state and the thermostat valve switch off under the action of the spring at this point the cooling system is in small circulation status namely the coolant cannot enter into the radiator for circulation but it can circulate between the bypass circuit and heater core when the coolant temperature rise the wax type component start melting into liquid state gradually followed by the increase of volume then the thermostat valve will open gradually under the action of the push rod at this point the cooling system is in big circulation status the coolant will flow into the cylinder block through the bottom hose of the radiator allow the hot coolant to flow through the radiator from the top hose to balance heat flow and keep the temperature at the optimal for engine operation when the thermostat is fully open the coolant flow through the radiator radiator and cooling fan the radiator is of aluminum longitudinal flow type with injection moulding water tank on both end for automatic transmission model automatic transmission oil cooler is locate in the water chamber below the radiator the cooling hose linked with transmission is connected to the bushing of the transmission oil cooler with nut the radiator support the cooling fan assembly air conditioning condenser the top of the radiator is located in the rubber bushing supported by the upper radiator beam bracket the bottom of the radiator is located in the rubber bushing supported by the bracket fixed on the front longitudinal beam sponge sealing strip fitted on both side of the radiator are used to guide the airflow through the radiator the condenser fixed by bolt is fitted on the front end of the radiator the cooling fan and motor assembly is fitted on the fan shroud at the rear of the radiator the suction type fan draw out air through the radiator beside the fan shroud is equipped with additional vent hole allowing more air flow through radiator when driving at high speed together with cooling fan low speed resistance of the cooling fan is fitted at the left side of the radiator engine coolant temperature ect sensor ectthe engine coolant temperature sensor is fitted on the engine cylinder block with packaged negative temperature coefficient ntc thermistor contacting with the engine coolant which is part of the bleeder circuit the circuit is composed of rated power supply an engine control engine cooling system engine module ecm internal resistance and variable resistor related to temperature ect sensor engine engine cooling system operation coolant flow dvct engine the cooling system utiliz the heat conduction principle conducting heat from the engine component to the coolant when the engine is at low temperature the coolant pump will circulate the coolant among the engine block cylinder head and passenger compartment heater core the thermostat will be kept closed to prevent the coolant from flowing through the radiator however small amount of coolant may pass through the bypass this help to prevent the accumulated pressure from getting too high when the engine reach the normal operating temperature the thermostat open the cool coolant enter into the cylinder block through the hose at the bottom of the radiator and the hot coolant will flow into the radiator through the hose at the top of the radiator so that the cool and hot coolant may be balanced to maintain the engine at the best temperature when the thermostat is fully open all coolant flow through the radiator the excess coolant produced by thermal expansion return to the expansion tank through the overflow pipe at the top of the radiator meanwhile the expansion tank will remove the air in the coolant the radiator pressure seal cap with relief valve and vacuum valve isolate the cooling system from the ambient atmosphere the coolant expand as the temperature rise and the pressure of the cooling system will increase accordingly the increased pressure raise the boiling point of the coolant allowing the engine to run at higher more efficient operating temperature without the risk of coolant boiling the pressurization of the cooling system is limited so pressure relief valve is provided on the expansion tank cap by this way when the max operating pressure is reached the excess pressure in the cooling system can be released cooling fan control policy the cooling fan will operate at high or low speed and the control is achieved by the ecm and relay unit ecm the ecm utiliz the information provided by the ect sensor bcm and pressure switch to control the fan speed and engine coolant temperature engine cooling system engine during the normal operation the cooling fan will operate at the following coolant temperature cooling fan status low speed high speed on off when the system is working the cooling fan will operate under the following pressure condition cooling fan status high speed on mpa off mpa the cooling fan control policy include the engine idle speed adjustment fan is on after the ignition key is removed cooling fan status low speed high speed on off the cooling fan will operate for to provide additional cooling for the engine at high temperature in case of ect failure the fan will operate at low speed cooling fan relay unit the cooling fan relay unit is connected to the ecm by wire and one wire provide the ground for the internal relay winding the cooling fan relay is powered by power supply through fuse of the engine compartment fuse box when the cooling fan run at low speed the low speed relay provide the power supply when the cooling fan run at high speed the high speed relay provide the power supply the cooling fan relay may have fault under the following condition high speed relay coil is open high speed relay coil is short circuited high speed relay contact has high resistance high speed relay contact is open low speed relay coil is open low speed relay coil is short circuited low speed relay contact has high resistance external wire is open external wire is short to the power supply external wire is short to the ground external wire has high resistance warning keep clear of the cooling fan the fan may start automatically without warning even after the engine is turned off engine engine cooling system service procedure coolant drain and refill drain remove the coolant expansion tank cap warning since injury such as scalding could be caused by escaping steam or coolant do not remove the filler cap from the coolant expansion tank while the system is hot secure proper container to collect the coolant remove the bottom deflector bottom deflector remove open the drain valve at the bottom of the engine cooling system radiator drain the engine cooling system refill close the coolant drain valve at the bottom of the radiator prepare the coolant of specified concentration fluid open the expansion tank cap fill coolant to the expansion tank until the coolant level reach the neck of the expansion tank and keep still when filling coolant to the vehicle with dvct engine unscrew the bleeder bolt on the radiator when founding that the coolant is about to spill screw up the bolt ensure that the is turned off start the engine and allow it to operate at the speed of rpm fill the expansion tank as required to keep the level just below the neck of the expansion tank when the coolant level reach the neck of the expansion tank due to thermal expansion fit the expansion tank cap continuously run the engine at the speed of rpm until the cooling fan operate and then run the engine at idle speed until the cooling fan stop turn off the engine and cool it down for minute check the cooling system for leak check whether the coolant level is between min and max if not open the expansion tank cap and top up the tank with coolant or drain it to be between min and max as required engine cooling system engine system pressure test check visually check the expansion tank cap for sign of leakage check the hose for sign of rupture and distortion and the attachment strength of pipeline connection open the coolant expansion tank cap warning since injury such as scalding could be caused by escaping steam or coolant do not remove the filler cap from the coolant expansion tank while the system is hot fit the to the expansion tank cap connect the pressure hose to the pressurize the expansion tank cap slowly to the specified pressure then check the pressure if the pressure range is maintained within the expansion tank cap is normal release pressure through the relief valve on the pressurizer remove the pressure hose from the remove the expansion tank cap from the and fit it to the expansion tank engine engine cooling system pressure seal cap pressure test check check the hose for sign of rupture and distortion and the attachment strength of pipeline connection open the coolant expansion tank cap warning since injury such as scalding could be caused by escaping steam or coolant do not remove the filler cap from the coolant expansion tank while the system is hot fit the to the expansion tank connect the pressure hose to the pressurize the system to the pressure of slowly and check it for leakage warning do not exceed the setting pressure value or the cooling system will be damaged visually check the engine and cooling system for sign of coolant leakage release pressure through the relief valve on the disconnect the hose from the remove the from the expansion tank and fit the expansion tank cap control diagram hard wire can bus engine fuel management engine can bus engine engine fuel management description overview the engine management system ecm is applied to control each aspect of the engine operation the system receive input from various sensor and then utiliz them to determine output including the fuel volume delivered to the combustion chamber ignition time air supply control etc engine use bosch me ecm which is continuous multi point fuel injection system controlled by ecm and adopt the speed density principle combined with electronic throttle control the control intention of the engine management system are to enhance power performance reduce fuel consumption and exhaust pollution engine control module ecm the engine control module is the control centre of the fuel injection system it monitor continuously the information from various sensor control various system influencing the vehicle performance it also execute system diagnosis function it is able to identify the operation fault remind the driver with malfunction indicator lamp mil and store the dtc at indication fault position to facilitate the repair of the service personnel ecmend view and pin information of connector pin no description hs can bus high main relay clutch top switch accelerator pedal ground brake vacuum sensor non continuous power supply non continuous power supply canbus low brake vacuum sensor continuity power supply brake switch brake lamp medium pressure switch high low pressure switch accelerator pedal sensor fan speed control anti theft input ignition switch accelerator pedal power supply accelerator pedal power supply fuel pump relay compressor relay accelerator pedal sensor sensor ground engine fuel management engine electronic vacuum pump relay at only fan speed control accelerator pedal ground ecm ground ecm ground ecm end view and pin information of connector pin no description fuel injector cylinder fuel injector cylinder variable camshaft timing exhaust variable camshaft timing intake fuel injector cylinder upstream oxygen sensor heater fuel injector cylinder throttle actuator ignition coil cylinder throttle position sensor signal throttle position sensor signal oxygen sensor upstream oxygen sensor ground speed exhaust phase sensor ground coolant oil temperature sensor ground throttle ground throttle actuator ignition coil cylinder knock sensor knock sensor main load sensor phase position sensor intake canister valve oxygen intake phase position sensor ground engine speed sensor input phase position power supply ignition coil cylinder ignition coil cylinder enginecoolanttemperature sensor signal intake air temperature sensor oil temperature sensor signal upstream oxygen sensor signal phase position sensor exhaust alternator feedback signal for non stop start throttle power supply speed exhaust phase position power supply engine engine fuel management intake air pressure temperature power supply oxygen sensor heater ecm ground ecm ground the engine management system is composed of the following component according to control principle external control engine air supply control system engine ignition control system engine fuel supply control system auxiliary heat management auxiliary external assistance external control the external control of the engine mainly include main relay ignition switch battery cancommunication immobiliser power supply the external control mainly refer to the power supply and communication one line of power supply of the engine control module ecm is directly connected to the power supply through fuse to ensure ecm is in powered state when the ignition switch is in on or ign position the engine control module can control the opening of the main relay or connect to ignition power supply the engine control can communicate with controller of other node on the network through can to ensure each performance of the system it can also be connected with the data link connector dlc to facilitate the diagnostic operation of the service personnel main relay the main relay is in the engine compartment fuse box it is pin relay adopting the din pin layout the relay doesn contain the reverse electrode protection diode this function is performed by the ecm when the engine control module ecm detect that the ignition switch is in ign position it will output low level signal control the relay to engage and the ubr pin of ecm is connected to the power supply voltage so as to meet the start condition failure the main relay may have failure occurred under the following condition relay winding short circuited relay winding open circuit relay winding high resistance relay contact normally open relay contact normally closed relay contact high resistance relay harness open circuit relay harness high resistance relay harness connected to the power supply is short circuited relay harness short to ground ecmfailure to provide ground access in order to determine whether the main relay work normally the following check may be carried out sound check place the ignition key in on position to check whether the fuel pump will work for whether the fuel injector or ignition coil is supported by power apply multimeter to check the voltage of battery if the above are verified it indicate that the main relay work normally cancommunication the engine management system apply can communication network for the communication between ecm and abs bcm and tcm and diagnose output through diagnostic interface immobiliser power supply when the ignition switch is turned to gear the engine control module ecm and body control module bcm communicate through can bus for mutual information verification after verification bcm will control the operation of starter motor ecm will control ignition fuel injection and the engine will be started engine air supply control system the engine air supply control system mainly include electronic throttle body accelerator pedal module intake air pressure temperature sensor camshaft position sensor variable camshaft timing valve engine fuel management engine if the ratio of the air to fuel mass is the fuel can be burnt sufficiently and the engine is at the best working state for this moment as for air supply portion the optimal fuel air ratio is achieved mainly by the control of air inflow and the master stroke of oil cylinder electronic throttle the electronic throttle is composed of throttle throttle actuator dc motor and throttle position sensor etc ecm give instruction to make the dc motor act and affect the throttle opening through the drive mechanism there are two potentiometer acting as position sensor which are fit in reverse phase when the position of the throttle change the two resistance vary linearly one increase and the other decrease when voltage is added the pedal position will be transformed into the voltage output corresponding to the change of the resistance value these two potentiometer along with two potentiometer on the accelerator pedal form part of whole electronic throttle control system monitoring function which can provide the redundancy protection expected by the system control throttle position sensor the throttle position sensor is used to provide throttle angle information for the ecm ecm can obtain the engine load information and operating condition such as start idle speed motored condition part load full load acceleration and deceleration information according to this information actually the throttle sensor is an angle potentiometer characterized by linear output the potentiometer rotor arm and throttle are fitted coaxially when the throttle rotate the potentiometer rotor arm will be driven to slide into certain position and the potentiometer output voltage signal proportional to the throttle position accelerator pedal module there are two potentiometer in the accelerator pedal module acting as sensor their resistance value will vary with the position change of the electronic accelerator pedal while the ecm can response precisely to the movement of the accelerator pedal therefore the movement of the accelerator pedal may be monitored because both potentiometer are fitted in the same phase when the accelerator pedal position change their resistance will increase or decrease linearly when voltage is added the pedal position will be transformed into the voltage output corresponding to the change of the resistance value these two potentiometer and two potentiometer fitted on the electronic throttle used for monitoring the position of the throttle form part of whole electronic throttle control system monitoring function which can provide the redundancy protection expected by system control intake air pressure temperature sensor tmap the intake air pressure temperature sensor measure intake air manifold absolute pressure and intake air temperature and provide engine load and intake air temperature information the intake air pressure portion is measured by piezoelectric type sensor which can provide the load signal to the controller in accordance with the pressure difference between atmosphere and intake manifold the controller provide voltage and feedback voltage to the controller depending on the intake air pressure the intake air temperature portion is measured by ntc negative temperature coefficient type sensor whose resistance change with the intake air temperature and which transfer voltage showing the change of the intake air temperature to the controller the pressure is proportional to output voltage and the temperature is inversely proportional to output resistance camshaft position cmp sensor the camshaft position sensor is applied to cooperate with the rotational speed sensor in the circumstance without electricity distributor to provide the ecm with the camshaft phase information namely identify the compression top dead centre and exhaust top dead centre of the cylinder the camshaft position sensor is composed of hall sensor and rotor made of steel plate the hall sensor is fixed and the rotor with arc boss is fitted on the camshaft the inner magnetic field of the hall sensor will change when the boss pass it which lead to the change of output signal voltage in this way the two top dead centre are identified failure sensor or magnetic ring may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition cmpsensor gap fail to comply with the specification cmpsensor is contaminated by metallic debris cmpsensor hall effect semiconductor is damaged cmpsensor magnetic field is too weak cmpsensor harness is open circuit cmpsensor harness is short circuited cmpsensor harness has high resistance magnetic ring is contaminated by metallic debris the precision of magnetic ring is low due to mechanical damage exhaust camshaft timing is incorrect intake camshaft timing is incorrect engine engine fuel management engine oil temperature sensor the engine oil temperature sensor eot is applied to monitor the engine oil temperature and control variable camshaft timing valve vct valve the variable camshaft timing valve is adjusted at different temperature to obtain optimal intake air failure the eot sensor may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition system lack of oil external harness open circuit external harness short to ground external harness connected to the power supply is short circuited external harness high resistance ecmfailure to provide ground access eotsensor thermistor damaged variable camshaft timing vct valve the engine control module output pulse width modulation pwm signal to drive the solenoid change in duty ratio may adjust the opening of the solenoid the vct system can under the condition of low speed reduce the fuel consumption and improve the performance of engine in the process of exhaust emission especially torque performance ecmit receive signal from the ckp sensor and cmp sensor with these signal the valve timing can be determined by ecm by consulting control chart then ecm operate the vct engine oil control valve installed on one side of cylinder head these solenoid valve control the oil to flow into phase modulator and change the relative position to the camshaft of the phase modulator so as to adjust the valve timing engine ignition control system the engine ignition control system mainly include ignition coil engine speed sensor knock sensor ignition sequence ignition coil the ignition coil turn the dc low voltage of battery to high voltage and ignite the air mixture in cylinder through the spark generated by the discharge of spark plug each ignition coil consist of pair of coil winding around laminated iron core there are four ignition coil fitted on the camshaft cover of the engine and each cylinder has an independent ignition coil connected with the spark plug failure the ignition coil may have failure under the following condition primary winding open circuit primary winding short circuited resistance of primary winding out of tolerance secondary winding open circuit secondary winding short circuited the circuit connecting secondary winding with primary winding is short resistance of secondary winding out of tolerance coil top insulation mechanically damaged crankshaft position ckp sensor crankshaft position sensor is applied to provide information of the engine speed and crankshaft top dead centre in the ignition system without electricity distributor it is hall effect sensor matched with pulse wheel in application the pulse wheel is tooth plate with small tooth separated by and tooth gap on magnetic ring the pulse wheel is fitted on the crankshaft and rotate with the crankshaft when the tooth tip pass by sensor end the pulse wheel made of steel magnetic material cut the magnetic force line of the permanent magnet in the sensor to produce the induced voltage in the coil which act as the rotational speed output signal failure sensor or magnetic ring may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition ckpsensor gap fail to comply with the specification ckpsensor is contaminated by dust ckpsensor hall effect semiconductor is damaged ckpsensor magnetic field is too weak ckpsensor harness is open circuit ckpsensor harness is short circuited ckpsensor harness has high resistance magnetic ring is contaminated by dust the precision of magnetic ring is low due to erosion or mechanical damage the ckp sensor signal is distorted due to out of round or drive disc the ckp sensor signal is changed due to crankshaft radial movement in order to check the ecm for receiving the signal from ckp sensor following operation may be conducted engine fuel management engine check the operation of the fuel pump in the engine starting process if the fuel pump supply fuel when the ignition is on but not operating it indicate that ecm doe not receive ckp sensor signal knock sensor the knock sensor is used to provide electronic controller ecm with the engine knock information to carry out knock control the knock sensor is vibration acceleration sensor it is fitted on the engine cylinder block the sensitive element of the sensor is piezoelectric the vibration of the engine cylinder block is transferred to the piezoelectric through the mass block in the sensor due to the pressure generated from the vibration of mass block the piezoelectric produce voltage on both pole surface to transform the vibration signal into voltage signal to output failure due to the following condition sensor open circuit sensor to ground short circuited sensor circuit intermittently open incorrect knock sensor refit in case of knock sensor signal failure the following may occur store the failure code ecmfailure to inspect engine combustion knock ecmrestore default setting engine fuel supply control system the engine fuel supply control system mainly include upstream oxygen sensor oxygen sensor fuel injector canister valve fuel pump relay the engine fuel supply control system is mainly applied to control the fuel supply for the engine under different working condition when the engine start for the first time and engine speed is higher than the preset speed the system begin open loop operation the control module ignore the signal from the heated oxygen sensor the control module calculate the air fuel ratio according to the input signal from the ect tmap and tp sensor the system stay in open loop state until the following condition are met the output voltage change the increased temperature of is sufficient for normal operation ectthe temperature of the sensor is higher than the specified temperature the engine has started for specified period of time with respect to above condition different engine have their specific value which are stored in an electronic erasable programmable read only memory eeprom after these value are met the system begin closed loop operation during the closed loop running the control module calculate the air fuel ratio and opening time of the injector according to the signal from various sensor especially the heated oxygen senor so that the air fuel ratio is alway close to oxygen sensor the oxygen sensor measure the oxygen content in the engine exhaust to determine if the gasoline and air are burnt completely the electronic controller implement the closed loop control aiming at excessive air coefficient according to the information to ensure that three way catalytic converter have the highest conversion efficiency in such three kind of pollutant as hc co and nox in the exhaust its sensing element is ceramic tube the inner surface and outer surface of which are contacted with exhaust gas and ambient air respectively when the sensing ceramic tube temperature reach it will have the property of solid electrolyte with this property the concentration difference of oxygen is turned to electric potential difference so as to form electric signal output if the mixture is rich the concentration difference of oxygen inside and outside the ceramic tube is high the electric potential difference is high large amount of oxygen ion move from inside to outside and the output voltage is high approximate to if the mixture is thin the concentration difference of oxygen inside and outside the ceramic tube is low the electric potential difference is low only few of oxygen ion move from inside to outside and the output voltage is low approximate to the oxygen sensor consist of the front oxygen sensor and rear oxygen sensor the former is used to measure the oxygen content in the engine exhaust to implement the closed loop control and the latter is used to monitor the conversion function of the three way catalyst failure may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition contaminated by leaded fuel contaminated by carbon or silicon deposit impact damage mechanical impact engine engine fuel management external harness open circuit external harness connected to the power supply is short circuited external harness short to ground external harness high resistance heating device open circuit heating device short circuited ecmfailure to provide ground access for heating device harness shielding not connected to the ground fuel injector the fuel injector according to the command of the ecm inject fuel within the specified time to supply the engine with fuel and have it atomized ecmthe ecm energiz the injector coil to form magnetic force when the magnetic force is strong enough to overcome the resultant force of the return spring pressure needle valve gravity and friction the needle valve begin to rise and the fuel injection process start when fuel injection pulse end the pressure of return spring make the needle valve close again failure the fuel injector may fail under the following condition nozzle is contaminated by dust or wax flow is reduced nozzle offset injection positioned incorrectly fuel filter blockage winding open circuit winding short to ground winding connected to the power supply is short circuited winding resistance out of tolerance external harness open circuit external harness short to ground external harness connected to the power supply is short circuited electric fuel pump the electric fuel pump transfer the fuel from the fuel tank to the engine and provide sufficient fuel pressure and rich fuel it is dc motor driven vane pump which is fitted in the fuel tankandimmersedinthefuel and lubrication the battery supply power to the electric fuel pump via fuel pump relay which allow the electric fuel pump circuit to be closed only when the relay start and the engine operate when the engine stop running due to accident the fuel pump will stop operating automatically failure if the fuel pump relay fail the following will occur at the time of start if the fuel system pressure fall to zero the engine will fail to ignite if the fuel system pressure is lower than normal operating pressure the engine may try to ignite but fail to work normally if the fuel system pressure is normal the engine will ignite to operate but will flame out finally with decrease in fuel pressure and fail to restart during driving if the fuel pump relay fail during driving the engine will lose power and finally shut down due to shortage of fuel the fuel pump relay may have failure under the following condition relay coil short circuited relay coil open circuit relay coil high resistance relay contact normally open relay contact normally closed relay contact high resistance relay harness open circuit relay harness high resistance relay harness connected to the power supply is short circuited relay harness short to ground ecmfailure to provide ground access carry out the following check and check the fuel pump relay for normal operation sound check place the ignition key in on position to check whether the fuel pump will work for check the ecm for providing an access to ground for the fuel on position and during the starting remove the fuel pump relay connect multimeter between the power supplied by the vehicle and the fuse box pin connected with fuel pump pin check the output according to the circuit diagram after the ignition is on as the ecm internal drive circuit consume voltage the measured voltage may be lower than the battery voltage canister control valve the canister control valve is applied to control the regeneration air flow of the fuel evaporation control system the canister in the fuel evaporation control system absorb fuel steam from fuel tank till it cannot absorb any more ecmit control the canister control valve to open and allow engine fuel management engine fresh air and saturated fuel steam in the canister to form regeneration air flow then send it to the engine air inlet pipe the electronic controller based on the different working condition of engine change the duty ratio of the pulse signal transmitted to the canister control valve electromagnetic coil to control the regeneration air flow moreover the flow is also influenced by the pressure difference on both end failure the canister control valve may have failure occurred under the following condition electromagnetic winding open circuit electromagnetic winding short circuited external harness connected to the power supply is short circuited external harness short to ground external harness high resistance ecmfailure to provide pwm signal mechanical problem occur in the valve auxiliary heat management the heat management portion mainly include compressor relay request on off engine coolant temperature sensor cooling fan relay low speed high speed pressure sensor the engine adjust the opening or closing of the high low speed cooling fan relay according to the coolant temperature after the driver press the request on off on the instrument panel ecm monitor the status of the pressure sensor signal and decide whether to turn on the according to the current engine coolant temperature sensor signal and engine speed signal engine coolant temperature sensor the coolant temperature sensor is used to provide the engine coolant temperature information whereby the controller can correct the fuel injection and ignition it is negative temperature coefficient ntc thermistor whose resistance value is reduced as the temperature rise but it is not linear relationship the thermistor is mounted inside copper heat sleeve failure ectthe sensor may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition the system is lack of coolant due to leakage external harness open circuit external harness short to ground external harness connected to the power supply is short circuited external harness high resistance ecm failure to provide ground access ect thermistor damaged cooling fan relay unit the cooling fan relay unit is fitted on the left side of the radiator which is equipped with two relay the cooling fan is controlled by the ecm and is manipulated through the relay unit ecm it use the information provided by the ect sensor bcm and pressure sensor to limit the cooling temperature of the engine auxiliary external assistance the external assistance portion mainly include alternator control input clutch switch brake pedal switch brake lamp alternator control input alternator for the battery charge while the engine operate brake switch ecmit monitor the switch status and transfer the data to the tcm via the can bus and the tcm control the gearbox clutch to open when the brake pedal is operated the brake switch is activated clutch switch mt only the clutch sensor is fitted on the master cylinder of the clutch and is powered by the ecm when the clutch pedal is depressed the ecm will receive the clutch release signal when the clutch pedal is reset the ecm will receive the clutch resonator engine manifold exhaust system engine exhaust tail pipe middle exhaust pipe rear lug exhaust pipe rear mounting bracket bolt exhaust pipe rear hook bracket exhaust tail pipe liner nut exhaust tail pipe front exhaust pipe assembly front exhaust pipe lug bolt front exhaust pipe nut exhaust manifold sealing gasket exhaust manifold engine engine manifold exhaust system description air filter the air filter outlet pipe is fitted between the air filter and the throttle body which is fixed by the worm drive clamp the air filter assembly is fixed to the upper part of the engine with air filter mounting pin exhaust system the exhaust system consist of the front exhaust pipe and rear exhaust pipe which are connected by nut the muffler of the exhaust system is located on the front exhaust pipe the tail pipe is designed with an one piece pipe with single hole the exhaust system is connected with the vehicle body through rubber lug the rubber lug can decrease the vibration of the exhaust pipe during driving two thermal baffle are fitted respectively on the upper part of the exhaust muffler and on the outer surface of the fuel tank this can decrease the thermal radiation from the exhaust pipe to protect the shift device and fuel system engine manifold exhaust system engine operation air filter air enter into the air filter through the air filter inlet pipe located at the front end cooling module the impurity in the air are filtered by the air filter element and kept in the air filter lower housing air from the air filter is transferred to the throttle body via the air filter outlet pipe exhaust manifold and exhaust system the high temperature pressure gas generated during the operation of the engine will enter into the exhaust manifold through each exhaust port the manifold branch integrally enter into flange and transport the gas into the exhaust system via the three way catalytic converter and finally discharge it through the exhaust tail pipe with the exhaust system muffler flow noise of the gas can be greatly reduced to meet relevant noise regulation at last three way catalytic converter twc warning twc operate at very high temperature therefore extreme caution is necessary when handling this component caution for the twc to work efficiently and to prevent premature failure do not run on leaded fuel and do not allow unburnt fuel to pass through the exhaust system the primary combustion happen in the combustion chamber and the exhaust gas enter into the twc through the exhaust manifold twcit operate effectively only when its minimum working temperature is achieved the exhaust gas enter into an integral structure coated with pt and rh catalyst and twoprocedure reductionandoxidation areperformedhere the nitrogen oxide nox is restored to nitrogen from which the oxide is combined with carbon monoxide co to form carbon dioxide the hydrocarbon hc and co will continue to conduct combustion in the three way catalytic converter with the oxygen in the air which form water and engine engine manifold exhaust system service procedure air filter assembly remove disconnect the battery negative remove bolt securing the metal clamp of the start motor to engine nut battery positive cable to start motor bolt battery positive cable to transmassion bolt battery positive cable to fuse block nut battery positive cable to battery positive clamp bolt battery negative cable to transmission case bolt battery negative cable bracket to the left side rail nut engine harness to battery negative cable nut battery negative cable to clamp bolt battery clip to battery bolt battery bracket to battery tray bolt battery tray to engine fuse box bolt battery tray to battery tray bracket bolt battery tray front of bracket to transmission mount bolt battery tray rear of bracket to body bolt battery tray rear of bracket to the left side rail idler idler to engine engine engine electrical system description and operation system layout starting and charging system layout starter motor generator engine electrical system engine description alternator the alternator is fitted on the right front of the engine the drive pulley is fitted on one end of the rotor which is driven by the multislot shaped belt of the crankshaft the location of the alternator is fixed and the belt tension is maintained by the automatic tension pulley of the wheel train the alternator are similar in structure including stator rotor rectifier and regulator the one way output terminal is connected to the battery positive with thick cable via fuse the alternator is connected to the ground through its bracket three pin connector is the load signal connecting terminal of charging warning lamp output ignition signal input and alternator output the rotor consist of an exciting winding which is wound on theironcoreandfittedontheshaft bothendsoftheironcore extend forming the north and south pole of the magnetic line in the coil the rotor is located in the stator which is fitted on the bearing so as to guarantee stable rotation and enable it to support the high load in the drive belt tension application the stator include stator core and stator coil which has bracket supporting at both end the stator is made of the thin and soft iron sheet its slot fit with the stator coil and is fixed on the alternator housing the stator has three set of coil which are made of copper wire with insulating paint these coil winding are connected in triangle connection scheme thus the tail end of each winding is connected together the output current is provided from the other end of each winding the stator coil allow the magnetic flux from the rotor magnetic pole to flow through the stator coil rotation of the rotor make the coil generate ac the rectifier is located at the rear side of the alternator convert the ac generated by the stator coil to dc required for the vehicle electrical system the rectifier include semiconductor diode which are mounted on heat sink for heat dispersion three diode are mounted on the front three on the opposite side and the other two in the middle when the output voltage of the alternator is lower than the battery voltage the rectifier also prevent the current from flowing from the battery to the alternator the integrated circuit regulator is also located at the rear side of the alternator control the output voltage of the alternator to avoid battery overcharging and protect the vehicle electrical system the regulator optimiz the battery charging according to relevant temperature set rated output voltage of and change the output voltage according to the battery load and required load for the vehicle electrical system transistor of the regulator can adjust the output voltage through the quick switch of the internal induced voltage the regulator has an output terminal of the charging indicator which can control the operation of the indicator when the output voltage of the alternator is lower than the battery voltage the indicator illuminate the alternator load signal output is also provided by the regulator and the output data is provided to ecm and ecm adapt to variety of electrical load by adjusting the idle speed the ignition system provide excitation current to the rotor through the collector ring on the alternator rotor shaft when the alternator is at low speed when the speed of the alternator increase the alternator become self excited starter motor the starter motor is located at the left rear side of the engine it is fixed to the mounting hole on the transmission housing with flange bolt the starter motor are of the pre engaged type which include solenoid one way clutch and motor body when the ignition switch is in start position and the engine is requested for start the body control module integrated with anti theft function will output signal to switch on the starter relay after passing anti theft verification battery battery instruction all vehicle have maintenance free lead acid battery equipped at the left side of the engine compartment which is fitted on the pressed steel bracket fixed on the transmission mount fastened by the battery retaining strap and covered by the battery heat shield the battery electrode are of chuck connected pole type warning battery fluid electrolyte contain sulphuric acid it may cause severe burn if it get on your skin or in your eye wear protective clothing and face shield if electrolyte get on your skin or clothe immediately rinse it off with water if electrolyte get in your eye immediately flush eye with water for at least minute and seek medical help the battery has three main function it provide power for starting the engine secondly it is used as voltage regulator of the electrical system lastly it can provide energy in certain amount of time when the electrical demand exceed the engine output compared with the conventional battery the packaging battery have following advantage there is no need to add water during the entire life cycle of the battery engine engine electrical system it adopt the overcharge protective measure if the voltage applied to the battery is too high it will not receive the excess current as the conventional battery will for the conventional battery if the battery continue to be charged when the voltage is too high air leakage will occur causing fluid loss compared with the conventional battery self discharge doe not tend to occur for this kind of battery this is especially important when the battery is not used for long time for the cigar lighter and smaller electrical equipment the electric quantity supplied is more reliable battery charging when the engine is not running by the reason of electric equipment opening for long time vehicle creepage and longtime parking or some malfunction in the alternator the battery might not be charged and will be flat the vehicle even not can be started on this condition the battery need to be charged caution when charging or emergency starting the negative cable should be connected to the body earth well while not the negative pole battery appearance checking before charging if the shell is broken or there is leak do not charge the battery and replace it after finding the reason if the pole is broken do not charge the battery and replace it after finding the reason the bulge battery for over discharging or over charging can not be charged the battery should be replaced before charging clean the pole rub off rust and smear some butter to avoid electricity corroding charging announcement please pull on the security glass keep ventilation and normal temperature when charging do not smoke when charging and avoid kindling in before charging connect the positive cable at first after charging disconnect the negative cable at first battery charging operation and connect two negative pole between charger and battery as well if the battery is not removed from the vehicle negative pole to the body ground well ensure the pole are clean and the charging circuit loop is all right during charging process if the battery temperature is higher than must stop charging and after he battery temperature falling to environment temperature the battery can be charged again do not charge the battery under for the battery whose voltage value is under at the first of charging it may appear the phenomena that the battery cannot be charged because badly discharging the inverse proportion of vitriol in battery is too small the liquid is close to pure water battery inner resistance value is too high with the charging going on the inverse proportion of vitriol in battery will rise up the charging process could become breath again battery measure battery can be measured by familiar equipment on the after sale market such as midtronic sery instrument if the equipment is not type suited the measured data can not be used to judge the battery status battery replacement caution when removing the battery ensure that the alarm is disarmed and that the ignition is switched off alway disconnect the negative terminal first and then the positive terminal when refitting the battery alway fit the positive terminal first followed by the negative terminal refer to battery remove refit when replacing the battery engine electrical system engine operation alternator alternator use the self excited system where many diode supply part of the current generated by the alternator for the rotor alternator can not supply the initial current required for start up of the charging procedure initial current is supplied by the battery power supply through ignition switch to activate the exciting winding to start up the charging procedure when the ignition switch is turned on the battery voltage is also supplied through the charging warning lamp and ground via the field winding of the alternator so that the led warning lamp illuminate when starting the engine the rotor is rotating in the stator generating the phase alternating current ac when the rotor speed increase the output voltage will rise quickly and the potential difference of the diode output end of the magnetic field will raise when the voltage increase the charging warning lamp will darken gradually when the alternator output voltage is equal to the battery voltage the lamp will go out at this moment the alternator can generate sufficient self excited current via the diode in the magnetic field and begin to charge the battery if the charging warning lamp remain on the charging system fail and check must be performed otherwise the battery will be damaged starter motor when the ignition switch is in start position the coil will supply power to connect the pinion and the gear ring the pinion move spirally around the motor reel thus the motor reel can achieve the connection through the gear ring on the starter motor the motor drive the pinion through the transmission instead of the motor reel directly when starting the engine the one way clutch prevent motor armature overspeed the driver should not remove the key from the start position so that the motor can keep running after start up of the engine when the ignition switch is released the solenoid coil will release energy to pull out the pinion from the gear ring engine engine electrical system service procedure alternator automatic transmission shift lever assembly nut transmission shift lever sleeve striker transmission shift lever cable clip transmission shift lever cable shift lock control new transmission description gear shift control mechanism the gear shift control mechanism mainly consist of shift lever base gear shift lock up solenoid valve gear shift cable etc the gear shift control mechanism base is fixed to the body central passage with screw the inner latch of the shift lever is engaged with the inner groove of the base shift lever gear unlock lever the shift lever position operation is as follow lock the transmission to prevent the wheel glide choose it only when the vehicle is still and the engine is idling no torque pass to wheel all forward gear can be used while in this position this is the default choosing position of normal driving sport mode tiptronic tiptronic shift if the shift lever move from position to the right side the system will automatically enter into sport mode with different shifting parameter then move the shift lever to separate the tiptronic mode and sport mode offering completely manual shift controlling move the shift lever forward to finish upshift and move the shift lever backward to finish downshift to exit tiptronic mode return the shift lever to position gear shift lock up solenoid valve the transmission is equipped with gear shift lock up solenoid valve located at the bottom of the shift lever the operation of the solenoid valve is determined by the bcm controlled gear shift lock up relay in the passenger compartment fuse box when the shift lever is in position with the ignition switch on and the brake pedal not applied the lock up solenoid valve is not activated at the time the lock tongue of position lock block contact with the corresponding groove at the shift lever bottom so as to prevent the accidental movement of shift lever the lock up solenoid valve is activated only when the brake pedal is depressed enable the solenoid valve to make the solenoid valve push rod move toward front part of the vehicle so as to push the lock tongue of position lock block away from the corresponding groove at the shift lever bottom simultaneously press the lock button on the shift lever knob to allow the shift lever freely change to other position from position shift lever moving out of position in emergency with the ignition on and brake pedal depressed when the shift lever cannot be moved out from gear turn off the ignition switch pull out the ignition key and apply the parking cable depress the brake pedal pry up the upper trim panel of the centre console with proper tool press the gear emergency unlock button located at the left upper corner of the gear shift mechanism move the shift lever to position simultaneously start the engine and shift to the required gear gear shift cable the gear shift cable connect the shift lever to the shift rocker arm on the transmission the outer cable is fixed to the shift lever assembly end with clip and fixed to the body and transmission end by two bolt respectively the inner cable at the shift control mechanism end is connected with the shift transmission shift lock control new lever and the inner cable at the transmission end is buckled to the transmission shift rocker arm via cable ball joint shift lock control new transmission service procedure shift lever knob automatic remove remove the gear panel gear panel remove pull up the sleeve loosen and discard the metal clamp on the knob pull up the shift lever knob with care and remove the knob and sleeve assembly from the shift lever note that do not touch the lock button by hand caution dododo not press press press the lock lock lock button button button tototo the shift shift shift lever lever lever knob knob knob when when when removing removing removing the knob knob knob refit place the shift lever in position locate new metal clip to the knob make sure the lock button of the shift lever knob is in the correct position if unable to avoid or mistake let the lock button in the shift lever knob please do the following remove the red limit lever of the new shift lever knob insert the limit lever into the very bottom of the shift lever knob pull out the limit lever and the lock button of the shift lever knob can restore to the normal state insert the knob assembly into the second slot of the shift lever press the lock button and check if it can retract freely if it doe the knob and the shift lever are correctly fitted clamp the bulge of the clip with caliper ensure that the knob can be pulled fit the gear panel gear panel refit transmission shift lock control new shift lever assembly automatic remove place the shift lever in position remove the battery tray battery transmission description overview this model is equipped with an aisin aw ts electronically controlled speed automatic transmission this transmission is applicable to front engine front drive vehicle with maximum input torque of nm tcmit control the system in way similar to that used by otherecuequipped on the vehicle it receive information from the sensor or otherecu calculate according to the pulse spectrum information stored in thetcmmemory and then output signal to the actuator or otherecu the signal exchange with otherecuis mainly conducted through the controller area network can bus the diagnostic test can be conducted through the data link connector when the transmission has some failure tcmwill store related diagnostic trouble code dtc which can be retrieved with the scan tool see diagnosis for more detail diagnosis the gear selection is conducted through the shift lever on the centre console the shift lever supply the driver with selectable gear including park reserve neutral drive and sport mode shift mode is controlled according to one of the preprogramming parameter set that grouped to different driving mode available mode economy mode sport mode the sport mode can activate the tiptronic mode and supply manual control the current driving mode can be displayed on the instrument pack after the vehicle is started it will not be displayed if the economy mode is selected when selecting the tiptronic mode the information of the selected gear will be displayed on the instrument pack transmission assembly ts adopt lightweight and compact ravigneavx planetary gear compared with traditional automatic transmission needing planet gear set to realize upshift and downshift ts can realize the gear shift with ravigneavx planetary gear set it make the drive mechanism simpler and lighter the ravigneavx planetary gear has pair of sun gear pinion in the same planetary gear unit have different siz so it have more transmission ratio compared with the traditional planetary gear unit all gear ratio are obtained through the planetary gear set in the transmission housing the single component in the planetary gear set is driven or locked by clutch brake and one way clutch the clutch brake and one way clutch control the engagement and rotation direction of these element in the planetary gear set so as to generate the selection of gear and gear drive gear ratio and reserve gear ratio the power take off of the drive system will be transmitted to the differential through the main reduction gear transmission gear ratio gear speed ratio gear gear gear gear reverse gear main reduction gear countershaft hydraulic control system based on the hydraulic pressure generated by the oil pump tcmsend signal to each solenoid valve these signal will control the pressure applied to the hydraulic torque converter planetary gear set clutch and brake according to vehicle driving condition valve body assembly the transmission use solenoid valve on the valve body the solenoid valve are activated or deactivated by thetcm and control theatfflow shift sequence supplied to the clutch and the brake hydraulic torque converter lubrication and cooling the solenoid valve may be divided into linear transmission transmission at control solenoid valve and switch solenoid valve according to their operation switch solenoid valve linear control solenoid valve slu emop each solenoid valve is provided with one inner coil and one needle valve the needle valve is activated by the voltage that pass through the solenoid coil and it open or close the hydraulic circuit the shift solenoid valve close the hydraulic circuit to accommodate to the current liquid flow the linear control solenoid valve can control the hydraulic circuit with better linearity all solenoid valve are powered the grounding of the shift solenoid valve and lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu and electromagnetic oil pump emop is provided by thetcmand the grounding of the shift solenoid valve is provided by the valve housing solenoid valve shift solenoid valve the shift solenoid valve is fitted on the valve body tcmcontrol the on off of the shift solenoid valve it switch the oil passage according to the on off state of shift solenoid valve to realize the fuel economy as failure protection function when any shift solenoid valve is abnormal tcmcan cut off the current to the solenoid valve shift solenoid valve is normally closed shift control solenoid valve and shift control solenoid valve shift control solenoid valve shift control solenoid valve the shift control solenoid valve are fitted on the valve body tcmcontrol the solenoid valve and and the shift control solenoid valve linearly control the oil pressure to the clutch and and the brake and to realize smooth shift according to the combination of different state of the shift control solenoid valve the transmission realiz the gear shift from gear to gear or from gear to gear as failure protection function when any shift solenoid valve is abnormal tcmcan cut off the current to the solenoid valve transmission at transmission inlet outlet discharge the shift solenoid valve and are normally open and the oil pressure decrease with the increase of the current the shift control solenoid valve is normally closed and the oil pressure increase with the increase of the current lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu the lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu is fitted on the valve body and it linearly control the operating pressure of the lock up clutch in the hydraulic torque converter based on the engine speed throttle opening input speed sensor signal and output speed sensor sp signal so as to realize the lock up control as failure protection function when any lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu is abnormal tcmcan cut off the current to the solenoid valve inlet outlet discharge the lock up clutch control solenoid valve is normally closed and the oil pressure increase with the increase of current transmission transmission at electromagnetic oil pump emop electromagnetic oil pump emop emop is fitted on the front of the valve body emop is of the same structure as the linear solenoid valve with its piston moving based on the on off signal sent generate hydraulic pressure the hydraulic pressure generated by the emop is only used for controlling clutch under the stop state of stop start to enable the smooth transition of the engine to start state if any abnormality is detected the operation of emop is not permitted under the stop state of stop start for failure protection meanwhile the current of emop will be cut off inlet outlet valve body solenoid valve transmission end harness assembly valve body harness connector pin no description oil temperature sensor ot electromagnetic oil pump emop lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu pressure control solenoid valve pressure control solenoid valve pressure control solenoid valve oil temperature sensor ot electromagnetic oil pump emop lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu shift solenoid valve transmission at transmission pin no description pressure control solenoid valve pressure control solenoid valve pressure control solenoid valve the valve body solenoid valve harness combine the harness of the shift solenoid valve the lock up clutch control solenoid valve slu the shift control solenoid valve and the emop andatfthe oil temperature sensor ot into connector and mount it in an automatic transmission hydraulic torque converter hydraulic torque converter assembly hydraulic torque converter housing lock up clutch turbine guide wheel pump impeller oil pump input shaft the hydraulic torque converter is in its housing in the right transmission housing the hydraulic torque converter function to combine the engine and the transmission the power output by the engine is transmitted to the transmission through the hydraulic force and mechanism through the lock up clutch of the hydraulic torque converter under certain gear and working condition the hydraulic torque converter is connected with the engine through the drive plate the hydraulic torque converter comprise the pump impeller guide wheel and turbine the engine drive the pump impeller while the turbine drive the transmission the guide wheel is on the one way clutch between the pump impeller and the turbine the pump impeller obtain oil and swing it into the turbine making the turbine rotate and transmit power the guide wheel make the liquid flow returning from the turbine change direction and enter the pump impeller again in the rotation direction same with that of the pump impeller and at the optimal angle of highest power transmission efficiency the one way clutch prevent the guide wheel from moving backward so as to obtain accurate liquid change direction when the engine is idling the pump impeller transmit extremely little liquid flow and the turbine remain no rotation and therefore no power is transmitted to the transmission when the engine speed increase the pump impeller swing more liquid flow to make the turbine rotate with the increase of the engine speed the turbine speed begin to increase the turbine speed become faster and faster more and more liquid flow is swung to the back of the guide wheel causing the guide wheel to rotate in the same direction with the turbine when the turbine speed is close to the pump impeller speed the centrifugal force of both are almost equal and all the three component rotate roughly in the same speed this is called coupling point the torque increase by time or the transmission rate remain changing until reaching to the coupling point for obtaining the required power to climb mountain road the driver step down the accelerator pedal and the hydraulic torque converter will promote the torque increment as response when driving on the flat road at the cruise speed the required power is not big therefore the hydraulic torque converter remain the speed ratio of transmission control module tcm tcmis located on the dash panel at the driver side it is connected with the automatic transmission harness through two connector the connector has pin and the connector has pin transmission transmission at tcmuse the electrically erasable programmable read only memory eeprom therefore the new or be used to conduct the external configuration eepromcan alsotcmupdate the new information and market specific data to input new information thetcmmust be configured with scan tool reconfigured for many time as required so as to meet the constantly changing parameter and regulation the signal value of the transmission sensor and the actuator these stored signal guarantee that the transmission can alway obtain the optimal performance if the battery voltage is too low such as battery discharge the information will be lost when the engine start for the first time after the battery is discharged or disconnected tcmwill resume to the default value tcminside the stored value to be rapidly accessed again input and output the sensor signal monitor the transmission state the signal and compare them with the data stored in the memory if these signal are not in thetcmrange of parameter stored tcmwill adjust the operation of the transmission through the actuator to provide the optimal driveability and other performance tcmadjust the operation of the transmission through the following actuator shift solenoid valve lock up clutch control solenoid valve of the hydraulic torque converter slu shift control solenoid valve electromagnetic oil pump emop tcmit also connect with the following unit through thecanbus ecm bcm instrument pack data link connector tcmreceive the input signal such as the throttle opening fromecm engine coolant temperature engine speed engine torque brake switch etc tcmalso receive the input signal such as the contact position of the neutral start switch and input and output speed atfoil temperature valve body solenoid valve state mode switch etc in addition thetcmoutput the torque decrease request shift gear information and failure warning information toecmand output execution signal etc to the valve body solenoid valve transmission at transmission wiring terminal of automatic transmission control module tcm tcmend view and pin shaft at the front end of the transmission tcmreceive the voltage output provided by the neutral start switch to determine the gear of the automatic transmission tcmdetermine the position of the shift lever by monitoring the group of gear contact on the neutral start switch each group of contact correspond to shift lever position respectively and only one group of contact alway provide battery voltage for tcm tcmmonitor this switch output the electric input of the neutral start switch is supplied by the fuse in the passenger compartment fuse box only when the shift lever is in or gear can the contact st close the loop in this case the starter motor relay will be controlled engage and the vehicle will be started speed sensor tcmuse the input of the following three speed signal to set the correct shift sequence input speed output speed vehicle speed the transmission speed sensor is the hall type and generate square wave signal which is converted into speed vehiclecanspeed signal generated by theabscontrol module is collected through the bus based on these signal tcmcontrol the engine torque shift time and lock up input speed sensor clutch hub output speed sensor countershaft drive gear input speed sensor the input speed sensor is on the transmission housing for measuring the speed of the transmission input shaft the magnetic resistance distribution ring on the hub of clutch will rotate when the hydraulic torque converter drive the inputshaft each gear will generate signal and send it totcm output speed sensor sp the output speed sensor is on the transmission housing for measuring the speed of transmission output shaft the output speed sensor operate in the same manner as the input speed sensor but its signal are generated when the gear tooth pass through the magnetic resistance distribution ring on the park gear transmission transmission at oil temperature sensor oil temperature sensor the oil temperature sensor is integrated with the transmission internal harness and mounted on the valve body it directly detect the temperature of the oil in the hydraulic control circuit tcmuse oil temperature sensor to monitor the oil temperature in the transmission to accommodate the oil temperature change and realize smooth gear shift in wide temperature range the oil temperature sensor has an electric output and ground viatcm the fluid temperature sensor is negative temperature coefficient sensor when the temperature rise the sensor resistance lower when the temperature decrease the sensor resistance increase the resistance change is inversely proportional to the transmission oil temperature calculate the transmission oil the sensor resistance brake pedal switch the brake pedal switch is located on the pedal box in the cab tcmuse this switch to monitor the state of the brake pedal ecmmonitor the switch state and transmit the data viacanbus totcm if signal error of the brake pedal switch occur the shift lever can not shift to other gear from or gear no additional engine braking will be generated transmission oil cooler the transmission oil cooler is located in the collector of the radiator the oil cooler pipe connect the transmission and the oil cooler controller area network can bus tcmcommunicate viacanbus with otherecuand control the following shift torque engine coolant temperature engine torque and speed vehicle
