bleeding bolt evp to bracket brake system brake description and operation system layout brake system front brake caliper assembly front ventilated brake hole disc abs modulator brake booster assembly brake master cylinder front wheel speed sensor rear brake drum rear wheel speed sensor brake reservoir air intake manifold vacuum tube brake brake system description vacuum servo assembly the vacuum servo booster assembly provide boost during braking to reduce the pedal force required for braking in case of the vacuum booster assembly failure the hydraulic system maintain brake function but greater pedal braking force is required due to lack of vacuum assistance the vacuum booster assembly is connected to the bulkhead lhd pedal box rhd via stud the brake master cylinder assembly is connected via two fixing bolt at the front end of the vacuum booster assembly the brake servo assembly consist of circular housing which contain two diaphragm central plate control valve assembly input and output push rod and filter the input push rod is connected to the brake pedal the output push rod locate in the primary piston of the master cylinder protective gaiter is installed on the control valve assembly where it extend from the rear of the housing line from the engine non return valve is incorporated into the vacuum line the control valve assembly consist of valve body containing valve piston valve spring and an input rod spring the valve control vacuum port in the valve body the piston control an air inlet port between the valve and the piston reaction disc and ratio disc separate the piston from the output push rod guide tube on the front of the valve body is attached to the front diaphragm and supported in bush in the central plate return spring locate in the open end of the guide tube brake master cylinder assembly tandem brake master cylinder is installed on the brake servo assembly fluid is supplied via remote reservoir connected to the master cylinder via two rubber hose the master cylinder is fixed to the front of the brake servo via stud fixing when the brake pedal is pressed the brake master cylinder assembly produce hydraulic pressure so that the brake operate the tandem master cylinder contain two chamber and associated piston each piston chamber control circuit the rear piston produce brake pressure for the primary circuit this cover lhf rhr the front piston produce brake pressure for the secondary circuit and this cover rhf lhr the remote brake fluid reservoir is installed higher than the master cylinder in order to maintain gravitational feed the fluid reservoir is partitioned inside to feed fluid to each brake circuit independently thus avoiding the condition that both circuit could become devoid of fluid the brake fluid reservoir utiliz fluid level switch reed style switch featuring permanent magnet and float is installed in the fluid reservoir which is linked to the fluid level handbrake and ebd warning lamp in the instrument pack front brake each front brake consist of single piston sliding brake caliper assembly fitted to the hub and ventilated brake disc rear brake each rear brake consist of back plate brake drum brake shoe ahydraulicwheelcylinder aselfadjustingrodandreturn spring the back plate is installed on the stub axle retained by the stub axle bolt and provide location for brake shoe and hydraulic wheel cylinder the brake drum is fitted to the flange of bearing and rotate with the wheel each rearward brake shoe is fitted with handbrake cable lever brake pedal and brake lamp switch the brake pedal is installed on pivot shaft in the pedal mounting bracket on the engine bulkhead clevis pin and clip connect the input rod of the brake servo to the brake pedal tang on the end of the brake pedal activate plunger style brake switch in the pedal mounting bracket the brake switch is adjustable and secured in position using lock nut the brake lamp switch provide two brake pedal state output one power the other ground this signal is supplied to the ecm and used to illuminate the stop lamp brake system brake service procedure low brake fluid level indicator switch rhd remove left front right front left warning braking operation may be seriously impaired if the incorrect bleed sequence is used lower the vehicle top up fluid level in brake reservoir to max mark apply brake module specification torque description value screw front speed wheel sensor screw rear speed wheel sensor bolt abs to bracket rear brake pipe to modulator brake system brake module description and operation system layout brake system control component layout brake fluid reservoir brake fluid level sensor vacuum booster brake master cylinder brake modulator front wheel speed sensor assembly rear wheel speed sensor assembly brake module brake system system control diagram brake system control chart brake system brake module description anti lock brake system abs during braking the function of the abs prevent wheel lock and thus the vehicle can stay stable even in emergency warning abs is an aid to retaining steering control and stability while braking abs cannot defy the natural law of physic acting on the vehicle abs will not prevent accident resulting from excessive cornering speed following another vehicle too closely aquaplaning etc the additional control provided by abs must never user the fitting of abs doe not imply that the vehicle will alway stop in shorter distance tip during normal braking the feel of the brake pedal on vehicle equipped with abs will be the same as that on non abs vehicle during anti lock braking operation the driver will experience feedback in the form of pulsating brake pedal and solenoid pump motor noise from the abs modulator in conventional brake the abs doe not work brake fluid directly flow into each wheel cylinder from the reservoir through hydraulic control unit hcu channel to apply brake to each wheel once abs ecu detect that wheel locking occur through the wheel speed sensor ecu will give an order to close the inlet valve and the brake fluid will no longer flow into the wheel cylinder meanwhile the outlet valve is opened to discharge the hydraulic oil from the wheel cylinder to allow the wheel to rotate if it is detected that wheel slip normally the outlet valve will be closed due to low brake pressure and the brake fluid will flow into the wheel cylinder from the inlet valve to increase the brake force if the wheel lock in emergency the inlet outlet valve will be closed at the same time to keep the brake pressure in the wheel cylinder so as to exert the best brake performance electronic brake distribution ebd ebdthe function of the ebd is to optimize the distribution of the hydraulic pressure between the front and rear axle to keep the vehicle at stable state in brake process according to the vehicle load condition and road condition with the abs ecu in the normal brake mode the vehicle deceleration of and above moderate to high brake pedal load the ebd function can be automatically implemented in forward or reverse mode buttheebdoperate at lower wheel slip limit and the ebd is only applicable to the rear wheel brake during braking if the abs ecu detect that the rear axle wheel is slower than the front axle wheel that is the possibility of wheel slip exist the abs ecu will send signal to the hydraulic pressure regulator to turn off the fluid inlet solenoid valve of the rear wheel brake this prevent further increase of the hydraulic pressure of the rear wheel brake but allow the hydraulic pressure of the front wheel brake to increase the whole brake force is thus maximized if the rear wheel speed return to the calibration wheel slip limit range the abs ecu will send signal to turn on the fluid inlet valve gradually which allow progressive increase of the hydraulic pressure of the rear axle brake ebdit operation can be felt through the following way the movement of the brake pedal is more violent by turning off the fluid inlet solenoid valve and the brake pedal has slight pulse as result of the fluid inlet solenoid valve turning on when the brake pedal is released the ebd will stop immediately ebdthe wheel slip limit that enable the ebd vary with the vehicle speed brake module brake system service procedure abs sensor front remove warning do not work constant velocity joint assembly constant velocity joint circlip clip constant velocity universal joint gaiter clip power vibration damping ring clip internal joint gaiter clip circlip tripod joint circlip driveline axle driveline axle description each drive shaft comprise of solid shaft with inner and outer constant velocity joint the inner joint is of the tripode type with spherical bushing to reduce sliding resistance the shaft and joint are one assembly is splined to the shaft and secured with snap ring all joint are packed with grease and protected by gaiter both drive shaft are fitted with dynamic damper the drive shaft transmit engine torque via the gearbox to the front wheel the dynamic damper reduce any vibration produced by the drive shaft rotation driveline axle driveline axle diagnostic diagnosis mechanical check when rotating rattle sound can be heard when the worn or damaged constant velocity universal joint of the front wheel drive shaft rotate rattle sound may be heard if there rattle sound check the constant velocity universal joint shield of the front wheel drive shaft for crack or damage clunk sound can be heard during accelerating and sliding if click sound can be heard during rotation check the following check the external universal joint for wear or damage check the seal for breakage or damage shake or vibration when accelerating start shake start shake refer to the vibration felt on the steering wheel and or the front of the vehicle when the vehicle is shifted from static starting to rapid accelerating during acceleration start shake may also refer to the swing occur in the front and the rear of the vehicle the start shake of the front wheel drive vehicle may caused by the following internal tee universal joint is worn or damaged internal universal joint is severely worn or ceratoid the universal joint show severe ceratoid angle which is normally caused by the excessive height of the front cocked head or the spring when the power assembly suspension is damaged or mismatched following situation may occur angle of the universal joint is oversize start shake during rapid acceleration high torque of the vehicle power assembly cause increase of the front suspension when the suspension raise the angle of the internal tee universal joint willincreaseaccordingly situation appear universal joint is worn oversize angle exist to floor bolt squab to cushion bolt striker to body interior fitting seat description and operation system layout seat layout driver seat front passenger seat rear seat seat interior fitting description front seat assembly overview seat headrest seat back assembly seat back cover and foam seat back frame seat cushion assembly seat cushion cover and foam seat cushion frame the common seat mainly consist of headrest seat back seat cushion and accessory among them both seat back assembly and seat cushion assembly consist of cover foam and frame the seat assembly and the main component are repaired as fitting according to the vulnerability and maintainability of the component the front seat assembly may have different configuration according to vehicle configuration and seat exterior for example way manual seat forward backward adjustment seat back inclination way power seat forward backward adjustment seat back inclination and seat height adjustment fabric of high quality or pvc and fabric optional seat side airbag rear seat assembly the rear seat of this model is type seat with reclinable seat back if you want to expand the luggage compartment space first fully lower or remove all the rear seat headrest then pull up the unlock strap on both side respectively and fold the seat back forward when unfolding the rear seat back pull up the seat back unlock strap push the seat back until it reach an appropriate position and the seat back is locked when it click interior fitting seat operation manual seat driver seat seat longitudinal position adjusting handle seat height adjusting handle seat back adjusting handle the front rear position of the seat can be adjusted by the handle in the front of the seat lift the handle and pull it to the desired position release the handle to lock the seat to the selected position the adjusting handle is located in the front of the trim panel outside the seat cushion which can adjust the seat height lift or press the handle repeatedly to raise or lower the seat the adjusting handle is located in the rear of the height adjusting handle which can adjust the seat back inclination lift the handle and pull it to the desired position release the handle to lock the seat to the selected position front passenger seat the passenger seat is adjusted in the same way as the driver seat the front rear position of the seat can be adjusted by the handle in the front of the seat the position of the sear back can be adjusted by the side handle seat interior fitting service procedure front seat remove if the seat is adjustable raise the seat fully fully move the seat backward using suitable lever remove fixing trim cover remove bolt fixing front of seat to body discard fully move the seat forward using suitable lever remove fixing trim cover remove bolt fixing front of seat to body discard disconnect all electrical connector remove seat from vehicle refit place the front seat in the body front seat mounting position connect all electrical connector warning alway ensure srs wiring is routed correctly be careful to avoidtrapping or pinching the srs wiring do not leave the connector hanging loose or allow srs component to hang from their harness look for possible point of chafing fix the seat to the vehicle body fit new rear bolt and tighten them to refit fixing trim cover fully move the seat backward fit new front retaining bolt and tighten them to refit fixing trim cover front seat squab remove remove the front seat front seat remove release the interior trim panel fixing screw remove switch it drive the front wiper to work by closing the two wiper relay in the engine compartment fuse box the switch for single wipe intermittent wipe and low high speed wipe form closed circuit with high speed wipe disconnected closed high speed wipe thebcm will send signal to the wiper relay when relay close the power supply connect the motor directly through the resistor for deceleration at this stage the wiper work at high speed low speed wipe when choosing low speed wipe mode the bcm send signal to the wiper relay the power supply pass through relay before connecting with the motor by resistor the resistor reduce the power so the wiper work at low speed auto wipe variable intermittent wipe the function of variable intermittent wipe the vehicle is provided with wiper delay potentiometer when receiving valid wiper interval request the bcm will control the wiper at slow speed the time of intermittent wipe vary with the vehicle speed level and the setting of wiper delay potentiometer when the switch is in intermittent low speed or high speed position the bcm will adjust the interval of intermittent wipe according to the vehicle speed abs ecu monitor the wheel speed signal and transmit it to the bcm through hs can bus if the wiper washer lever switch is in the intermittent wipe the bcm adjust the selected delay time by comparing vehicle speed with the resistance corresponding to the intermittent delay rotary switch programmed wash wipe when the ignition switch in acc or on position and the front wiper switch is enabled the bcm control the front wiper to work direction indicator lamp switch is enabled the bcm flash the corresponding direction indicator lamp by identifying the switch status of the direction indicator lamp then the direction indicator lamp on the instrument pack flash and provide the current status of the direction indicator lamp for the driver by acoustic warning rear fog lamp when the ignition switch is in on position the low beam headlamp is enabled then the activation of the rear fog lamp switch can illuminate the rear fog lamp the bcm send the on off status of the rear fog lamp to the ipk by receiving the activation signal of the rear fog lamp and the indicator of rear fog lamp on the ipk illuminate when the rear fog lamp switch low beam headlamp or front fog lamp if equipped is disabled the rear fog lamp is cancelled if the rear fog lamp is already activated at the time of engine start it will remain on hazard warning lamp the hazard warning lamp is comprised of the front rear direction indicator lamp and side direction indicator lamp the hazard warning lamp switch is marked with triangle which is touch switch when the hazard warning lamp is activated the triangle mark on the switch will flash when the position lamp or headlamp is enabled the triangle mark dim but it will still flash the hazard warning lamp can be activated manually regardless of current position of the ignition switch operation priority of direction indicator lamp and hazard warning lamp if the direction indicator lamp switch is enabled when the hazard warning lamp is working the direction indicator lamp will cancel the hazard warning lamp then the hazard warning lamp will still flash after the direction indicator lamp switch or ignition switch is disabled indicator lamp is working the hazard warning lamp will cancel the direction indicator lamp the direction indicator lamp will resume after the hazard warning lamp is disabled the hazard warning lamp can be activated automatically under the following situation in case of impact when the bcm detect an impact signal it will activate the hazard warning lamp automatically accidental activation of the hazard warning lamp during impact can be cancelled by pressing the hazard warning lamp switch for more than low beam headlamp when the ignition switch is in on position and the lamp lever switch is turned to the low beam the low beam headlamp illuminate the low beam headlamp is controlled by the relay in the bcm high beam headlamp when the ignition switch is in on position the bcm close the high beam relay and illuminate the high beam headlamp by identifying the status of the lamp lever switch when the high beam is enabled the indicator of high beam on the ipk illuminate position lamp tail lamp license plate lamp the position lamp tail lamp and license plate lamp are controlled by the same output port of the bcm when moving the headlamp and width lamp knob to width lamp position the bcm can detect the on signal of the width lamp tail lamp and license plate lamp and output signal to turn on an external relay so that the width lamp tail lamp and license plate lamp will illuminate simultaneously reverse lamp with the ignition switch in on position the reverse lamp illuminate when the shift lever is moved to gear lamp lighting system note for vehicle vehicle vehicle with with with automatic automatic automatic transmission transmission switch is enabled the bcm will wake up the security system lighting system and diagnostic system when the ignition switch is in acc position the bcm allow the washer wiper and power window system to operate when the ignition switch is in on position the fuel system start to work and the bcm communicate and transmit information with other ecus through the can and lin bus load management bcm can manage the load of some electric appliance by configuration and ensure low battery power consumption during storage and transportation or idle time production mode it is configured for the bcm during vehicle assembly transportation mode after vehicle assembly programme the bcm so that the transportation mode can be used during transportation and low battery problem can be avoided when the factory deliver the vehicle to the authorized local after sale service centre this mode may restrict the function of some electric appliance the rear fog lamp doe not work when the engine is off the high beam headlamp doe not work when the engine is off the low beam headlamp doe not work when the engine is off the reverse lamp doe not work when the engine is off the direction indicator lamp doe not work when the engine is off the daytime running lamp if equipped doe not work when the engine is off follow me home function cannot be realized if the engine is disabled the hazard warning lamp backlight doe not work but the function of this lamp is not affected when the engine is disabled the window can be ascended but cannot be descended with the aftermarket diagnostic tool the local mg authorised repairer can shift the vehicle load management configuration from transportation mode to normal mode normal mode this mode apply the default setting after completion of the pdi the vehicle can enter into normal operation sleep mode with the ignition switch off and the can and lin buse disabled if the battery is still connected the bcm will stay in sleep mode and be prepared for receiving the signal from the can and lin buse wake up mode in sleep mode when one of the following operation is done the bcm will be waked up activation signal of hazard warning lamp switch is received activation signal of direction indicator lamp switch is received lock signal of internal lock is received unlock signal of internal lock is received activation signal of driver door switch is received activation signal of passenger door switch is received receive the activation signal of the luggage compartment lid switch receive the activation signal of the luggage compartment lid release switch activation signal of driver door lock switch is received activation signal of driver door unlock switch is received ignition switch is in acc position ignition switch is in on position activation signal of internal lamp switch linwake up signal from lin bus canwake up signal from can bus wake up signal from local hard wire valid radio frequency signal load management to meet the demand of the starter motor for high current during engine start some electric appliance shall be powered off for example disconnect the power of the hrw intelligent stop start energy saving system disable switch high beam headlamp reverse lamp horn and interior lamp etc after the engine is started reactivate each electric appliance to limit the current consumption of the battery diagnosis bcm monitor information input and output if any fault is detected the corresponding dtc will be stored in the fault record bcm can detect short and open circuit as well as wrong signal of the can and lin buse when any fault is detected the bcm will close the corresponding function when the fault is eliminated the corresponding function will be activated next time when the activation request is received body control module panel assembly tail gate panel assembly collision repair body repair collision repair body material for different strength requirement of different body part this model adopt various high strength steel plate and ultra high strength steel plate hot formed steel plate the detailed position of different material are as follow only service as per relevant instruction in the manufacturer service manual and service of high strength steel plate and hot formed steel plate as per the modern inverter technology are allowed during the service of the galvanized steel plate the following matter should be noted the paint and zinc coating should be completely ground off before grinding be careful when grinding apply the spot welding paint between the weld contact surface before the spot welding with hairbrush or spray particle on the surface will be absorbed by the electrode so it is necessary to clean the electrode tip frequently body repair collision repair damage diagnosis damage detection during damage detection visually inspect the location collided to determine the direction and size of the impact and check for potential damage for vehicle damaged in an accident query the vehicle speed impact location or rollover location andunderstand the impact form location and angle of the impacted vehicle etc by the impact and area that may be affected by the impact test drive and measuring instrument can also be used for the thorough check of the vehicle confirm if the underbody is deformed and if the whole body is damaged and twisted check if the door can function properly etc to determine the level of damage and the method of repair vehicle visual inspection detail visual inspection damage determination deformation occur not only at the impact location but also at the whole body such as beam suspension and engine etc along the course of the impact systematically check the related part for damage until there is no any sign of damage and the damage in the surrounding area is found characteristic of body damage characteristic of damage caused by front impact characteristic of damage caused by middle impact characteristic of damage caused by rear impact damage measurement accurate measurement is one of the necessary process for completion of impact repair for integral body measurement is more important for successful damage repair because the steering system and suspension are on the body and some suspension are designed according to the requirement for assembly to ensure correct steering and driveability the fit tolerance of the key machining size shall be within the permissible range measurement shall combine with remove operation or it is impossible to determine the damage to facilitate body repair and thorough examination of damage and avoid damage of the part removed due to repair remove the related part the principle for remove is to avoid any component damage measuring point selection screw hole process hole of the component overlap of the body steel sheet machining gap or raised imprint on the steel sheet collision repair body repair method of measurement distance measurement the simplest and most practical method is to measure the distance between the reference point of the component with steel tape centreing measure by locating the centre three dimensional coordinate measurement measure the data of the three dimensional coordinate on the measuring point selected and compare the data with those of the vehicle to determine body damage electronic measurement more accurate and more effective reason for error in body size measurement the error is due to the accuracy of the measuring tool or equipment itself error due to human factor failure to locate the measuring point wrong method is used for measurement failure to use the measuring tool and equipment in proper way precaution for measurement level the body before measurement mark measuring point on the body to prevent confusion make accurate measurement carry out multiple measurement recheck the result of the measurement body repair collision repair repair plan determination underbody correction when the vehicle is severely damaged the underbody is deformed no need to replace the whole body correct the underbody first underbody correction ensure the three dimensional position of the underbody and the general position of the body determine the position of the engine assembly and front suspension and recover the angle for wheel alignment and the position of other assembly after underbody correction repair the body panel body and covering component correction after an impact if the body and covering component only have repairable depression or deformation no serious damage occur no need to replace the component recover its shape by correction sheet metal replacement when the body part has damage at one location or more than one location and the damage or corrosion is beyond the specified degree it is worthless to repair it or repair cannot help it to achieve the specified technical standard and function requirement then replace the sheet metal body replacement when the vehicle is severely damaged the whole body is smashed by the impact the underbody is seriously deformed the two side roof cover engine hood and trunk lid are almost destroyed and the whole body is unrepairable then replace the body according to user demand remove usable assembly and component from the damaged vehicle thoroughly check and repair the major assembly such as engine replace the body assembly and part that shall be replaced and reassemble them according to the assembly process of the vehicle partial replacement when impact occur and only local damage appear for example damage to the front rear fender door engine hood or trunk lid partial replacement can be performed for the body this save trouble time and reduce the cost body pillar replacement when the body pillar windshield pillar front or rear pillar is badly damaged and cannot be corrected by simple repair replace it recover the large scale deformation before replacing the pillar collision repair body repair body correction the purpose of body correction is to recover the correct position and size of the sheet metal by eliminating the deformation plastic deformation and residue stress caused by collision introduction of correcting equipment the correcting equipment shall provide the following feature the equipment shall have high strength body positioning device and fixture it shall have service puller with various shape and different function that can meet the repair requirement for different location be able to perform multipoint and comprehensive correction drawing be able to accurately measure and detect the deviation and repair error for each reference point operation process for correcting equipment regardless of the type of the equipment used its operation is broadly divided into the following step get on the vehicle position clamping drawing measurement auxiliary fixture prevent motion of the vehicle force caused by even and disperse drawing auxiliary support prevent secondary damage to the body due to drawing focus the drawing force on the damaged location and improve the efficiency of the drawing operation prevent moment resulted from drawing correction drawing method drawing sequence note the method and procedure for drawing during drawing draw for little while then release the chain unload and make measurement operate from inside to outside take length as the priority draw the vehicle in the longitudinal direction along the centreline of the vehicle then correct the width correct the vehicle in the horizontal direction finally correct the height stress elimination eliminate the stress during drawing knock the raised location beside the deformed region and the back knock the welding location of the deformed region knock the ridge of the deformed region knock the reinforcement of the deformed region the high strength integral body is susceptible to heat so do not attempt to complete the correction drawing just by operation of one step generally follow the cyclic process draw keep balance draw keep balance if some body are folded after an impact and there is risk of metal damage then heat it when heating only heat the corner or the location where the two panel are connected heating is merely mean to eliminate metal stress not method to be used to soften some location note do not service the body by heating oxygen acetylene apply tensile force to the predetermined location recover the size and shape of the damaged steel sheet carefully and slowly body repair collision repair completely eliminate the stress of the curved steel sheet to achieve accurate repair of the body note overstretch shall be avoided during body correction the only solution to overstretch is to replace the damaged sheet metal collision repair body repair panel repair panel repair method puller method for sheet metal repair meson machine in recent year for this method number of flat gasket puller is welded according to the size of the damaged sheet metal the flat gasket puller is traction medium insert the wire into the medium and draw the wire with manual or mechanical manner apply force to the damaged sheet metal through the medium so that it can be drawn outward and resume its position and shape especially for larger deformation bilayer structure inaccessible location corner transition and door pillar etc the puller traction method provide more convenient method for repair heat shrinkage method local heat shrinkage is commonly used method for sheet metal repair deformation of the sheet metal refer to tensile useheatshrinkagemethod to eliminate tensile stress and resume the shape hammer and jacking block method the hammer and jacking block method is conventional method for sheet metal repair the metaler hold the hammer with one hand and the jacking block with the other hand or use other tool to strike the sheet metal for repair the manual work of the sheet metal require the metaler to have the appropriate skill and experience good repair skill cannot be obtained overnight repair of the sheet metal include repair of the deformed surface and correction of the shape line technical requirement for panel repair no matter which method is used to repair the body surface the specified technical requirement shall be met and the performance and appearance quality of the vehicle shall be resumed shape recovery for repair the shape of the large smooth structure and the wedge structure of the local transition shall be recovered when it is hard to recover the shape change the original shape in an appropriate way the shape shall be symmetrical beautiful firm and durable integrity and fineness of continuous surface most of the car body are produced by mould stamping which have the integrity and fineness of the surface for repair the streamline surface shall have continuous transition the transition point of the surface shall have smooth transition ripple wrinkle dent knock mark scratch and visible defect or obvious defect that can be sensed by touch are not allowed especially for large scale repair ensure the integrity streamline feature continuity and fineness of the continuous surface sufficient strength and rigidity the body of the original design has sufficient strength and rigidity the body repaired shall ensure sufficient strength and rigidity thepermissiblerange ensure the body has no any fatigue damage within the certain distance the entire body shall have the specified rigidity make sure the body panel is able to maintain its shape in use body repair collision repair sheet metal replacement the process for sheet metal replacement is as follow preparation before cutting new old sheet metal remove damaged sheet metal position adjustment and cutting of new part treatment and corrosion prevention before welding final position and welding of the panel treatment after welding accessory installation and inspection after operation principle for sheet metal replacement thin panel replacement thin panel replacement is made for aesthetic purpose and shall follow the requirement below cutting is performed on the shortest position from the foam damage to the foam by welding shall be avoided position ofofof the foam when replacing the pillar and rear fender please remove the windshield do not cut location including any reinforcement thick panel replacement thick panel replacement is made for strength purpose and shall follow the requirement below do not cut the energy absorption area no cutting is allowed within from the front rear of the position for laser welding cutting is performed on the shortest position from the foam damage to the foam by welding shall be avoided position ofofof the foam do not cut location including any reinforcement to ensure welding penetration the size of the cutting seam shall be equal to the thickness of panel preparation before cutting new old sheet metal remove all sealer residue with hot air gun to reduce the toxic fume generated due to heat during welding straighten the existing panel side seam with form block and hammer indicate the actual cutting position on the old body part reserve paper adhesive tape and indicate the location for welding spot elimination welding information provided manufacturer indicate the actual cutting position on the new body part and paste the paper adhesive tape damaged sheet metal remove position the welding spot grind the welding spot carry out rough cut and remove the welding spot collision repair body repair remove the damaged sheet metal position adjustment and cutting of new part position the new panel on the body and adjust its position and size compare the hole adjust the clearance surface cut the new old panel by overlap take care not to cut the reinforcement inside the body remove the new panel and cut the excess old panel section by section mark the punch position for welding the plug hole treatment and corrosion prevention before welding new body part grind the location for welding and sealant application to remove the paint film grind the location for welding and sealant application to remove the old paint film burr and rust from the welding joint but do not grind the cross section of the joint clean the location for welding and sealant application on the new part and body and wait for to volatilise the detergent paint zinc spray to the welding junction of the new part and body that doe not need sealant and control the thickness of the zinc spray apply structure sealant before application remove and discard the sealant to ensure uniform bond of the panel welding shall be finished within the time specified by the structure sealant or bond of the panel will be affected final position and welding of the panel adjusting panel refit confirm the position of the ridge compare the hole body repair collision repair adjust surrounding clearance adjust the difference between the high surface and the low surface position of gas shielded welding select gas ratio of argon to ensure the accuracy of the position positioned welding shall be performed on both side of the ridge instead of on the ridge and the distance of the positioned welding shall be above trial welding before spot welding select an electric welding arm for resistance spot welding and shape the electrode head with nozzle finisher finish the nozzle making its diameter equal to time the welding thickness and plus another caution use arm not exceeding mm in length fit the resistance spot welding arm and test equipment with test silicon slice so as to meet the operation requirement if there is no monitoring device the weld strength can be tested by checking welding pool pull wire around the metal under tensile force during the stretch welding sequence welding sequence to decrease the possibility of strength reduction due to welding shunt it is recommended that you begin with the direct welding and follow the sequence shown below the distance between the welding spot shall be collision repair body repair welding requirement if the resistance spot welding has been used in production new resistance spot welding must be done at any other feasible position during spot welding to ensure the quality of the welding test coupon of the same metal gauge and material are recommended for peeling test if condition permit the welding quality monitor can be used to ensure welding quality if the satisfactory spot welding can not be achieved plug weld welding must be used length of the welding rod holder of hand spot welding gun must be no more than single sided spot welding is not allowed neither braze welding nor gas welding is allowed unless it is specified in production gas shielded welding plug welding when welding the region with layer or more than layer of panel or the welding nozzle is close to the restricted area and resistance spot welding is impossible use the hole made by the cutter for spot welding or punch or drill hole to perform the mag plug welding the diameter of the hole for plug welding of the structural component is and that for plug welding of the covering component is the ratio of the welding spot for plug welding to the manufacturer welding spot is gas shielded welding butt welding treatment after welding process for corrosion prevention use disc polisher or belt polisher to grind or finish all welding location and clean the location paint anti corrosion primer which can prevent corrosion and improve the adhesion of the sealant apply sealant fill the seam between the panel to realise the leak prevention purpose position ofofof sealant deliver the vehicle to the painter for painting apply wax to the cavity before fitting the accessory information ofofof cavity wax application body repair painting painting overview the purpose of painting is to provide an excellent appearance decoration gloss protection colour protection corrosion resistance and longer service life so it has distinctive feature painting is high level protective painting the coating shall have superb corrosion resistance weather resistance acid alkali resistance inclusion resistance and an extensive adaptability etc painting mainly refer to body painting is high level decorative painting and careful design good working environment and condition are required for providing the coating with good decoration vehicle decoration mainly rely on painting the quality of the painting has direct influence on the commercial value of the vehicle painting is the most typical industrial painting it require reasonable and effective pretreatment method drying method and process preparation painting is multi coating painting system and the painting quality and process control of each coating is of great importance coating introduction coating composition duringmanufacture resistant coating zinc coating of the raw material zinc phosphate coating electrocoating intermediate coating and top coat aftermarket paint repair is composed of the primer coat putty coat intermediatecoating coating function of each coating zinc coating with good function of anti corrosion anti rust good paint adhesion decorative performance and forming property zinc phosphate coating the first paint that is directly applied to the treated workpiece surface and is the base for the whole coating its characteristic are as follow it shall have good adhesion to the treated surface of the workpiece and the primer film formed shall have an excellent mechanical strength it shall serve as corrosion inhibitor and the primer coating shall have an excellent corrosion resistance water resistance and chemical resistance it shall well fit the intermediate coating or top coat it shall have good workability electrocoating enhance cavity corrosion resistance improve the uniformity and leveling of the paint film thickness raise the quality of the paint film painting efficiency and paint utilization efficiency putty coat used for the object surface with primer precoat so as to fill the pit weld seam scratch rust hole etc on the object surface until the surface become smooth intermediate coating it is the last intermediate coat before applying the top coat its binder content is between the primer and the top coat and the paint film is bright and semi bright it provide the following function enhance the adhesion between the primer and the top coat increase the total thickness of the coating and improve the film build improve the corrosion resistance and weather resistance of the primer top coat repair minor defect on the substrate surface raise the decoration performance of the top coat painting body repair top coat top coat is used for the final coating of the multi coating it directly affect the decoration performance weather resistance gloss protection colour protection chemical resistance contaminant resistance and appearance of the vehicle classification of top coat the classification of the top coat by colour effect is as follow pure pigmented paint containing resin pigment and additive only metallic paint containing metal powder with better gloss and saturation pear paint comparing with the metallic paint instead of using metallic paint it use mica powder coated with titanium dioxide and ferrum dioxide to improve the texture and gloss of the paint film by application procedure it can be classified into the following process single process system that use one type of paint to form complete surface coating single process pure pigmented paint and metallic paint are included double process paint to form complete surface coating generally the pigmented paint is applied before the finishing varnish is painted double process pure pigmented paint metallic paint and pearl paint are included three process usually layer of primer is required for thenalayerofpearlpaint andfinallyalayerof finishing varnish the combination of these three coating form complete surface coating three process pearl paint and coloured varnish are included principle of paint repair the following principle must be followed when repairing the painted surface use the certified jointing sealant to seal the inner and outer jointing repair all damaged part of the sealant under the vehicle body use two pack paint to repair the system and it shall be consistent with the original production part apply the inner wax to the entire inner surface without paint finishing steam cleaner or high pressure cleaner must be used to clean the vehicle thoroughly before paint repair clean the local part needing requirement with warm and water soluble cleaning solution then thoroughly clean it with solvent painting must be done immediately after this operation in order to thoroughly clean the area of exposed metal due to damage of the painted surface the cleaning area shall be larger than the original damaged area use phosphate to eliminate the dust contaminant etc on the exposed metal and offer good repair environment for the new coating body repair painting painting process generally metal treatment before painting also known as pretreatment application process surface pretreatment surface pretreatment consist of the following work surface cleaning before painting thoroughly remove the mud dirt other foreign material on the vehicle especially the dirt on the door frame luggage compartment engine cover seam and wheel house failure to do so may cause the new paint film to be contaminated treatment of dissolved substance apply degreaser to the surface with the special cloth then remove the degreaser and grease residue etc with clean and dry cloth treatment of moisture and dust use clean and dry cloth toremovemoistureanddust pressure air gun coating identification and damage assessment coating identification identification of the coating on the body panel is crucial for the reapplication process improper identification may lead to serious problem for top coat application if no respray is performed the type of the coating can be determined by the vehicle manual but the type of the coating for the resprayed body shall be determined for repair damage assessment correct assessment of the damage is one of the key factor for determining the service cost and ensuring the quality of painting range of the repair treatment process transitional region location to be covered for protection and part to be removed etc are determined by correct assessment of the damage this lay the foundation for correct implementation of the subsequent process and guarantee of satisfactory repair quality assessment of the damage to the paint film include visual inspection touch and ruler removing old paint film clean the body and check the body paint carefully to see if there is any sign of damage such as bubble crack peeling rust and partial damage caused by baking repair gas welding etc for the above damage the old paint film shall be removed selection of partial elimination or full elimination of the old paint film is made according to the degree of the damage and the quality requirement after recoating sandpaper is recommended for grinding feather edge not less than precaution for mechanical grinding do not use an electric grinding machine to grind surface with big radian highly raised surface or concave surface in cross way do not use an electric grinding machine to grind the corner wrinkle seam weld junction or location with plastic sealant for grinding the grinding machine can be started only when it contact the surface of the sheet metal in this process ordeepgroove may occur and the grinding machine shall be aligned with the edge line to be ground before of the machine to avoid thermal deformation of the sheet metal do not stay the grinding machine at one position for prolonged time do not allow coarse grinding material to contact with the intact paint surface of the accessory in the grinding area it better to protect the intact coating with tape operation of sanding fine processing of the surface treated by coarse grinding its purpose is to acquire flatter surface align the front of the rotating grinding wheel the surface to be ground and keep the rear away from the surface maintain this angle move the painting body repair grinding machine up and down the proper coverage area of each grinding mark is replacing sheet metal use sandpaper to grind the electrophoretic primer by dry grinding degreasing and descaling of the metal surface remove surface rust descaling of the metal include manual descaling polishing grinding and high pressure water etc mechanical method chemical method and ultrasonic method different rust apply different method for descaling clean the metal surface again body repair painting coating application for paint repair of the pretreated sheet metal of the finished surface please refer to the detailed process below primer application cover the area around the region to be repaired use soft cloth with detergent to wipe the surface to be painted and use another clean cloth to wipe off the detergent residue on the surface purge the surface with compressed air spray anti rust primer and dry it putty application not required for new part mix the putty and curing agent according to the ratio specified by the instruction use clean putty mixing plate only mix putty enough for current use fill sandpaper mark and pore first then apply putty in the full scale flash off for after application and bake it with the infrared baking lamp according to the instruction of the putty wear dust mask select appropriate sandpaper and hard grinding block rail grinding machine can be used and take care not to grind the surrounding area when using grinding block grind in the horizontal direction and begin with the top then move down gradually clean the repair surface with an air gun use soft cloth with detergent to wipe and dry with another clean cloth intermediate coat application cover the area around the repair region prepare to apply intermediate coat the surrounding of the covered area shall be larger than the area to be sprayed above use soft cloth with detergent to wipe the surface to be painted and use another clean cloth to wipe off the detergent residue on the surface purge the surface with compressed air mix intermediate coat curing agent and diluent according to the ratio specified by the instruction apply thin film of intermediate coat first flash off for and make two wet application after levelling of cure the paint film with an infrared baking lamp coat grinding indicator layer on the surface so that the uneven surface can be easily found during grinding painting body repair perform coarse grinding with sandpaper first then perform fine grinding with sandpaper remove the covering paper from the surrounding of the repair area purge the inner outer surface of the repair area with compressed air and thoroughly remove the abrasive debris and moisture top coat application check the colour code and check the colour formulation according to the code polish the body surface around the repair area to acquire correct colour check for the difference between the colour mixed and that of the original body in the stage fluorescent lamp or sunlight when it is consistent with the vehicle to be painted dilution ratio distance pressure paint output temperature flash off time etc paint the colour test panel flash off the test panel for bake the test panel as per the specified temperature and time take care not to overheat the test panel during baking use soft cloth with detergent to wipe the area to be repaired and dry with another clean cloth pastepaperfrom the inside of the repair area and take care not to tear the covering paper purge the panel to be painted and the vehicle with compressed air take care not to damage the covering paper then wipe the surface with cloth to prevent dust particle etc from entering into the paint use strainer to pour the mixed paint into the cup of the spray gun carry out trial application check the barometric pressure paint output sector and atomization spray two wet paste coating the leveling of each shall be for silver paint virtual spraying can be carried out at the last to guarantee the silver powder is uniform varnish application after the pigmented paint is applied flash off for about when the paste coating lose its gloss completely apply varnish spray wet varnish first flash off for spray the second wet varnish flash off for before baking note do not turn off the ventilation system of the spray booth during drying use the temperature and duration specified by the product to bake the paint film infrared baking lamp can also be used to perform forced drying for afterbaking paint film is completely cool such as grinding polishing etc polishing waxing when the paint film is completely cool use sandpaper or to grind the dust sag etc on the paint film use slack wax and wool wheel to grind the location to be polished at the beginning of polishing the speed of the polisher shall not be high the speed shall be increased gradually polish with polishing wax and sponge wheel use clean sponge wheel use polishing wax to remove the swirl mark caused by polishing to restore the gloss of the paint film check if the colour of the painted location is correct in the lamplight then check it in the sunlight repair general film coating defect paint film defect caused by application and treatment particle the phenomenon that bump in the film coating are spread over the whole or local surface in granular form analysis of possible cause poor air cleanliness and impurity in the painting environment such as dust worker wear dirty clothe or the fiber contained in the material are easy to fall out substrate that are not properly handled have bump the vehicle body is not cleaned thoroughly etc the painting pressure is insufficient atomization is poor the spray gun is not cleaned up and an improper screen is selected the pigment or flashing material in the paint are badly dispersed the paint are unfiltered or filtered improperly the paint become deteriorated and show abnormal sign such as separate out flocculation the film is moved out of the baking booth before drying the surface to dust free preventive measure ensure that the spraying environment is clean ensure that the object to be painted and worker are cleaned thoroughly before entering the baking booth ensure the paint performance the deteriorated and poorly dispersed paint are not applicable set the optimal parameter for paint application and use the spray gun to spray after the paint being filtered defect treatment the tiny dirty spot can be polished after removed by abrasive paper if the impurity particle are large or the dirty spot on the surface of primer and even surfacer can only be ground to the extent that the spot disappear completely perform the spraying again sag upon the liquid paint is sprayed onto the workpiece surface some wet film surface run down creating unevenly stripe and sag which are thin on the top and thick on the bottom resulting in the local thicker film on vertical surface it can be divided into sinking sagging and running depending on the shape of sag analysis of possible cause the painted workpiece surface is too cold low application environment temperature slow wind speed and high solvent vapor content in the ambient air low product temperature low spraying pressure slow gunning speed short distance to spray gun thicker film large caliber of spray gun poor atomization of spray gun slow solvent evaporation rate and low paint viscosity short distance between spray gun and workpiece slower gunning speed thicker spraying coating insufficient flash off interval between layer preventive measure improve the painting environment control the appropriate ambient temperature humidity and wind speed choose the correct solvent and pay attention to select the dissolving capacity and evaporation rate of the solvent strictly control the viscosity and the temperature for coating application andincrease the gun distance and gunning speed full flash off between layer defect treatment for the local varnish sagging it can be sanded to eliminate the sagging defect before being polished if this defect exist or if the sagging defect is more severe or it appear on the surfacer layer and the color paint layer finally revealed on the layer of varnish the sagging must be sanded thoroughly and make the spraying again pin hole eye small needle hole on the top of them which are referred to as pin hole the solvent contained in the film coating evaporate rapidly during the surface drying its left trace form hole these pin hole are known as defect that the hole are still on the substrate are not filled up they are easily generated at the corner of workpiece analysis of possible cause pin hole the paint are mixed with impurity for example water enter into the solvent based coating the painted object have dirt on their surface the flash off interval between layer is insufficient during application the continuous spraying is too thick the temperature rise up suddenly and the surface is dried rapidly when stoving pin eye the uneven location on the original workpiece surface are not filled up precaution and improvement set appropriate temperature and humidity for application wind speed and other condition body repair painting mix the paint and solvent in proportion to ensure the proper fluidness it should be heated up slowly to avoid the rapid solvent evaporation full flash off interval between layer defect treatment the defect should be completely ground to remove the pin hole and pin eye then repaint it blistering part of the film coating float off the painted surface or prime coat and is filled with liquid or gas which is about in diameter or float off in large piece if there is some water under the film the vapor generated by evaporation of water will plump up the film to form blister when the temperature rise up analysis of possible cause the ambient humidity is high and the film is dried naturally after spraying resulting in moisture infiltrating into the film the substrate is not dried properly the drying time is short between coating the heating and drying are too fast grind the putty while adding water and the putty absorb water there is oil or water in the compressed air line preventive measure grind the dry putty ensure that the previous coating has met the requirement for cleanness and dryness before spraying reduce the humidity in the painting and spraying room use the curing agent and diluent suitable for environment temperature make it dry as soon as possible once the spraying is completed to prevent the moisture from infiltrating into the film defect treatment once blister appear on the film surface this surface should be ground to remove the blistering defect completely then respray it depending on the surface condition orange skin the orange skin is used to describe the leveling property of the film when you observe the film surface against the light you can see different ripple of presenting on the film surface and the brightness and plumpness vary in different ripple area such texture on the film surface which is similar to orange peel is known as orange skin analysis of possible cause the substrate is not flat or the finish coating is sprayed to the surfacer surface without having the orange skin ground smooth the ambient temperature is high the wind speed is fast and the substrate temperature is high the diluent volatiliz rapidly the paint has high viscosity and the film is too thin the distance from spray gun is too far the gunning speed is too fast the spraying pressure is low and the amount of paint out of spray gun is little preventive measure grind the intermediate coating completely smooth according to the process requirement control the ambient temperature and wind speed use the diluent suitable for that temperature and adjust the paint viscosity as per the paint manufacturer product instruction adjust the gun pressure then regulate the gun to proper distance and reduce the gunning speed as per the manufacturer instruction spray the appropriate layer to ensure that the film get the desired thickness defect treatment only perform the grinding and polishing if the defect of orange skin is slight the orange skin should be ground to completely removed and then be resprayed if the defect is serious sanding scratch the sanding scratch left by sanding substrate are visible on the film after spraying the paint sometime the sanding scratch on the substrate will extend due to the erosion caused by solvent contained in the film the upper film would be subsided gradually after period resulting in the sanding scratch more and more obvious analysis of possible cause if the improper abrasive paper are used to sand the substrate the sanding scratch will be wide and unable to be covered and filled up by upper film if the lower film is sanded and sprayed with upper film over it before it isn totally dry it is easy to make the sanding scratch to extend due to the erosion caused by solvent in the upper film and form the sanding scratch defect the diluent that has high solubility for upper paint or too slow to dry has been misused preventive measure painting body repair when you select the abrasive paper number to make sanding you should observe the principle that the abrasive paper number should not be jumped by level each replacement ensure that the lower film is totally dry before sanding select the correct upper film solvent as per the product instruction defect treatment for slight sanding scratch they can be removed or made less obvious by sanding and polishing as they have been filled up by paint for the sanding scratch that are more obvious they cannot be removed by sanding and polishing it should be resprayed after removing the scratch putty mark putty mark mean the defect that trace is left along the edge of putty application area after spraying the surfacer or finish analysis of possible cause the feather edge are improperly treated before putty application the putty is mixed with curing agent in improper proportion the next action is taken before the putty is completely dry sanding spraying the surfacer etc the putty is not flat enough the feather edge do not meet the standard and it is higher or lower than the old surrounding coating or metal the curing agent and diluent mixed in the surfacer or finish are dried slowly or the flash off interval between layer are too short when spraying the surfacer or finish resulting in the edge expanded and extended preventive measure the width of the feather edge must be and the surface is smooth without step before putty application adding the curing agent in proper proportion according to the product instruction make sure to perform the sanding and spray the surfacer when the putty is totally dry use the correct curing agent and diluent and ensure the flash off interval between layer is enough the periphery of putty must be sanded to be qualified not only the putty but also the extended old surrounding coating should be sanded when you sand the putty edge to allow the putty together with the surrounding to form flat surface defect treatment once the putty mark appear those area should be sanded and apply the putty again and respray the surfacer and finish lifting the lower layer paint is single component or even if it is two component it is not dry and cured completely including improper amount of curing agent is used when the paint is sprayed above it the solvent contained in upper film will erode the lower film causing the uplift and shrink present on the lower film surface analysis of possible cause the lower film is not totally dry the lower film become deteriorated the continuous spraying of surfacer or finish result in too thick surface or the used curing agent and diluent are too slow to dry or the diluent with high solubility is used or the flash off interval between layer are too short when spraying the surfacer or finish or the low air temperature during the spray cause the infiltration and erosion of solvent contained in the surfacer or finish resulting in the lifting occurred at the putty edge one component putty or surfacer edge preventive measure for the film not totally dry it must be sanded and sprayed with the next layer paint after it is completely dry if it cannot be totally dry due to the improper adding of curing agent it need to respray the paint mixed with proper curing agent after film removal for the deteriorated film shown as chalking cracking loss of gloss they must be removed totally and resprayed with two component surfacer or finish use the correct mating curing agent and diluent and ensure the flash off interval between layer is enough use the two component putty and surfacer defect treatment when the lifting defect appear it should be sanded to remove any defect at the lifting area if the lifting is severe the film that has lifting should also be sanded and removed completely then spray the two component surfacer and finish paint film defect caused by operating environment and treatment paint film defect caused by external corrosive material defect phenomenon the film defect occur when the film is affected by the acidic and alkaline material as well as industrial fall out such as asphalt oil battery electrolyte bird dropping sap acid rain pollen dust produced by steel plant and black carbon from boiler stack it generally appear body repair painting as uplift discolor and even cracking and peeling caused by the contaminant infiltrating into the film defect treatment if the film defect is tiny it can be recovered only by polishing the surface damage if the film has been discolored the defective area should be sanded until eliminating the defect then respray it water spot defect phenomenon when the rainwater on the film surface or the tap water left by car washing is heated to evaporate white ring drop mark will be left on the water drop area and cannot be removed by common cleaning method cause as the water contain mineral substance such as calcium when the water is heated to evaporate some warm water will infiltrate into the softened film with heat and the left mineral substance infiltrate into the coating defect prevention and treatment try not to wash the car under the sunlight and wipe the water drop off the vehicle surface after washing to avoid evaporation caused by heating it should only be polished when water spot appear stone crash damage defect phenomenon some small stone may hit the film and cause the film peeling during vehicle traveling the area where the film is peeled off usually form the surface with sharp and jagged edge sometime small indentation will appear in the middle of this area the front edge of the vehicle engine hood or the roof is usually damaged by stone defect treatment when the stone crash damage occur the film on the defective area must be removed and sanded to feather edge and filled up with putty or surfacer then continue to apply the surfacer and finish if the area hit by stone is rusted the rust must be removed thoroughly when new original part are sprayed if the finish is directly sprayed onto the electrophoretic coating without spraying surfacer and when the plastic part are sprayed if the surfacer and finish are not added with softening agent depending on the plastic hardness the stone crash protection for the film will be reduced and the film is easy to hit off by stone and sand discolor chalking loss of gloss defect phenomenon as the vehicle expose to the ultraviolet ray the pigment deterioration of the film cause color fading the resin deterioration cause yellowing the film peel off as powder and lose gloss defect treatment color fading is representation of film aging it is usually shown up together with chalking and full loss of gloss once any one of these defect occur the defective film must be sanded and removed thoroughly then respray the two component surfacer and finish seal body repair seal overview process area of underbody seal underbody area and doorsill outer panel should be processed with plastisol pvc underbody sealer this sealer shall not be reused when repairing the sealer for the underbody area the underbody sealer upon delivery should be removed and returned to the proper breakpoint expose the clean metal surface and the current sealing edge shall also fit firmly to the panel use new chassis sealant between the primer and the intermediate primer if necessary use the seam sealant before applying the chassis sealant before coating the chassis sealant fit the plug and insulating ring on the disk type floor except the part for wax spray refit the heat fusible plug damaged in repair with heat blower or replace them with rubber insulating ring before using new chassis sealant cover the suspension wheel tyre power unit exhaust system and brake system including all mounting point seam sealant for factory assembly apply pvc sealant treated by baking to special joint weld and sheet metal edge this kind of material is not suitable for repair so approved special sealant shall be used for aftersale service seam sealant is used after the primer and before the intermediate primer and outer coat the spray of the seam sealant must be smooth and continuous and the spray line depend on the type of the seam if the sealant is coated by brush pay special attention to the coverage area required for the weld at the place needing sealant formation use cloth dipped in the solvent oil to complete the required operation remember to seal all the seam to be processed after each repair the area away from the joggled joint often distort when the vehicle is damaged thus sealant for these place will frequently occur in the following repair and correction check all the weld around the area to be repaired to see whether it is damaged then process the damage as required then coat new sealant specific step are as follow clean the affected seam re process the exposed metal area with proper etch primer coat proper sealant at the required place process the affected area with acid etch primer chassis sealant is also acceptable and apply proper colour paint film after reassembly or refit of part if the seam is plugged then apply paste seam sealant to such seam blockage of the seam may occur after panel repair in this case apply sealant and paint before final assembly if the inlet is of proper size apply seam sealant to the two side of the joint to be repaired if the spray can be only done to one side such as compartment then use cavity wax to process the affected compartment beam seam which require sealant when assembling in the plant is described in the figure below test overview process of the leaking problem need to combine technique experience and instinct to take proper and effective measure do not come to conclusion fast only by naked eye test tool and instrument thebelow tool and instrument are recommended to use garden sprayer manual wet dry vacuum cleaner dry absorbent cloth battery torch mirror weatherstrip locating tool trim panel remover small wooden or plastic wedge dry compressed air supply hot blower sealer applicator ultrasonic leak detector when checking for leak at least the following part should be checked front inner space rear seat space storage compartment or luggage compartment test method and measure the operator at the fitting center can judge from where to check the leakage by information offered by the customer the next step is to find the leaking spot after the leaking place is founded for the first case an easy and effective method is the common garden sprayer nozzle of this extruding garden sprayer ought to be adjusted so that water can be sucked into the nozzle directly and spray out proper spray at the dark corner mirror or battery torch cab be used the test light using supply voltage is not available the test order is very important begin from the lowest point and go up gradually this can avoid blocking off the other leaking place when an area is being checked if the test is begun from the any water flowing into the pumping chamber may drop on the pedal by baffle insulating ring even though it may be judged wrongly that seal of the fail another part for leaking check is to visually observe whether the door hole sealer insulating ring or weather strip is damaged aged or displaced meanwhile align the door with the seal note check the colour of the water around the leak if the water is dirty the leak is from under the vehicle if the water is clean it is from above when leaking spot is found the method below are necessary for resolution of the leak problem replace all of the damaged displaced and aged door hole seal and weatherstrip check all the body seal and ensure it is fixed on the fixing flange fixing face correctly if necessary the simitar can be used blow the body weld seam to be processed with compressed air and or hot blower when necessary if necessary apply sealant to the outside of the connector to prevent water when correcting leakage of the windshield and weatherstrip or polish between the upper glass and the body directly try not to remove the glass use qualified material at proper place such as glass to the weatherstrip or glass to the body initial door seal replacement the initial seal for both front and rear door are stuck on the specified position alway comply with the regulation below when replacing the weatherstrip clean the position where the door weatherstrip is located do not clean the door with paint diluent agent the ideal temperature for sticking the weatherstrip is for either the door or the weatherstrip when the temperature is less than it is hard to stick the weatherstrip do not apply too much force to stick the weatherstrip this will make disassembly or re installation much easier if the weatherstrip shall be refitted do not touch the contact face or stain it body repair seal sealant position information body seal identification between panel connected with bolt riveting joint type panel edge connected with bolt
