information body repair corrosion protection corrosion protection overview treatment in production during production the vehicle body apply the following method for anti corrosion treatment use galvanized steel sheet phosphate anti corrosion primer dip coating electrophoresis chip resistant coating underbody coating spray on the underbody of the main floor and rear floor and on the rear wheel opening cavity wax spray on the sheet metal part such as doorsill and rear window sill side extension and the lower sheet metal of the door protective wax spray on the opening not covered by the wheel house whenever repairing the body make sure the anti corrosion material from the repair area is also repaired or updated ensure the material used are the approved anti corrosion material precaution for body repair and operation in the workshop it is necessary to exercise caution in the operation of the vehicle for example when lifting the vehicle any carelessness and negligence can cause damage to the underbody sealant seam sealing material underbody sealing wax and body panel etc when fitting the accessory ensure that the vehicle anti corrosion protection is not impaired by broken protective layer or moisture do not screw the self tapping screw directly into the body panel pre fix the matching plastic insert to the body panel alway brush the sheet metal chassis component and drill hole edge of other body part with proper zinc rich or acid etch primer and brush the surrounding area with wax never contact the painted metal surface of the part with the body without taking appropriate protection measure between the metal surface requiring bolt connection be sure to have appropriate media such as weldable zinc rich primer or zinc strip etc the heat from the steam cleaning equipment has the risk of damaging some decorative part softening or liquefying certain adhesive and anti corrosion material so the equipment shall be adjusted so that the nozzle temperature doe not exceed do not have the steam nozzle stay in one place for long period of time the distance between the nozzle and the sheet metal surface shall be at least above during service do not remove the protective wax or paint from the underbody or lower part of the engine cover if the above area shall be cleaned with steam then apply new layer of protective wax or an underbody protection layer as soon as possible maintenance check when doing the anti corrosion check on the body observe the following step lift the vehicle visually inspect the underbody sealant for any damage lower the vehicle check the body exterior paint for damage and the body panel for corrosion note if the vehicle is dirty it will need to be washed prior to inspection of bodywork the above check refer only to visual check when checking for corrosion and paint damage do not remove the decorative panel or sound insulation material lift the vehicle and use the test lamp or spotlight to perform visual check for the following corrosion damage and paint damage and condition of the lower panel of the front rear vehicle doorsill and the underbody sealant at the wheel opening damage to underbody sealant erosion condition in surrounding area of the suspension mounting point and fuel tank mounting point note for unexposed metal the little bubble on the underbody sealing material can be neglected pay special attention to damage of the sheet metal or anti corrosion material due to incorrect lifting method warning it is essential to follow the correct jacking and lifting procedure after lowering the vehicle visually check all the visible painted surface for sign of damage and corrosion and pay special attention to the following area leading edge of the engine cover visible flanging of the engine compartment underbody and door panel take feasible measure to correct or repair any body damage or corrosion found during the inspection as soon as possible these include diminishing breakage and ensuring the long term effectiveness of the anti corrosion treatment performed in the production if the corroded area is increasingly clear and diffuse or expand from the bottom of the replaceable part such as various trim panel window glass seat etc then replace the part to achieve the most effective repair corrosion protection body repair underbody protective maintenance whenever doing maintenance work of the body ensure that the body sealing and anti corrosion protection is recovered to the original condition this apply not only to the repair work on damaged area but also to the recovery work on the protective layer of certain area indirectly weakened due to accident or maintenance operation before straightening or doing panel repair you must remove the corrosion protection layer of the damaged area it particularly important especially for the panel with protective wax layer the underbody with polyvinyl chloride pvc sealing material and the sound barrier area warning do not use oxy acetylene gas equipment to remove corrosion prevention material large volume of fume and gase are liberated by these material when they burn note the device device device used used used for removing removing removing strong strong strong anti corrosion anti corrosion anti corrosion sealant sealant sealant can provide provide provide several several several speed speed speed and tototo remove remove remove the sealing sealing sealing material material material the most common way is to remove the sealing material by using hot air dryer integrated with blade one of the most effective way is to use hot knife with fast cutting characteristic this tool use wide blade that can easily access the outline area where it is difficult to reach in some other way when doing the repair on the underbody sealant follow the following step remove the existing underbody coating after the sheet metal are repaired use solvent to clean the affected area and use the etching phosphate material to process bare metal apply new primer to the affected area caution do not under any circumstance apply underbody sealer directly to bare metal surface replace all damaged heat fusible plug if this type of plug doe not use such rubber grommet of appropriate size ensure this type of plug be implanted to the sealant after the mechanical part hose and pipe clip on the underbody are removed cover all the mounting surface the underbody sealant must be sprayed before the above component are refitted brush all exposed seam with sealant use approved underbody sealant for service to spray the affected area remove all the covering on the mounting surface of the component after refitting the above mechanical part including hose pipe and other clip cover the brake disc spray layer of approved underbody protective wax after repairing the sheet metal at the damaged wheel opening spray new wax which is also applicable for the painted surface and underbody sealant remove the old wax on the underbody and the range of the underbody wax is at least larger than the range of the new underbody sealant note where repair include the application of finish paint coat in the area requiring underbody wax carry out paint operation before applying wax cavity wax injection after repair be sure to use approved cavity wax for treatment in addition for all inner surface damaged by the repair whether these area have been treated in the production process they shall be subject to cavity wax treatment this include all the cavity and the door interior if necessary allow additional hole to be made for wax injection but these hole can not be located in the load bearing part make sure that these hole are treated by using the proper zinc rich primer and brushed with protective wax and finally sealed with the rubber ring before injecting the wax make sure the cavity to be treated is free of contaminant and foreign material if necessary use compressed air to remove the debris in the cavity alway ensure cavity wax injection is performed after the final finish and before re assembling other component during the spray process ensure that the cavity wax cover all the flange and seam and be fully distributed to the repair area of all new and existing sheet metal it is noteworthy that there is no wax injection process when assembling new sheet metal and complete body shell such operation shall be performed after repair it is quite essential to take efficient cavity wax protective measure alway observe the following point before wax injection ensure the paint repair work is completed if necessary clean the body panel area and blow the cavity prior to the operation during wax injection and drying the temperature should be at check the pattern of the injection equipment body repair corrosion protection cover all area where no protective layer is needed and the area that may be contaminated due to overspray in case of possible contamination remove the body device such as seat belt retractor before dealing with the internal structure of the door lift the door glass up to the completely closed position before refitting the component spray wax on the body area which are usually covered by trim after the wax layer is completely dry check whether the drain hole on the body and door is clean keep the equipment clean and tidy especially the wax nozzle corrosion protection body repair anti corrosion product and construction equipment and supplier cavity waxing machine and technology air inlet flow control adjust spray mode pressure cup max pressure psi bar kg cm spray gun connector nozzle connection of spray gun pipe retractable spray gun pipe rigid hook rod forward conical spray movable nylon spray gun pipe mm spray rigid spray gun pipe mm spray for repair if the waxing area is scratched handle it again compressed air spray gun with the integral pressure cup and replaceable spray gun pipe shall be used according to the configuration of the add on device please strictly observe the following in use when handling the film area use the rigid or movable spray gun to give spray to ensure maximum coverage for restricted opening use hook nozzle to provide more directional spray narrow inside or short compartment wax is directly sprayed to the bottom surface through the smaller spray pipe of the spray gun disconnect the liquid mm rigid spray gun the nozzle of the rigid spray gun give forward conical spray of circumference though the wax film is sprayed by single stroke it can affect all surface of the compartment internal and external spray can provide effective and complete wax coverage for the long straight structure and compartment by visual inspection the rigid spray pipe also provide position accuracy for handling the shapely location caution do not force the lance into access hole when using this attachment mm movable nylon spray gun pipe the elastic nylon spray gun share the same type with the rigid spray gun but it provide stronger penetrability it can be used to spray the inaccessible vessel of the curved section or nozzle the main disadvantage of the elastic nylon spray gun is that it cannot precisely position the internal compartment when spraying spray outward by the stroke of the spray gun when pulling the spray gun out retract it slowly and ensure the integrity of the finished spray do not pull the spray gun out with rush ensure the nylon pipe of the spray gun is away from the edge and located in the hole so that wear of the nylon pipe can be avoided and the service life of the nylon pipe can be prolonged ensure the spray operation is stopped before removing the nozzle from the port to assist in the completion of this step use red paint to mark the rear of the nozzle mm hook nozzle of movable spray gun the spray gun with rigid hook nozzle provide forward spray its conical spray is characterised by long range and good dispersion the combination of these two feature provide good capability of orientation and good effect on the short and narrow location to be sprayed beside it can also be used for direct spray of the inner wheel arc etc when using place the smooth area at the end of the spray gun to form an angle of with the location to be sprayed for location hidden in box or hole this help to finish the spray successfully for general spray arch the nozzle and move from one end to the other as required and ensure complete spray of the surface note alway clean gun after use with appropriate solvent to maintain efficiency recommended equipment schutz pistol spray gun for body the pistol spray gun is constructed of the housing and machined light alloy which is fitted by bolt to the schutz container pneumatic cartridge pistol spray gun the direct feed pneumatic gun is used for the cartridge product it has good performance in smooth spray and an adjusting valve for additional control is provided pneumatic pistol spray gun direct feed pneumatic gun are used for the product corrosion protection door and luggage compartment lid spray cavity wax from the process hole inside the door and luggage compartment lid to cover the internal sheet metal and frame with layer of protective film most of the cavity wax deposit is at the bottom of the sheet metal cavity and it will be finally drained from the hole at the bottom corrosion protection body repair body repair corrosion protection chassis adhesive function of chassis adhesive improve the stone chipping resistance of the underbody improve the corrosion resistance of the underbody sheet metal improve the seam sealing performance of the underbody sheet metal improve the sound insulation performance of the underbody caution ensure that suspension unit wheel tyre power unit exhaust and brake including all mounting point are shielded prior to application of fresh underbody sealer operation procedure for chassis adhesive cleaning to ensure the maximum viscosity of the product the underbody surface shall be clean and free from any dirt oil stain grease or wax residue inspection before operation thoroughly inspect the underbody for any potential damage or rust key location wheel arch lower side seam and overlap joint dust elimination eliminate the rust spot on the underbody with shovel or wire brush pay particular attention to the inaccessible location for example wheel frame overlap or seam check the type of the underbody coating check the type of the underbody coating and select suitable product read use instruction before operation please carefully read the instruction on the package of the product and view the technical data sheet read the safety instruction in the material safety data sheet carefully so that you can minimize some potential risk and hazard and achieve the optimum effect use cross spray technique spray the rust prevention product at distance of around spray in the horizontal direction first then spray the stiffener of the base plate from proper angle cross spray technique to achieve an optimum thickness of the wet film at least three spray shall be done drying time specific drying time shall be defined the coating material shall adhere to the underbody firmly and permanently different solution and different coating thickness may require different drying time product with high volatile solution require drying time at least clean spray gun use spray gun detergent to clean the spray gun immediately after use welding process body repair welding process welding process overview characteristic of welding process it is applicable to any shape and can be used to connect the integral body and the vehicle body integrity can be maintained after welding mass reduction can be achieved due to lack of the connecting part good sealing performance for air and water high production efficiency the welding connector strength is affected by the skill level of the operator excessive heating may cause surrounding panel deformation for modern vehicle the body welding repair usually adopt mag shielded arc welding resistance spot welding and soldering for the body panel welding repair an optimum welding method that will not reduce the original strength and durability of the body shall be applied depending on the specific situation spot welding or inert gas welding is preferred mag shielded arc welding mag shielded arc welding is mainly used for welding high strength low alloy steel body and aluminum casting etc advantage of the mag shielded arc welding include easy operation this method can melt the base metal completely low current can be used to weld thin metal which can diminish damage to the adjacent area strength stable and controllable arc suitable for welding location with gap and misalignment temperature and duration of the welding is controllable short heating time of the welding zone reduce fatigue and deformation of the base metal typical welding location and method butt welding fit two adjacent metal edge together fit mutually along the two metal panel or weld the butted edge overlap welding weld two overlapping metal panel plug welding punch hole on one or several exterior workpiece arc pass through the hole before entering into the interior workpiece and the hole is filled with the melting metal spot welding when the wire feed timing pulse is triggered current will be introduced into the two metal panel to be welded resistance spot welding the resistance spot welding is the most common method for welding the integral body this method can be used for the non deformable thin steel sheet on the body which require high welding strength suitable for roof window doorsill and external component etc the quality of the resistance spot welding depend on the voltage current pressure time and thickness of the metal panel to be welded etc the detail are as follow material and cleanness of the weldment the location between the weldment or the contact point between the weldment and the electrode shall be clean electrode pressing force insufficient force can cause spatter on the contact point excessive force can generate large current but it will lead to small welding spot diameter and penetration due to increase of the heat distribution zone welding current the welding spot diameter and welding strength increase with the current and large current and small pressure may also lead to spatter between the panel the spatter will be otherwise minimized welding time long welding time will cause much heat large diameter and deep penetration of the welding spot however prolonged welding time will lead to electrode impress and thermal deformation rather than increase of the welding spot arrangement of the welding spot the gap and margin of the welding spot distance between the spot and the panel edge is also crucial for determining the welding spot strength welding spot gap decrease can improve the weldment connection strength but the effect is actually limited excessive gap may cause the welding current to transfer through the last welding spot or leak then the added welding spot will not strengthen the connection strength of the weldment any more and it will have an adverse impact therefore the gap of the welding spot shall be beyond the area of current leak correct welding sequence shall be applied for the resistance spot welding continuous welding in one direction is generally not allowed when the electrode tip is hot and discolour stop welding and allow it to cool down to avoid crack due to stress concentration do not weld the boundary of the corner body repair welding process welding protection measure electronic control unit before welding repair is operated in consideration of the electronic control unit ecu refitted on the vehicle the following protection measure must be followed the plentiful heat and sharp vibration produced during these operation may damage the electronic control unit when disconnect or remove the airbag control module sdm pay special attention to the following proper protection measure protection measure for assistant protection system implement and remove or replace any item related to the assistant protection system srs be especially careful and comply with proper protection measure equipment when starting any test on the vehicle ensure that the related test equipment work normally and all harness and connector are in good state this is especially important to the electronic control unit seat belt rivet joint seat belt rivet joint is crucial for safety when repairing these area it is important to follow the following design specification note high strength low alloy steel hsla can be used at the installation position for seat belt try best to use the original production assembly including rivet joint of seat belt or the layout of cutting line should not interfere with the original rivet joint of seat belt carefully check the welding quality of the jointing for seat belt rivet joint within including the spot welding spacing welding process body repair welding part remove expose the resistance spot welding use rotary bowl grinder or wire brush installed into the air drill or manual wire brush for the spot welding is not obvious caution underbody coating using hot air gun prior to exposing spot weld the drill or clamping platoon welder for spot welding can be used to remove the welding spot location for laser welding use grinder and grinding disk mag arc welding location rivet junction body repair welding process big panel cutting cutting by edge of steel panel system control diagram vehicle access and safety control diagram hs can bus rf signal hard wire vehicle access safety and restraint description security system the function of the security system is to prevent illegal start of the vehicle when the ignition switch is turned to acc position the anti theft system can be disabled by the transmitter on the remote key this transmitter can be identified by the reader coil around the ignition switch and the coil transmit electric power and data to the remote key in frequency within certain distance away from the signal reader coil the security system include door switch luggage compartment switch bonnet switch security warning lamp hazard warning lamp button remote key ignition switch body control module bcm door switch each door lock assembly contain microswitch to notify the bcm of the door status when the door is open the microswitch contact will close to connect the ground circuit of the bcm when the door is closed the microswitch contact will disconnect and convert into an open circuit door switch bonnet and luggage compartment open switch are the main component of the alarm system when the alarm system work these switch will monitor the external intrusion into the vehicle the door open signal is also used by the bcm to indicate the door status information in the instrument pack information centre and control the interior lighting luggage compartment switch the luggage compartment lock motor contain microswitch used to notify the bcm of the luggage compartment lid status when the luggage compartment lid is closed the microswitch contact disconnect and the circuit of the bcm become an open circuit when the luggage compartment lid is open the switch will be closed and the ground circuit of the bcm will be connected canwhen the bus detect that the ground circuit is connected it will trigger the alarm the luggage compartment open microswitch is also used by the bcm to indicate the luggage compartment status information in the instrument pack information centre and automatically control the interior lighting safety and restraint vehicle access operation overview operation of vehicle access system the vehicle security system include the following three part peripheral system bonnet alarm if fitted door and luggage compartment lid open switch ignition switch central lock lock unlock switch acoustic alarm vehicle horn visible alarm hazard warning lamp the security system is divided into three independent zone and each zone include many discrete input signal see the table below when the security system is working any intrusion into these zone can trigger the alarm safety zone discrete signal front bonnet open switch middle inside driver door open switch front passenger door open switch left rear door open switch right rear door open switch driver door key unlock timed for wrong key is inserted into and placed in acc on position central lock lock unlock switch rear luggage compartment lid release switch complete anti theft status when the three zone of the security system are closed if lock request from the remote control or mechanical key is received the security system formally enter the complete anti theft status once in the complete anti theft status all activated safety zone will be monitored at all time when the switch of zone or is enabled if you attempt to lock the system from outside the vehicle will enter the partial lock status and give an error lock alarm bcmit will monitor the following outside lock request signal valid lock request from the remote key is received valid lock request from the driver door lock switch is received when the security system enter the complete anti theft status the bcm will allow the direction indicator lamp to give three flash complete anti theft status to partial anti theft status in the complete anti theft system if valid unlock request of the luggage compartment lid is received the bcm open the luggage compartment lid and the security system enter the partial anti theft status partial anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone only for example the bonnet is open zone and will enter the complete anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone only expect driver door for example door or more door are open zone and will enter the complete anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone only for example the luggage compartment lid is open zone and will enter the complete anti theft status partial anti theft status to complete anti theft status when each safety zone is in the security system the vehicle will enter the complete anti theft status for partial lock caused by error lock due to open door when the security system enter the anti theft status it can be disabled after being enabled within second and the alarm will not be triggered if the partial anti theft is caused by several zone when the relevant door cover of these zone are closed these zone will enter the complete anti theft status the entire security system enter the complete anti theft status only when all the zone enter the status error lock alarm with the door bonnet luggage compartment lid open and the ignition switch in the acc gear on gear or start gear or the engine running when using the mechanical key or remote key to send the lock request outside the vehicle the bcm will activate the horn to send an acoustic alarm appr to indicate error lock disable security system any of the following operation will cause the bcm to disable the security system valid unlock request from the remote key is received valid unlock request from the driver door switch is received valid emergency key is inserted into the ignition switch and switched from the acc gear to the on gear vehicle access safety and restraint caution ififif the driver driver driver door door door unlock unlock unlock switch switch switch isisis stuck stuck stuck ororor the switch switch switch circuit circuit circuit isisis short impact when the airbag control module sense an impact it will send an unlock request and then the door will unlock automatically except in the case of external locking auto relock after being unlocked with the remote key the vehicle will automatically return to the previous locked status if there is no operation within second in the auto relock status if the ignition switch is in acc position or valid key exist the current timing of the relock timer is cancelled and the auto relock function is cancelled auto lock if the driver door is closed and the vehicle is unlocked when the vehicle speed reach certain value preset the door will lock automatically auto unlock with the vehicle internally locked turn the ignition switch to the off position or pull out the key the vehicle will unlock automatically door lock controlled by internal central lock switch with the driver door closed and the impact signal disabled press the lock button of the central lock switch to lock all door with the vehicle door internally locked press the unlock button of the central lock switch to unlock all door when an impact occur and the impact signal is triggered the door unlock automatically the door cannot be relocked within current ignition cycle even if the lock button of the central lock switch is pressed when the vehicle door are externally locked the lock unlock button of the central lock switch do not work safety and restraint alarm system alarm system description and operation system layout lf anti theft coil alarm system safety and restraint system control diagram hs can bus rf signal hard wire safety and restraint alarm system description the vehicle is configured with an anti theft system connected with the ecm the characteristic value from the rf transceiver will be verified if the value match the corresponding data the engine can be started when the power is connected the key send the characteristic value to the lf anti theft coil and the serial data circuit to the bcm through the hard wire mounting position lf anti theft coil on the ign lock cylinder end view and pin information of lf anti theft coil harness connector pin no description immo transmission line immo transmission line alarm system safety and restraint operation the function of the anti theft system is provided by the bcm ecm and module which store and report environment identifier lf anti theft coil when the ignition key is inserted into the ign lock cylinder and the ignition switch is in on position by the lf anti theft coil of the ign lock cylinder then the rf transceiver transmit signal which contain its characteristic value bcm receive the signal and compare the value with that stored in the memory if the two value match the bcm send pre cancellation password to the ecm through the serial data circuit if the characteristic value of the rf transceiver is wrong the bcm send fuel disable password to the ecm when ecm receive the pre cancellation password from bcm the ecm will verify the password ecmthe ecm return the password verified to the bcm through the serial data circuit ecmthe ecm and bcm calculate the password if the result calculated by the bcm and ecm is consistent the ecm will allow the vehicle to start safety and restraint alarm system service procedure anti theft coil remove disconnect the battery negative remove the steering column trim cover hs can bus hard wire safety and restraint seat belt description front seat belt pretensioner in case of collision the front seat belt pretensioner tighten this ensure the occupant are securely kept in their seat the seat belt pretensioner unit is fixed by bolt to the seat belt retractor assembly in the pillar the seat belt pretensioner connector is pin connector connected with the body harness in case of collision the seat belt pretensioner is controlled by the signal from the srs ecu the retractor of the two pretensioner are positioned and work in the same way each pretensioner unit is provided with an initiator and propulsion generator which act on the rotor of the seat belt retractor warning once the pre tensioner has been operated it cannot be reset the gas generator will be exhausted the unit must be replaced seat belt the seat belt provide the most basic protection for all occupant the restraint system also provide further protection for the front occupant through the airbag module and the pretensioning seat belt the front seat are all fitted with appropriate retractor and three point belt the vehicle retractor is type of emergency locking retractor elr the retractor is provided with an additional lock mechanism which include webbing sensor and an sensor if the webbing is suddenly pulled the webbing sensor will immediately start the lock mechanism if the vehicle is suddenly slowed down or tilt at large angle the sensor will start the lock mechanism the retractor will lock in case of impact so the front seat belt retractor is equipped with load limiter if the load limit has exceeded the critical level the load limiter make the seat belt webbing loose so that the load acting on the occupant are reduced front seat belt the retractor of the front seat belt is fitted on the body in the cavity at the bottom of the pillar lower trim panel pull the belt webbing from the retractor and fix it to the mounting point under the pillar rear seat belt the retractor of the rear seat belt at both side is fixed to the reinforcement panel of the rear wheelhouse inner panel with bolt one end of the webbing is fixed to the upper end of the pillar the other end is fixed to the mounting point of the body floor the retractor of the central seat belt is fixed to the seat frame with bolt and the other end is fixed to the lower part of the rear seat the right rear seat belt buckle and the central seat belt buckle form an assembly which is fixed by one bolt between the right rear seat and the central rear seat the left rear seat belt buckle is located at the occupant right hip seat belt safety and restraint seat belt warning the front seat belt buckle are integrated with the seat belt not tiedwarningswitch through srs ecu the seat belt warning will be triggered if the seat belt is not tied firmly during driving the warning message comprise an acoustic alarm and warning lamp on the instrument panel to prevent unnecessary warning some vehicle specification specify that pressure sensor shall be fitted within the foam of the passenger seat when there is no passenger on the seat if there is any passenger on the seat but the pressure sensor is not activated then the seat belt warning will not be triggered even if the occupant doe not wear the seat belt safety and restraint seat belt operation overview when the remote key is inserted the restraint system is activated with the ignition switch in on position the srs warning lamp illuminate for second then goe out this indicate that the system is in normal condition side impact normal operation of the side seat airbag and head curtain airbag depend on whether the severity level of the side impact detected by the side impact sensor exceed the threshold for triggering the side impact sensor when the srs ecu receive trigger signal from the side impact sensor it will activate the side seat airbag and curtain airbag module the triggered gas generator can produce large quantity of gas to expand the airbag the expanded airbag will eject from the seat upholstery similarly if the head curtain airbag module is triggered above the door to protect the passenger from head injury the fully expanded airbag will release excess gas to reduce the risk of passenger injury warning lamp check when the ignition switch is turned on the srs warning lamp shall illuminate then check the lamp if no failure occur in the srs the warning lamp shall go out after about second to monitor the subsequent ignition cycle system check include the state of srs ecu and srs harness system continuity fault with the ignition switch on if any of the following fault occur in the system the srs warning lamp will illuminate and remain on during subsequent ignition cycle ecuand srs failure harness failure ground connection failure open circuit airbag module failure seat belt pretensioner failure if the system detect any failure during driving the warning lamp will also illuminate to indicate failure of the srs system when the warning lamp illuminate the srs will not work when any impact occur withtheignitionswitchon thediagnosisfunctionofthesr ecu will monitor the srs if any fault is detected the srs ecu will store related dtc in nonvolatile memory and output signal to illuminate the srs warning lamp the failure information in the memory can be read with the scan tool low voltage failure when the voltage supply is not within the specified range the malfunction indicator lamp will illuminate the dtc is stored in the memory intermittent failure the warning lamp will illuminate at the moment and remain on in subsequent driving cycle the warning lamp will not illuminate in the next ignition unless the failure occur again but the dtc is still stored in the srs ecu memory permanent failure for permanent failure the srs lamp will illuminate when the test is started and it will remain on in every subsequent ignition cycle until the cause of the failure is solved in addition the diagnosis system will also record the information of the failure occurred when any failure is detected the system will retain certain operating function if it is detected that the circuit of the srs outside the srs ecu is faulty srs ecu will still activate the airbag and seat belt pretensioner module when the srs ecu failure or power supply failure is detected the whole system will not work if the circuit of the srs warning lamp is faulty the warning lamp will not illuminate when the ignition switch is turned on for initial check of the warning lamp if there is no other fault in the system all function of the system are available in case of crash additional information provided by the scan tool for dlc include srs ecudtc version level of hardware and software impact mode state vehicle identification number vin data srs ecuthe srs ecu record fault information in the memory and the information can be read by connecting the scan tool to the dlc below the closure plate of the driver side instrument panel seat belt safety and restraint service procedure front seat belt assembly remove remove key from ignition switch disconnect battery lead earth first and wait minute for thesr back up power passenger airbag to facia bracket bolt airbag control module to body bolt side impact sensor to body supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint description and operation system layout airbag control diagram airbag warning lamp passenger side airbag body control modulebcm communication module driver seat airbag side impact sensor airbag control modulesdm steering wheel airbag gateway safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint system control diagram airbag control diagram hs can bus hard wire supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint description overview the srs can provide passive protection for the passenger when severe impact occur this system is usually based on application of the standard protection system seat belt the srs consist of the following component supplementary restraint system ecu srs ecu driver airbag module front passenger airbag module seat side airbag module if equipped seat belt pretensioner if equipped side impact sensor if equipped rotary coupler srs warning lamp on the ipk seat belt not tied warning lamp on the ipk srs ecuthe srs ecu connect the vehicle dlc through the hs can bus supplementary restraint system ecu srs ecu srs ecuit determine the affected range of the front impact side impact or rear impact through the internal and external impact sensor if equipped so as to control the srs srs ecuit is fitted on the central passage beneath the centre console which is secured with three screw srs ecuit is connected to the body harness through two connector srs ecuit monitor the vehicle impact through the internal accelerometer and the external sensor srs ecuit can identify the type and severity level of the impact according to these input data and ensure normal operation of the airbag if the severity of the front impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send signal to activate the front airbag module and seat belt pretensioner when fitting the side airbag two side impact sensor shall be fitted to monitor the side impact if the severity of the side impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send signal to activate the corresponding side airbag module and seat belt pretensioner if the severity of the rear impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send signal to activate the seat belt pretensioner caution it is important that the srs ecu is correctly mounted and is fitted in the designated location and orientation to ensure correct operation ecumonitoring after the vehicle is powered on the srs ecu monitor the preparation of the srs module after start it monitor the system continuously srs ecuthe srs ecu monitor the status of the following module srs ecuinternal accelerometer srs ecumicro processor side impact sensor if equipped driver side airbag module passenger side airbag module side airbag module if equipped seat belt pretensioner if equipped diagnosis count if any system failure is detected the srs warning lamp will illuminate to warn the driver the recommended diagnostic device can be used to check the srs malfunction the diagnostic information can be read include current malfunction frequency of malfunction and duration of each malfunction power supply and backup situation the power supply provide voltage for the srs ecu through special system fuse in the passenger compartment fuse box in case of power failure check the connection status of the passenger compartment fuse box and the fuse and harness of the srs ecu when the ignition switch is in on position the srs will be activated srs ecuthe srs ecu contain capacitor which can save electricity and ensure the corresponding trigger device and power supply is cut off by an impact side impact sensor for vehicle with side airbag module the side impact sensor is besidethebodysillframe the sensor is designed with mounting position for correct refit each sensor include an electronic circuit and sensing unit the side impact sensor send an acceleration signal to the safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint ecu and the ecu determine whether to trigger it through calculation caution the side impact crash sensor must be fitted in the correct orientation ensure the fixing screw do not damage the sensor or sensor harness when refitting the front seat driver airbag module airbag cover housing gas generator airbag connector the driver airbag module is fitted on the steering wheel by three bulged pin and one airbag module connector when fitting the driver airbag module push the airbag module toward the steering wheel firmly to embed the wire spring around the airbag module into the opening of the pin the electrical connection between the driver airbag module and the srs ecu is realized by the rotary coupler which is connected to the steering wheel by connector the driver airbag module is triggered by the control signal from the srs ecu folding airbag and gas generator are mounted in the module when severe front impact is detected by the srs ecu it will trigger the gas generator and expand the nylon bag rapidly front passenger airbag module warning after replacing the vehicle the passenger restraint system may not be capable of normal operation for hour warning if new airbag module show any sign of damage do not use airbag housing airbag cover airbag connector gas generator the front passenger airbag module is on the glove box of the instrument panel right before the front passenger seat the airbag module is fixed on the instrument panel with bolt the front passenger airbag module is triggered by the control signal from the srs ecu folding airbag and gas generator are mounted in the module when severe front impact is detected by the srs ecu it will trigger the gas generator and expand the nylon bag rapidly side airbag if equipped warning if new airbag module show any sign of damage do not use airbag housing airbag connector bracket the side airbag is outside the seat bracket below the seat upholstery it can protect the rib pelvis and upper visceral organ in case of side impact if the side impact is strong enough to deploy the side airbag the srs ecu will send control signal to trigger the side airbag the side airbag module connector is pin connector which is below the seat cushion and connected with the srs ecu through the body harness supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint caution do not try to remove the connector at the module end it is permanent connection safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint operation overview when the ignition switch is in on position the srs will be activated with the ignition switch in on position the srs warning lamp illuminate for several second then goe out this indicate that the system is in normal condition front impact if the front impact is severe and exceed the threshold set in thesrsecu seat belt will work when the accelerometer and front impact sensor in the srs ecu detect any impact the ecu will produce current to trigger the front airbag module the airbag the passenger side front airbag module is activated to expand the airbag when the front airbag is fully expanded it will release the gas immediately from the passage port and provide the passenger thisreducestherisk level of injury to the occupant srs ecuthe srs ecu can determine the type and severity level of the impact if the front impact detected by the sensor is severe the srs ecu will send an ignition signal to the front airbag module and seat belt pretensioner side impact normal operation of the side seat airbag and head curtain airbag depend on whether the severity level of the side impact detected by the side impact sensor and acceleration sensor in the srs ecu exceed the threshold for triggering the side impact sensor when the srs ecu detect severe side impact it will activate the side airbag curtain airbag module and seat belt pretensioner the triggered gas generator can produce large quantity of gas to expand the airbag the expanded airbag will eject from the seat upholstery similarly if the head curtain airbag module is triggered the airbag will eject from the interior trim panel above the door to protect the passenger from head injury the fully expanded airbag will release excess gas to reduce the risk of passenger injury front impact with angle when front impact with angle happen whether the airbag and seat belt pretensioner work depend on the speed and angle of the impact there are several condition the impact is below the threshold of the impact sensor in the srs ecu and no airbag or seat belt pretensioner is activated the speed and angle of impact exceed the triggering threshold of the front impact sensor and the front driver and passenger airbag and seat belt pretensioner are activated side airbag may also be triggered warning lamp check when the ignition switch is turned on the srs warning lamp shall illuminate then check the lamp if no failure occur in the srs the warning lamp shall go out after several second to monitor the subsequent ignition cycle system continuity fault with the ignition switch on if any of the following fault occur in the system the srs warning lamp will illuminate srs harness failure ground connection failure open circuit airbag module failure seat belt pretensioner failure if the system detect any failure during driving the warning lamp will also illuminate to indicate failure of the srs system when the warning lamp illuminate the srs may not work when any impact occur withtheignitionswitchon thediagnosisfunctionofthesr ecu will monitor the srs if any fault is detected the srs ecu will store related dtc in nonvolatile memory and output signal to illuminate the srs warning lamp the failure information in the memory can be read with the scan tool low voltage failure when the voltage supply is not within the specified range the malfunction indicator lamp will illuminate the dtc is stored in the memory intermittent failure when intermittent failure occur the warning lamp illuminate and it will be disabled after the failure is eliminated the warning lamp will not illuminate in the next ignition unless the failure occur again but the dtc is still stored in the srs ecu memory permanent failure for permanent failure the srs lamp will illuminate when the test is started and it will remain on in every subsequent ignition cycle until the cause of the failure is solved in addition the diagnosis system will also record the information of the failure occurred additional information provided by the scan tool for dlc include srs ecudtc version level of hardware and software vehicle identification number vin data supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint srs ecuthe srs ecu record fault information in the memory and the information can be read by connecting the scan tool to the dlc below the closure plate of the driver side instrument panel safety and restraint can bus hard wire park assistant system safety and restraint description rear obstacle detection if equipped during reversing if the vehicle has to go through obstruction on the road the pdc will send warning to the driver the system consist of the ultrasonic sensor fitted on the rear bumper and the instrument pack when the vehicle is in reverse gear the ultrasonic sensor is used to monitor the area around the rear bumper if any object is detected within the monitoring area the acoustic alarm device in the instrument pack will send an acoustic alarm the system is able to detect relatively hard solid object and object like wire fence if the rear camera is fitted the image of the rear vehicle will be displayed on the entertainment mainframe display during reversing rear ultrasonic sensor if equipped the ultrasonic sensor are fixed on the rear bumper all the sensor share the same structure with the color matching the vehicle the detection range of the rear ultrasonic sensor involve the area away from the back of the bumper the alarm frequency increase with the shortened distance between the object and the vehicle when the distance range from to when the object is away from the vehicle the alarm become consecutive alarm acoustic alarm the acoustic alarm in the instrument pack can inform the driver of the system status by acoustic alarm rear camera if equipped the rear camera is between the two rear license plate lamp under the tail gate which can provide the driver with real time image around the rear of the vehicle and help the reverse operation end view and pin information of harness connector end view and pin information of rear camera connector pin no description signal ground ground video signal camera power supply end view and pin information of right pdc sensor connector pin no description ground power supply safety and restraint park assistant system end view and pin information of middle pdc sensor connector pin no description ground power supply end view and pin information of left pdc sensor connector pin no description ground ground power supply park assistant system safety and restraint operation overview the system start to run when the ignition switch is in on position after the ultrasonic sensor receive the reverse gear signal it output signal to the acoustic warning device then the acoustic warning device will give an acoustic alarm beep this indicate that the system is activated then it receive the distance information from the ultrasonic sensor and determine whether there is any object in the detection range if there is no object it will not send an acoustic alarm if any object is detected it output signal to the acoustic warning device and control the device to provide repetitive acoustic alarm from the edge of the detection zone about from the rear vehicle the alarm frequency increase with the shortened distance between the object and the vehicle when the object is away from the vehicle the alarm become consecutive alarm detection zone the table below show the relationship between the alarm level obstacle distance and the acoustic alarm sn alarm level distance mm alarm cycle msec no obstacle zone zone zone zone zone zone zone caution blind spot area where the sensor cannot detect obstruction may occur if obstruction are present at distance of around mm from the sensor safety and restraint view foreword foreword about this document outline this document is intended to help you diagnose electrical malfunction this document is divided into the following part foreword contain the following content about this document electrical protection abbreviation wire color code wire harness abbreviation wire harness configuration code chart power supply line function connector list malfunction diagnosis and how to use the wiring diagram locator of connector and ground point show the location of each connector on the vehicle through the three dimensional space diagram in addition many connector will be shown in an illustration for convenience fuse description contain detailed description for location rated current and the protection circuit power distribution circuit contain engine bay power distribution and passenger compartment power distribution it is intended to understand the condition of power distribution service circuit contain the repair electrical wiring diagram detailed connector list instruction and connector end view of the section for each system on the vehicle note ensure that the relative mechanical function operate normally before performing the electrical test reference reference to the lh or rh side given in this document is viewed from the rear of the vehicle the operation instruction in this document do not contain the test reference after the vehicle is repaired after all the operation finished it is necessary to perform the inspection test it is especially important while performing the operation related to the safety part caution please refer to the electrical protection before performing the operation related to the electrical equipment battery voltageopen circuit voltage test before starting the electrical malfunction diagnosis check if the battery is available with the open circuit voltage test turn off all the electrical equipment on the vehicle adjust the digital multimeter to the appropriate scale to read the dc voltage try to contact the two pole of the battery with the probe to ensure the two pole are connected correctly and record the voltage displayed when the displayed voltage is or more it mean that the battery is available or the battery need to be charged caution if the vehicle has been used within hour before testing the static on the surface of the battery must be removed by turning on the headlight for about second wait for second before checking the open circuit voltage the battery voltage can be used as known reference value to determine whether the voltage in the circuit is enough high to operate each component normally this reference value can only be used as reference because many circuit can operate in large range of voltage in addition the change of the reading caused by the voltage drop and the voltage fluctuation caused by the length of the wire should be taken into consideration when the voltage passing through certain component foreword electrical protection outline the following instruction are intended to ensure the personal safety of the operator when installing the electrical equipment and electronic component equipment ensure that the relative test equipment operate normally and the wire harness and the connector are well connected before performing vehicle test procedure pay more attention to the condition of the wire and the connector used to operate the equipment caution before starting any work on ignition system make sure the high voltage terminal adapter and diagnostic equipment for testing have been insulated or shielded absolutely to minimize the risk of electric shock in order to prevent personal accident the medicinal pacemaker carrier should be away from the ignition circuit and the diagnostic equipment polarity never connect the two pole of the battery reversely ensure that both pole are connected correctly when connecting them to the test equipment high voltage circuit please use the insulated plier to disconnect the live circuit to prohibit the bare conductor contacting other component especially various ecus pay attention to the ignition coil terminal as it may produce high voltage pulse when the engine is running connector and wire harness it is difficult to install the electronic part and the connector in the engine bay therefore care should be taken ensure that the electronic part and the connector are dry without any oil before disconnecting and connecting the test equipment never use tool or drag the wire harness to disconnect the connector forcibly before disconnecting be sure to release the connector locking tab and reconnect them in the correct direction be sure to replace any protective cover and insulating material immediately once they are damaged refer to the service guide of the repair manual before replacing the defective part be sure to turn the ignition switch off disconnect the battery refer to battery disconnection to avoid unnecessary terminal damage support any wire harness disconnected when replacing the part keep the oily hand away from the electronic connection area and ensure that the connector locking tab are engaged completely battery disconnection turn all the electrical equipment off before disconnecting the battery some data time of the clock will be lost when the battery is disconnected caution to prevent damage the electrical equipment alway disconnect the battery when repairing the electrical system on the vehicle when the battery is disconnected the ground terminal must be disconnected first and connected last be sure to connect the wire harness of the battery correctly locate it in place and keep it away from any potential hazard area all the time battery charging ensure good ventilation of the battery top when charging the battery outside the vehicle after charging or discharging the battery for approximately minute the battery will release the hydrogen the gas is combustible foreword alway keep the charging area of the battery in good ventilation and prevent the creation of flame and spark rule turn off the ignition system before connecting or disconnecting any part in the system or the electrical component may be damaged by the electromagnetic excitation produced due to disconnection of the live circuit ensure your hand and the working surface have no oil or metal particle etc for example short connection or high resistance may be caused by the oil when handling the printed circuit board pay attention to the electronic component sensitive to the body static do not remove or install the connector forcibly especially the connector in the board the damaged connection will cause short or open circuit perform the ground protection periodically before or during the test for example cigar lighter doing so can release the static of the vehicle body as the static may cause some electronic component damaged foreword abbreviation outline acc accessory ampere air conditioning there are stand for the battery power with the ignition switch in on or st ign stand for the battery power with the ignition switch in on ign stand for the battery power with the ignition switch in on or acc acc stand for connecting the battery power directly bat stand for ground gnd stand for the battery power with the headlight switch in and ill illumination circuit stand for the battery power information is attached behind the wiring diagram of each system in the form of standard table and the connector are instructed in detail connector information include the following content connector no pin color attribute connector no the number is distributed by the system with prefix as wire harness id such as bd which stand for body wire harness refer to the wire harness abbreviation list for wire harness id pin stand for the total number of the connector pin caution not all the hole have lead connected color stand for the color of the connector housing attribute stand for the attribute of the connector include male connector female connector and eye connector the connector are usually inserted into the element directly and they commonly have convex pin connector function description usually derive from the connected element connector location usually determine the location of the connector by referring to the connector locator inline connector no optional item fit for the connector location inline connector no white male connector instrument pack panel to transmission case wire harness dash panel lh foreword malfunction diagnosis outline when diagnosing the electrical malfunction observe the following step study the wiring diagram carefully to make sure that you are familiar with the wiring diagram related to the malfunction study the power distribution and the fuse and check the relative circuit connected to the fuse or ground check if the circuit are operating normally use the connector layout to confirm the connection point in the circuit perhap between the power supply and the ground point and these place are damaged easily check if the lead out line information and the terminal signal information of the connector are correct check the next connector in the circuit with the above method using the above method check until the malfunction is found and troubleshoot to make sure that the circuit operate normally caution do not contact the face of the connector with probe as it may damage the terminal and cause an incorrect inspection in general the inverse side of the terminal should be detected note do not damage the terminal or seal caution do not detect the insulation part of the wire otherwise the small diameter wire will be disconnected and cause the humidity to enter into the wire and corroded it foreword how to use the wiring diagram wire jump page it show that the lead is connected to connector no and pin no see page is shown that you can refer to page of the repairing circuit for specific loop screened wire the screened wire is shown as above in the wiring diagram all brake lamp lh see page bare foreword twisted pair wire the twisted pair wire are shown as above in the wiring diagram component the name and description of the component are shown as above if the pin of the connector connected with the component are all shown in this page the component will be indicated by solid frame otherwise it will be indicated by dashed frame lead attribute lead attribute is shown above the lead in the wiring diagram and the additional information separated by is shown beside the lead color stand for the diameter of the lead with the unit of millimetre stand for the color of the lead if the lead has main color and color stripe the main color will be placed ahead for example stand for yellow with red stripe all stand for the configuration of the vehicle and it mean that any model is applicable caution the number of the screened wire mean the attribute of the lead bare stand for the screened wire dg body shown that the pin of the female connector is connected with component vanity mirror lamp lh cup symbol mean female connector inline connector inline connector are shown as above in the wiring diagram stand for the two inline connector number cup symbol mean female connector the connector on the right of the cup symbol is female and the connector inlined with it is male the number in the cup symbol mean the pin number for recognizing lead the example diagram mean that pin of male connector is inlined with pin of female connector vanity mirror lamp lh fuse description fuse description outline there are two fuse box in the vehicle one is installed on the left of the engine bay and the other is installed on the driver pillar instrument pack panel side the capacity and name of each fuse and relay are marked on the cover of each fusebox and relay block three different type of fuse are installed in the engine bay fusebox blade type fuse thinner plug in type the available current is case fuse square plug in type the available current is bolt type fuse generally also called fusible link the available current is only blade type fuse is installed in the passenger compartment fusebox fuse
